      As of this pass, the first, second, and fourth bullets are largely
      pinned for the displayed theorem surfaces, but the third bullet still
      marks the Hall-Wightman branch law, Lemma-3 relative-openness theorem,
      max-rank scalar chart, scalar continuity/local-boundedness theorem, and
      normal analytic-space removable theorem as unimplemented proof-doc
      obligations.  This is the precise reason production Lean must still
      stop before `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`.

      Local source-text audit, 2026-05-01: the OCR text
      `/tmp/hall_wightman_1957_current.txt`, generated from
      `references/hall_wightman_invariant_analytic_functions_1957.pdf`, was
      rechecked against this decomposition.  The source explicitly supports
      the route split used here: Lemma 1 gives complex Lorentz invariance and
      extended-tube single-valuedness; Lemma 2 is a rank-three/four orbit
      theorem plus rank-one/two limiting argument in four-vector notation;
      Lemma 3 is the scalar-product neighborhood theorem; Lemmas 4--7 give
      the infinitesimal equations, power-series scalar dependence, and
      non-exceptional scalar-product rank theorem; the final step invokes a
      removable-singularity theorem and records that the scalar image is open
      and connected as a continuous image of the tube.  This audit confirms
      the theorem surfaces below, but it does not by itself close the Lean
      implementation gate: the dimension-general replacements and the normal
      analytic-space removable theorem still must be proved locally or
      accepted as an explicit source-import boundary.

      Dimension and analytic-space audit for this source import:

      Hall-Wightman is printed for four-vectors.  The repo theorem surfaces
      are dimension-general with spacetime dimension `D = d + 1` and
      `hd : 2 <= d`.  The proof docs therefore cannot cite the printed
      `D = 4` theorem without also exposing the dimension-general linear
      algebra replacement.  Every occurrence of `4` in Lemmas 2, 3, 6, and 7
      is replaced by `D`; every occurrence of the direct-orbit threshold `3`
      is replaced by `D - 1 = d`; the maximal scalar-chart rank is
      `min D n = min (d + 1) n`.  The proof is the same Witt-extension,
      Schur-complement, and infinitesimal-invariant proof, but it must be
      recorded as a theorem about the repo's `ComplexLorentzGroup d`, not as
      an informal appeal to the four-dimensional paper.

      The second audit point is stronger: for `n > d + 1`, the scalar Gram
      variety has singular points.  Hall-Wightman's text proves ordinary
      power-series analyticity directly at non-exceptional points and
      continuity/local boundedness at singular points, then invokes the
      Bochner-Martin analytic-space notion.  This is a theorem about
      holomorphic germs on the scalar-product analytic variety, not a theorem
      that arbitrary local ambient extensions agree off the variety.

      This forces an API correction before production Lean.  The current
      production predicate `BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` is stronger than
      the Hall-Wightman output because it asks one total ambient function
      `Phi` to be holomorphic on full ambient neighborhoods.  That strong
      predicate can be recovered from the analytic-space germ only if a
      separate global ambient-extension theorem is supplied.  The local
      source audit of `references/hall_wightman_invariant_analytic_functions_1957.pdf`
      says `S'_n` is open and connected in the scalar-product variety, but it
      also says the set need not be the natural domain of analyticity for
      field-theory functions; therefore the proof docs must not assume, as a
      Hall-Wightman consequence, that `S'_n` is a Stein/domain-of-holomorphy
      subspace admitting global Cartan extension.  The active route is to
      refactor the source representative API to the honest analytic-space germ
      predicate below.  The Siu/Cartan ambient bridge remains documented only
      as an optional stronger support theorem if independently proved; it is
      not part of the current Lean-ready route.

      ```lean
      /-- Holomorphicity on the source Gram analytic variety.  A single value
      function `Phi` is stored on ambient scalar coordinates, but the local
      holomorphic representatives are required to agree with it only on the
      source Gram variety.  This is the Bochner-Martin/Hall-Wightman
      analytic-space notion and is strictly weaker than the old
      `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` predicate. -/
      def BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn
          (d n : Nat)
          (Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop :=
        ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U ->
          Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
            IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
            DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
            Set.EqOn Phi ╬¿
              (U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) Γêº
            U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå U

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn.to_germ
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn d n Phi U) :
          BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.continuousOn
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (hU_sub : U Γèå BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :
          ContinuousOn Phi U

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.of_subset_relOpen
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          {U V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (hV_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n V)
          (hV_sub : V Γèå U) :
          BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi V

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.sub
          {Phi Psi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (hPsi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Psi U) :
          BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n
            (fun Z => Phi Z - Psi Z) U

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (╧â : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n
            (fun Z => Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧â Z))
            {Z | BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧â Z Γêê U}

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          {╬⌐ : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (hGramU :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' ╬⌐ Γèå U) :
          DifferentiableOn Γäé
            (fun z => Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)) ╬⌐

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_differentiableOn_chart
          {╬╣ : Type} [Fintype ╬╣]
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          {D : Set (╬╣ -> Γäé)}
          {╬ô : (╬╣ -> Γäé) -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (h╬ô_diff : DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬ô D)
          (h╬ôU : ╬ô '' D Γèå U)
          (h╬ôvar :
            ╬ô '' D Γèå BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :
          DifferentiableOn Γäé (fun q => Phi (╬ô q)) D

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle
          {U W : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          {H : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_conn : IsConnected U)
          (hW_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n W)
          (hW_ne : W.Nonempty)
          (hWU : W Γèå U)
          (hH_holo : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n H U)
          (hH_zero : Set.EqOn H 0 W) :
          Set.EqOn H 0 U
      ```

      Production migration status: the field
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.Phi_holomorphic` now uses
      `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn`, not the old strong ambient predicate.
      Downstream identity-principle, uniqueness-set, and composition lemmas
      have been migrated to the germ predicate; this is not a wrapper refactor,
      because it removes an analytically false off-variety equality demand
      from the Hall-Wightman route.  The identity principle above is the
      analytic-space identity theorem on the connected normal source Gram
      variety: each local ambient representative vanishes on a nonempty
      relatively open subset of the regular locus, hence vanishes as a germ;
      overlap compatibility transports the zero germ across the connected
      relatively open domain.

      Lean-shaped proof of the basic germ support API:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn.to_germ ... := by
        intro Z hZU
        rcases hPhi Z hZU with
          Γƒ¿U0, hU0_open, hZU0, hPhi_diff, hU0_subΓƒ⌐
        exact Γƒ¿U0, Phi, hU0_open, hZU0, hPhi_diff,
          (fun W hW => rfl), hU0_subΓƒ⌐

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.continuousOn ... := by
        rw [continuousOn_iff]
        intro Z hZU T hT_open hPhiZT
        rcases hPhi Z hZU with
          Γƒ¿U0, ╬¿, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬¿_diff, hEq, _hU0_subΓƒ⌐
        have hZvar : Z Γêê BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := hU_sub hZU
        have h╬¿ZT : ╬¿ Z Γêê T := by
          simpa [hEq Γƒ¿hZU0, hZvarΓƒ⌐] using hPhiZT
        rcases (continuousOn_iff.mp h╬¿_diff.continuousOn)
            Z hZU0 T hT_open h╬¿ZT with
          Γƒ¿V, hV_open, hZV, hV_subΓƒ⌐
        exact Γƒ¿V Γê⌐ U0, hV_open.inter hU0_open, Γƒ¿hZV, hZU0Γƒ⌐,
          by
            intro W hW
            have hWU : W Γêê U := hW.2
            have hWvar : W Γêê BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := hU_sub hWU
            have hPhi_eq : Phi W = ╬¿ W := hEq Γƒ¿hW.1.2, hWvarΓƒ⌐
            simpa [hPhi_eq] using hV_sub hW.1Γƒ⌐

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.of_subset_relOpen ... := by
        intro Z hZV
        rcases hPhi Z (hV_sub hZV) with
          Γƒ¿U0, ╬¿, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬¿_diff, hEq, hU0_subΓƒ⌐
        rcases hV_rel with Γƒ¿V0, hV0_open, hV_eqΓƒ⌐
        exact Γƒ¿U0 Γê⌐ V0, ╬¿, hU0_open.inter hV0_open,
          by
            rw [hV_eq] at hZV
            exact Γƒ¿hZU0, hZV.1Γƒ⌐,
          h╬¿_diff.mono (by intro W hW; exact hW.1),
          by
            intro W hW
            exact hEq Γƒ¿hW.1.1, hW.2Γƒ⌐,
          by
            intro W hW
            rw [hV_eq]
            exact Γƒ¿hW.1.2, hW.2Γƒ⌐Γƒ⌐

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram ... := by
        intro z hz
        have hZU :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Γêê U :=
          hGramU Γƒ¿z, hz, rflΓƒ⌐
        rcases hPhi _ hZU with
          Γƒ¿U0, ╬¿, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬¿_diff, hEq, _hU0_subΓƒ⌐
        have h╬¿_at :
            DifferentiableAt Γäé ╬¿ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) :=
          h╬¿_diff.differentiableAt hU0_open hZU0
        have hGram_at :
            DifferentiableAt Γäé (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n) z :=
          (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).differentiableAt
            (by simp)
        have h╬¿_comp_within :
            DifferentiableWithinAt Γäé
              (fun w => ╬¿ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)) ╬⌐ z :=
          (h╬¿_at.comp z hGram_at).differentiableWithinAt
        have hpre_nhds :
            {w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé |
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Γêê U0} Γêê ≡¥ô¥ z :=
          ((BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous.continuousAt z)
            .preimage_mem_nhds (hU0_open.mem_nhds hZU0)
        have hlocal_eq_eventually :
            (fun w => Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)) =ß╢á[≡¥ô¥[╬⌐] z]
            (fun w => ╬¿ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)) :=
          (mem_nhdsWithin_of_mem_nhds hpre_nhds).mono (by
            intro w hwU0
            exact hEq Γƒ¿hwU0, Γƒ¿w, rflΓƒ⌐Γƒ⌐)
        have hlocal_eq_at :
            Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
              ╬¿ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) :=
          hEq Γƒ¿hZU0, Γƒ¿z, rflΓƒ⌐Γƒ⌐
        exact
          h╬¿_comp_within.congr_of_eventuallyEq
            hlocal_eq_eventually hlocal_eq_at
      ```

      The proof uses the exact Mathlib congruence API
      `DifferentiableWithinAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq` from
      `Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.FDeriv.Congr`.  The equality event is only
      on the within-filter `≡¥ô¥[╬⌐] z`, obtained by pulling back the open
      representative neighborhood `U0` along the continuous Gram map.  This
      is the crucial analytic-space point: all nearby Gram values are in the
      source Gram variety by the witness `Γƒ¿w, rflΓƒ⌐`, so the local equality
      `hEq` is used only on `U0 Γê⌐ sourceComplexGramVariety d n`; no equality
      of ambient representatives off the variety is assumed.

      Lean-facing standard-geometry inputs for these two audits:

      ```lean
      /-- Complexified Minkowski bilinear form on one source vector.  This is
      the one-vector version of `sourceMinkowskiGram`; keeping it explicit
      avoids smuggling the bilinear form into later Witt-extension statements. -/
      def BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear
          (d : Nat)
          (z w : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) : Γäé :=
        Γêæ ╬╝ : Fin (d + 1),
          (MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature d ╬╝ : Γäé) * z ╬╝ * w ╬╝

      /-- Nondegeneracy of the complexified Minkowski form restricted to a
      finite-dimensional subspace.  This is the precise hypothesis needed
      when the low-rank residual frame is placed in the orthogonal complement
      of the selected Hall-Wightman span. -/
      def BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)) : Prop :=
        ΓêÇ x : M,
          (ΓêÇ y : M,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
              (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0) ->
          x = 0

      /-- Standard nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form after diagonalizing the
      complexified Minkowski form. -/
      def BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
          {D : Nat}
          (z w : Fin D -> Γäé) : Γäé :=
        Γêæ ╬╝ : Fin D, z ╬╝ * w ╬╝

      /-- One-vector action underlying the existing configuration action
      `BHW.complexLorentzAction`. -/
      def BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction
          {d : Nat}
          (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé :=
        fun ╬╝ => Γêæ ╬╜ : Fin (d + 1), ╬¢.val ╬╝ ╬╜ * v ╬╜

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzAction_apply_eq_vectorAction
          (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
          (i : Fin n) :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z i =
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢ (z i)

      /-- Complex linear change of coordinates identifying the complexified
      Minkowski bilinear form with a standard nondegenerate symmetric
      bilinear form.  This allows the Hall-Wightman linear algebra to use the
      standard complex orthogonal Witt-extension theorem and then transport
      back to `ComplexLorentzGroup d`. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel
          (d : Nat) :
          (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel_preserves_bilinear
          (d : Nat) (z w : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d z w =
            BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel d z)
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel d w)

      /-- Dimension-general Witt extension for the nondegenerate complex
      symmetric form in spacetime dimension `d + 1`. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension
          (d : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {M N : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (e : M ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] N)
          (he :
            ΓêÇ x y : M,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d x y =
                BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (e x) (e y)) :
          Γêâ ╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ΓêÇ x : M,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢ (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) =
                (e x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 in dimension `d + 1`: near any realized
      source Gram point, every nearby point of the rank-bounded symmetric
      determinantal variety is realized by a uniformly small perturbation of
      the chosen source vectors. -/
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization_smallPerturbation
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {╬╡ : Γä¥}
          (h╬╡ : 0 < ╬╡) :
          Γêâ O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen O Γêº
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Γêê O Γêº
            ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê O Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              Γêâ v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
                (ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûv i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡) Γêº
                (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) Γêê
                  BHW.ExtendedTube d n Γêº
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) = Z

      /-- The source Gram variety is the existing repo symmetric
      determinantal variety of scalar matrices of rank at most `d + 1`,
      transported through the Minkowski-to-standard bilinear model.  The
      symmetry condition is part of the statement; replacing this by the bare
      `rank <= d + 1` subset of all matrices would be false. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n =
            BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1)

      /-- Rank-and-symmetry characterization of the same variety, matching the
      checked local API in `SourceComplexGlobalIdentity.lean`. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n =
            {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé |
              Z Γêê BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n Γêº
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j) <= d + 1}

      Active standard-geometry predicates used by the source-germ route:

      These declarations must live before the Hall-Wightman source descent
      theorem in the Lean port.  They are not QFT assumptions and they are not
      wrappers around the desired scalar representative.  They are the
      finite-dimensional SCV/algebraic-geometry vocabulary needed to state
      normality, analytic exceptional sets, and Riemann extension on the
      source Gram analytic space.

      ```lean
      /-- Relative openness inside an arbitrary analytic subspace of the
      finite source-coordinate ambient space. -/
      def BHW.IsRelOpenIn
          {n : Nat}
          (V A : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop :=
        Γêâ ╬⌐0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé), IsOpen ╬⌐0 Γêº A = ╬⌐0 Γê⌐ V

      /-- Local boundedness on a scalar-coordinate domain. -/
      def BHW.LocallyBoundedOn
          {n : Nat}
          (f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop :=
        ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U ->
          Γêâ V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen V Γêº Z Γêê V Γêº
            Γêâ C : Γä¥, 0 <= C Γêº
              ΓêÇ W, W Γêê V Γê⌐ U -> ΓÇûf WΓÇû <= C

      /-- Analytic-subvariety predicate relative to an ambient analytic set.
      The concrete implementation may use the repo's eventual SCV sheaf API;
      mathematically the subset is locally cut out, inside `V`, by finitely
      many ambient holomorphic equations. -/
      def BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
          {n : Nat}
          (V A : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop :=
        A Γèå V Γêº
        ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê V ->
          Γêâ U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
            Γêâ m : Nat, Γêâ g : Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
              (ΓêÇ a, DifferentiableOn Γäé (g a) U0) Γêº
              A Γê⌐ U0 = {W | W Γêê V Γê⌐ U0 Γêº ΓêÇ a, g a W = 0}

      /-- Standard SCV predicate: `V` is a reduced closed analytic subvariety
      and every analytic local ring `≡¥Æ¬_{V,Z}` is integrally closed.  The Lean
      implementation may encode this through the eventual analytic-local-ring
      API; it must not be a vacuous placeholder. -/
      def BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop :=
        BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn (Set.univ : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) V Γêº
        ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê V -> BHW.AnalyticLocalRingNormal V Z

      /-- Standard commutative-algebra predicate for analytic local rings.
      This is used only to invoke Serre's criterion for the symmetric
      determinantal variety. -/
      def BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop :=
        BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn (Set.univ : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) V Γêº
        ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê V -> BHW.AnalyticLocalRingCohenMacaulay V Z

      /-- Serre `R1`: the singular locus has codimension at least two, or
      equivalently `V` is regular in codimension one. -/
      def BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop :=
        BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn (Set.univ : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) V Γêº
        BHW.SingularLocusCodimAtLeast V 2
      ```

      The auxiliary names `AnalyticLocalRingNormal`,
      `AnalyticLocalRingCohenMacaulay`, `AnalyticLocalRingRegular`,
      `SingularLocusCodimAtLeast`, `ReducedAnalyticSubvariety`,
      `ComplexAnalyticDimension`, `AnalyticCodimensionIn`, and
      `AnalyticLocalProductModelAt` are intentionally standard
      analytic/algebraic-geometry predicates.  If the repo has no such API
      when the Lean port reaches this layer, it must add the genuine
      local-ring/stratification definitions or import them from a local SCV
      support file; replacing them by `True` or by the target normality
      theorem would violate the no-wrapper/no-placeholder rule.

      /-- Normality of the symmetric determinantal source Gram variety.  The
      proof is the standard finite-dimensional algebraic-geometry theorem:
      symmetric determinantal varieties over `Γäé` are irreducible, reduced,
      Cohen-Macaulay, regular in codimension one, hence normal by Serre's
      criterion.  Locally, the Schur-complement charts reduce the rank-`<= r`
      statement to a smaller symmetric rank cone, giving the induction used
      in the Lean port. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- The maximal scalar-rank locus is dense in every relatively open
      piece of the source Gram variety.  This source-specific density input is
      needed by the normal-space removable theorem; the exceptional locus
      being analytic is not by itself enough for the extension argument. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank
          (d n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U) :
          U Γèå closure
            (U Γê⌐ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z})

      /-- Symmetric determinantal normality reduced to the standard
      algebraic-geometry package.  These are not QFT inputs; they are the
      finite-dimensional commutative-algebra facts behind
      `sourceComplexGramVariety_normal`. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_cohenMacaulay
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)

      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)

      The normality invocation is Lean-ready only after the following
      determinantal subpacket is present.  These names are still
      finite-dimensional analytic geometry, not BHW locality content; their
      purpose is to prevent `sourceComplexGramVariety_normal` from becoming a
      black-box assertion that hides singular-rank work.

      ```lean
      /-- The ordinary analytic singular locus of an analytic subvariety,
      defined by non-regularity of the analytic local ring. -/
      def BHW.AnalyticSingularLocus
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) :
          Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) :=
        {Z | Z Γêê V Γêº
          ┬¼ BHW.AnalyticLocalRingRegular V Z}

      /-- Symmetric rank-bounded matrix cone on an arbitrary finite index
      type.  This avoids pretending that a Schur-complement block indexed by
      `q` is definitionally a `Fin (Fintype.card q)` source matrix.  Whenever
      a `Fin`-indexed version is needed, transport through a chosen
      equivalence `q Γëâ Fin (Fintype.card q)`. -/
      def BHW.symmetricRankLEMatrixSet
          (q : Type*) [Fintype q] [DecidableEq q]
          (D : Nat) : Set (Matrix q q Γäé) :=
        {S | Sß╡Ç = S Γêº S.rank <= D}

      /-- Local Schur product model for the symmetric rank-bounded variety.
      Around a point whose selected principal block has size `r` and
      invertible determinant, the rank-`<= D` variety is analytically
      isomorphic to a smooth factor `(A,B)` times the smaller symmetric
      rank-`<= D-r` cone in the Schur-complement coordinate `S`.  The
      implementation uses the checked graph
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph` and its checked membership theorem
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_mem_rankLE_iff`; the theorem below is
      the genuine analytic-local-product packaging of those checked
      coordinate equalities. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_schurLocalProductModel
          (n D : Nat)
          {r q : Type*} [Fintype r] [Fintype q]
          [DecidableEq r] [DecidableEq q]
          (e : Fin n Γëâ r Γèò q)
          {Z0 : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé}
          (hZ0_rank :
            Z0 Γêê BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum n (Fintype.card r))
          (hA0_unit :
            IsUnit
              ((((Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z0 i j).reindex e e)
                .toBlocksΓéüΓéü).det))
          (hrD : Fintype.card r <= D) :
          BHW.AnalyticLocalProductModelAt
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)
            Z0
            (BHW.symmetricRankLEMatrixSet q (D - Fintype.card r))
            (0 : Matrix q q Γäé)

      /-- In the nontrivial range `0 < D < n`, the analytic singular locus of
      the symmetric rank-`<= D` variety is exactly the lower-rank variety
      rank `<= D - 1`.  For `D = 0` the variety is a point, and for
      `n <= D` it is the full symmetric vector space; those easy cases are
      handled separately by the normality proof. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_singularLocus_eq_lowerRank
          (n D : Nat) (hD0 : 0 < D) (hDlt : D < n) :
          BHW.AnalyticSingularLocus
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) =
            BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (D - 1)

      /-- Complex dimension of the rank-exact symmetric stratum.  This is the
      Hall-Wightman count `n*D - D*(D-1)/2`, proved through the selected
      Schur chart dimension theorem already documented for the global source
      identity principle. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum_complexDimension
          (n D : Nat) (hDle : D <= n) :
          BHW.ComplexAnalyticDimension
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum n D) =
            n * D - (D * (D - 1)) / 2

      /-- Codimension of the lower-rank singular locus inside the
      rank-`<= D` symmetric determinantal variety.  In the singular range
      `0 < D < n` this is `n - D + 1`, hence at least two. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim
          (n D : Nat) (hD0 : 0 < D) (hDlt : D < n) :
          BHW.AnalyticCodimensionIn
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (D - 1)) =
            n - D + 1

      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim_ge_two
          (n D : Nat) (hD0 : 0 < D) (hDlt : D < n) :
          2 <=
            BHW.AnalyticCodimensionIn
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (D - 1))

      /-- Reduced Cohen-Macaulay theorem for symmetric determinantal
      varieties over `Γäé`.  This may be implemented from the standard
      straightening-law/Eagon-Northcott/Jozefiak-Pragacz package, but it may
      not be replaced by a predicate that is definitionally true. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.ReducedAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) Γêº
            BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)
      ```

      theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_of_serre
          {n : Nat}
          {V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hCM : BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hR1 : BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne V) :
          BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V

      Archived optional strong-ambient bridge:

      The following Stein/Siu/Cartan surfaces are **not** part of the active
      source-scalar route.  They document the additional SCV theorem package
      that would be needed only if the old production predicate
      `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` were kept unchanged.  The local
      Hall-Wightman audit does not prove that `S'_n` is a domain of
      holomorphy or that it admits a global Stein ambient neighborhood; it
      proves the analytic-space germ statement formalized above.  Therefore no
      theorem in this archived block may be moved to production as a wrapper
      around Hall-Wightman, and no implementation of
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` may depend on it unless the
      extra SCV hypotheses are independently proved and explicitly reopened.
      Gemini Deep Research interaction
      `v1_ChZIeHYwYWE5cng1Mzd4Z19ldGRDQURREhZIeHYwYWE5cng1Mzd4Z19ldGRDQURR`
      judged this stronger bridge mathematically sound if Step 1 is sourced
      not as a bare Hall-Wightman assertion, but as the additional
      Bochner-Martin/invariant-quotient theorem that the scalar image `S'_n`
      is a domain of holomorphy in the normal Gram analytic space (with the
      extended-tube/Stein perspective related to Zhou's theorem).  This
      validates the bridge as optional SCV support, not as an already
      Lean-ready consequence of the local Hall-Wightman source import.

      /-- A Stein open set in the finite ambient scalar-coordinate space.
      The Lean port should implement this as the chosen SCV API's Stein
      predicate; mathematically the fields are open, holomorphically
      separable, holomorphically convex on compact subsets, and equivalently
      the existence of a smooth strictly plurisubharmonic exhaustion. -/
      structure BHW.SteinOpen
          {n : Nat}
          (╬⌐ : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop where
        isOpen : IsOpen ╬⌐
        holomorphically_separable :
          ΓêÇ Z Γêê ╬⌐, ΓêÇ W Γêê ╬⌐, Z Γëá W ->
            Γêâ f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
              DifferentiableOn Γäé f ╬⌐ Γêº f Z Γëá f W
        holomorphically_convex :
          ΓêÇ K : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsCompact K -> K Γèå ╬⌐ ->
              Γêâ L : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
                IsCompact L Γêº L Γèå ╬⌐ Γêº K Γèå L Γêº
                ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê ╬⌐ -> Z Γêë L ->
                  Γêâ f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
                    DifferentiableOn Γäé f ╬⌐ Γêº
                    (ΓêÇ W, W Γêê K -> ΓÇûf WΓÇû <= 1) Γêº
                    2 <= ΓÇûf ZΓÇû

      /-- Closed analytic subvariety in the finite source-Gram coordinate
      space, stated only at the level needed by the BHW source route. -/
      structure BHW.ClosedAnalyticSubvariety
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop where
        isClosed : IsClosed V
        locally_cut_out :
          ΓêÇ Z, Γêâ U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
            Γêâ m : Nat, Γêâ g : Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
              (ΓêÇ a, DifferentiableOn Γäé (g a) U0) Γêº
              V Γê⌐ U0 = {W | W Γêê U0 Γêº ΓêÇ a, g a W = 0}

      /-- Coherent ideal sheaf cutting out a closed analytic subvariety.  The
      Lean port can replace this with the eventual SCV sheaf API, but the
      theorem-2 route must keep the finite local generator condition visible. -/
      structure BHW.CoherentIdealSheaf
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop where
        local_finite_presentation :
          ΓêÇ Z, Γêâ U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
            Γêâ m r : Nat,
            Γêâ g : Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
            Γêâ a : Fin r -> Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
              (ΓêÇ i, DifferentiableOn Γäé (g i) U0) Γêº
              (ΓêÇ b i, DifferentiableOn Γäé (a b i) U0) Γêº
              V Γê⌐ U0 = {W | W Γêê U0 Γêº ΓêÇ i, g i W = 0} Γêº
              (ΓêÇ b W, W Γêê U0 ->
                Γêæ i : Fin m, a b i W * g i W = 0) Γêº
              ΓêÇ h : Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
                (ΓêÇ i, DifferentiableOn Γäé (h i) U0) ->
                (ΓêÇ W, W Γêê U0 ->
                  Γêæ i : Fin m, h i W * g i W = 0) ->
                ΓêÇ P, P Γêê U0 ->
                  Γêâ V0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
                    IsOpen V0 Γêº P Γêê V0 Γêº V0 Γèå U0 Γêº
                    Γêâ c : Fin r -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
                      (ΓêÇ b, DifferentiableOn Γäé (c b) V0) Γêº
                      ΓêÇ i, Set.EqOn (h i)
                        (fun W => Γêæ b : Fin r, c b W * a b i W) V0

      /-- Stein analytic subspace structure on a relatively open subset of a
      closed analytic subvariety.  The fields deliberately name the
      analytic-space Stein theorem rather than pretending that relative
      openness alone gives a Stein ambient set. -/
      structure BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
          {n : Nat}
          (V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop where
        subset : U Γèå V
        relOpen : Γêâ ╬⌐0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé), IsOpen ╬⌐0 Γêº U = ╬⌐0 Γê⌐ V
        holomorphically_separable :
          ΓêÇ Z Γêê U, ΓêÇ W Γêê U, Z Γëá W ->
            Γêâ f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
              (ΓêÇ P, P Γêê U ->
                Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                  IsOpen U0 Γêº P Γêê U0 Γêº
                  DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                  Set.EqOn f ╬¿ (U0 Γê⌐ V)) Γêº
              f Z Γëá f W
        holomorphically_convex :
          ΓêÇ K : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsCompact K -> K Γèå U ->
              Γêâ L : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
                IsCompact L Γêº L Γèå U Γêº K Γèå L Γêº
                ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U -> Z Γêë L ->
                  Γêâ f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
                    (ΓêÇ P, P Γêê U ->
                      Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                        IsOpen U0 Γêº P Γêê U0 Γêº
                        DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                        Set.EqOn f ╬¿ (U0 Γê⌐ V)) Γêº
                    (ΓêÇ W, W Γêê K -> ΓÇûf WΓÇû <= 1) Γêº
                    2 <= ΓÇûf ZΓÇû

      The generic relative-open predicate `BHW.IsRelOpenIn` is declared in
      the active standard-geometry block above, before the normal Riemann
      theorem.  The archived Stein/Cartan bridge reuses that same predicate;
      it must not redeclare a second incompatible relative-openness notion.

      /-- A scalar-space holomorphic function analytically continues across a
      boundary point of a relatively open analytic-space domain if it has an
      ambient holomorphic representative on a neighborhood of that boundary
      point which agrees with the original germ on a nonempty relatively open
      piece of the old domain. -/
      structure BHW.AnalyticallyContinuesAcross
          {n : Nat}
          (V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé))
          (f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) : Prop where
        ╬⌐ : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)
        ╬¿ : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé
        ╬⌐_open : IsOpen ╬⌐
        center_mem : Z Γêê ╬⌐ Γê⌐ V
        ╬¿_hol : DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ ╬⌐
        agreement_set : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)
        agreement_relOpen : BHW.IsRelOpenIn V agreement_set
        agreement_nonempty : agreement_set.Nonempty
        agreement_subset : agreement_set Γèå U Γê⌐ ╬⌐ Γê⌐ V
        agreement : Set.EqOn f ╬¿ agreement_set

      /-- Domain-of-holomorphy predicate for a relatively open subset of the
      source Gram analytic space.  In this blueprint it is archived support
      for the optional strong ambient API only.  It is deliberately not
      recorded as a Hall-Wightman consequence: the paper gives openness and
      connectedness of `S'_n` and the invariant analytic germs, while also
      warning that this set need not be the natural domain of analyticity for
      field-theory functions. -/
      structure BHW.DomainOfHolomorphyIn
          {n : Nat}
          (V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)) : Prop where
        subset : U Γèå V
        relOpen : Γêâ ╬⌐0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé), IsOpen ╬⌐0 Γêº U = ╬⌐0 Γê⌐ V
        maximal_for_scalar_holomorphic_functions :
          ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê frontier U Γê⌐ V ->
            Γêâ f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
              (ΓêÇ P, P Γêê U ->
                Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                  IsOpen U0 Γêº P Γêê U0 Γêº
                  DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                  Set.EqOn f ╬¿ (U0 Γê⌐ V)) Γêº
              ┬¼ BHW.AnalyticallyContinuesAcross V U f Z

      /-- Closed analytic-subspace structure of the source Gram variety.  This
      packages the already checked closedness and the polynomial defining
      equations: symmetry equations `Z i j - Z j i = 0` plus the
      `(d + 2) x (d + 2)` minors in the strict rank-bounded case. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.ClosedAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Optional strong-API theorem, not active: if one independently proves
      that `S'_n` is a domain of holomorphy in the source Gram analytic space,
      then the Stein/Cartan bridge below can be used to recover the old total
      ambient-function API.  This statement is not currently justified as a
      Hall-Wightman source import. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_domainOfHolomorphy
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.DomainOfHolomorphyIn
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)

      /-- Cartan-Thullen/Bochner-Martin theorem on normal analytic spaces:
      a domain of holomorphy in a finite-dimensional normal analytic space is
      Stein. -/
      theorem BHW.domainOfHolomorphy_steinAnalyticSubspace
          {n : Nat}
          {V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hV_normal : BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hU_domain : BHW.DomainOfHolomorphyIn V U) :
          BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace V U

      /-- Optional strong-API consequence, not active: if the independent
      domain-of-holomorphy theorem above is proved, then the scalar image is
      Stein as an open analytic subspace of the closed source Gram variety.
      The active germ route does not use this theorem. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat) := by
        exact BHW.domainOfHolomorphy_steinAnalyticSubspace
          (V := BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          (U := BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal d n)
          (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_domainOfHolomorphy
            (d := d) hd n)
      ```

      /-- Siu's Stein-neighborhood theorem in the finite ambient scalar space:
      a Stein analytic subspace of a complex space admits an open Stein
      neighborhood realizing it by intersection with the ambient closed
      analytic subvariety. -/
      theorem BHW.siu_steinNeighborhood_sourceGramSubspace
          (d n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_stein :
            BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U) :
          Γêâ ╬⌐ : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen ╬⌐ Γêº BHW.SteinOpen ╬⌐ Γêº
            U = ╬⌐ Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n

      /-- Optional Stein-neighborhood support for the source Gram domain.
      This is needed only for the archived total ambient-function API. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAmbientNeighborhood
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          Γêâ ╬⌐ : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen ╬⌐ Γêº BHW.SteinOpen ╬⌐ Γêº
            BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n =
              ╬⌐ Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n

      /-- Coherence of the ideal sheaf of the source Gram variety.  In this
      concrete finite-dimensional setting the generators are the symmetry
      equations and the Gram minors, so coherence is the Oka coherence theorem
      for finitely generated ideals of holomorphic functions. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramIdealSheaf_coherent
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.CoherentIdealSheaf
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Generic Cartan-B restriction theorem in the exact shape consumed
      below: for a coherent ideal sheaf cutting out a closed analytic
      subvariety `V` inside a Stein open set `╬⌐`, every holomorphic section of
      the structure sheaf on `╬⌐ Γê⌐ V` is the restriction of a holomorphic
      function on `╬⌐`. -/
      theorem BHW.cartanB_restriction_surjective_for_coherentIdeal
          {n : Nat}
          {╬⌐ V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hV_closedAnalytic : BHW.ClosedAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hV_ideal : BHW.CoherentIdealSheaf V)
          (h╬⌐_open : IsOpen ╬⌐)
          (h╬⌐_stein : BHW.SteinOpen ╬⌐)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          (hphi_germ :
            ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê ╬⌐ Γê⌐ V ->
              Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº U0 Γèå ╬⌐ Γêº
                DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                Set.EqOn phi ╬¿ (U0 Γê⌐ V)) :
          Γêâ Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
            DifferentiableOn Γäé Phi ╬⌐ Γêº
            Set.EqOn Phi phi (╬⌐ Γê⌐ V)

      /-- Cartan theorem B / Oka extension in the form used by the source Gram
      variety: restriction from holomorphic functions on a Stein ambient
      neighborhood to holomorphic sections on the closed analytic subvariety is
      surjective.  The ideal sheaf is coherent because the source Gram variety
      is cut out by finitely many Gram minors. -/
      theorem BHW.cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety
          (d n : Nat)
          {╬⌐ : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (h╬⌐_open : IsOpen ╬⌐)
          (h╬⌐_stein : BHW.SteinOpen ╬⌐)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          (hphi_germ :
            ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê ╬⌐ Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº U0 Γèå ╬⌐ Γêº
                DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                Set.EqOn phi ╬¿
                  (U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)) :
          Γêâ Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
            DifferentiableOn Γäé Phi ╬⌐ Γêº
            Set.EqOn Phi phi
              (╬⌐ Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Archived Cartan/Oka extension bridge forced only by the old
      production API `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn`.  A holomorphic scalar germ
      on the closed
      source Gram analytic subspace, given by local ambient representatives
      that agree as germs on the variety, can be represented by one total
      ambient function `Phi` near the relatively open scalar domain.  The proof
      uses a Stein neighborhood of the source Gram domain in the finite ambient
      scalar-coordinate space and Cartan theorem B for the ideal sheaf of the
      closed analytic subvariety.  This is not part of the current
      Hall-Wightman route unless the Stein-neighborhood input is separately
      proved. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceVariety_holomorphicGerm_cartanAmbientExtension
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_stein :
            Γêâ ╬⌐ : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
              IsOpen ╬⌐ Γêº BHW.SteinOpen ╬⌐ Γêº
              U = ╬⌐ Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          (hlocal :
            ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U ->
              Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
                DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                Set.EqOn phi ╬¿
                  (U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) Γêº
                U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå U) :
          Γêâ Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
            BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn d n Phi U Γêº
            Set.EqOn Phi phi U

      Archived Lean-shaped proof of the optional Stein/Cartan bridge:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAmbientNeighborhood
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat) := by
        have hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected
            (d := d) hd n).1
        have hU_stein :
            BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace
            (d := d) hd n
        exact BHW.siu_steinNeighborhood_sourceGramSubspace
          (d := d) (n := n) hU_rel hU_stein

      theorem BHW.cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety ... := by
        -- Let `V := sourceComplexGramVariety d n`.
        -- `sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic` gives the coherent ideal
        -- sheaf `I_V` generated by the symmetry equations and Gram minors.
        -- The sequence `0 -> I_V -> O_╬⌐ -> O_{╬⌐ Γê⌐ V} -> 0` is exact.
        -- Since `╬⌐` is Stein, Cartan theorem B gives `H^1(╬⌐, I_V) = 0`;
        -- hence global sections of `O_╬⌐` surject onto global sections of
        -- `O_{╬⌐ Γê⌐ V}`.  The local data `hphi_germ` is exactly the section of
        -- `O_{╬⌐ Γê⌐ V}` represented by `phi`, so choose its ambient lift
        -- `Phi╬⌐`.  Extend `Phi╬⌐` arbitrarily outside `╬⌐`; the conclusion only
        -- asks for `DifferentiableOn Γäé Phi ╬⌐` and equality on `╬⌐ Γê⌐ V`.
        exact BHW.cartanB_restriction_surjective_for_coherentIdeal
          (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic d n)
          (BHW.sourceGramIdealSheaf_coherent d n)
          h╬⌐_open h╬⌐_stein hphi_germ

      theorem BHW.sourceVariety_holomorphicGerm_cartanAmbientExtension
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_stein :
            Γêâ ╬⌐ : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
              IsOpen ╬⌐ Γêº BHW.SteinOpen ╬⌐ Γêº
              U = ╬⌐ Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          (hlocal :
            ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U ->
              Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
                DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                Set.EqOn phi ╬¿
                  (U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) Γêº
                U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå U) := by
        rcases hU_stein with Γƒ¿╬⌐, h╬⌐_open, h╬⌐_stein, hU_eqΓƒ⌐
        have hphi_germ :
            ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê ╬⌐ Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº U0 Γèå ╬⌐ Γêº
                DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                Set.EqOn phi ╬¿
                  (U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
          intro Z hZ
          have hZU : Z Γêê U := by simpa [hU_eq] using hZ
          rcases hlocal Z hZU with
            Γƒ¿U0, ╬¿, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬¿_diff, hEq, hU0_subΓƒ⌐
          refine Γƒ¿U0 Γê⌐ ╬⌐, ╬¿, hU0_open.inter h╬⌐_open,
            Γƒ¿hZU0, hZ.1Γƒ⌐, ?_, ?_, ?_Γƒ⌐
          ┬╖ exact fun W hW => hW.2
          ┬╖ exact h╬¿_diff.mono (by intro W hW; exact hW.1)
          ┬╖ intro W hW
            exact hEq Γƒ¿hW.1.1, hW.2Γƒ⌐
        rcases BHW.cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety
            (d := d) (n := n) h╬⌐_open h╬⌐_stein hphi_germ with
          Γƒ¿Phi, hPhi_diff, hPhi_eq_phiΓƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿Phi, ?_, ?_Γƒ⌐
        ┬╖ intro Z hZU
          have hZ╬⌐V : Z Γêê ╬⌐ Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
            simpa [hU_eq] using hZU
          refine Γƒ¿╬⌐, h╬⌐_open, hZ╬⌐V.1, hPhi_diff, ?_Γƒ⌐
          intro W hW
          simpa [hU_eq] using hW
        ┬╖ intro Z hZU
          have hZ╬⌐V : Z Γêê ╬⌐ Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
            simpa [hU_eq] using hZU
          exact hPhi_eq_phi hZ╬⌐V
      ```

      /-- Riemann extension on the normal source Gram variety, in exactly the
      form needed by Hall-Wightman's Bochner-Martin conclusion.  A scalar
      germ which is ambient holomorphic on the maximal-rank regular locus and
      continuous or locally bounded on the whole `S'_n` domain has a strong
      local ambient holomorphic representative at every point.  Implement
      `BHW.LocallyBoundedOn` before this theorem; its exact definition is
      displayed again in the chart packet below. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
          (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
          (hExceptionalAnalytic :
            BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z})
          (hMaxDense :
            U Γèå closure
              (U Γê⌐ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z}))
          (hregular :
            ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
                DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå U Γêº
                Set.EqOn phi ╬¿
                  (U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)) :
          ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U ->
            Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
              IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
              DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
              U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå U Γêº
              Set.EqOn phi ╬¿
                (U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Generic normal analytic-space Riemann extension theorem specialized
      to the finite scalar-coordinate ambient type.  This is the SCV theorem
      consumed by the source-specific theorem above.  It is allowed as a
      standard complex-analytic support theorem only; it carries no QFT or
      locality content. -/
      theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension
          {n : Nat}
          {V U E : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          (hV_normal : BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenIn V U)
          (hE_analytic : BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn V E)
          (hDense : U Γèå closure (U \ E))
          (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
          (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
          (hregular :
            ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U \ E ->
              Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
                DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                U0 Γê⌐ V Γèå U Γêº
                Set.EqOn phi ╬¿ (U0 Γê⌐ V)) :
          ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U ->
            Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
              IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
              DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
              U0 Γê⌐ V Γèå U Γêº
              Set.EqOn phi ╬¿ (U0 Γê⌐ V)

      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.to_isRelOpenIn
          {d n : Nat}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hU : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenIn (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U
      ```

      Proof transcript for these standard-geometry inputs:

      1. `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel` is the finite coordinate change
         diagonalizing the complexified Minkowski form.  Over `Γäé`, signs are
         removed by multiplying the time coordinate by `I`; the linear
         equivalence is explicit in coordinates and the preservation theorem
         is a direct finite sum calculation.
      2. `complexMinkowski_wittExtension` is Witt extension for a
         nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form over an algebraically closed
         field of characteristic not two, transported back through the model
         above.  In Lean this can be proved by a Gram-Schmidt/Witt basis
         construction: split off hyperbolic pairs for the radical, extend an
         isometry on the nondegenerate quotient, then add matching orthogonal
         complements.
      3. `hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization_smallPerturbation` is the
         quantitative Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 in Schur-complement coordinates.
         Choose a principal rank `r` minor
         of `sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0`, move it to the upper-left block, and
         write nearby rank-`<= d + 1` matrices as
         `[[A, B], [Bß╡Ç, Bß╡Ç AΓü╗┬╣ B + S]]` with `S` symmetric of rank
         `<= d + 1 - r`.  Realize `A` and `B` by analytic perturbations of the
         first `r` source vectors and realize `S` by vectors in the
         `(d + 1 - r)`-dimensional orthogonal complement via
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail`.
         The estimates are Hall-Wightman's inequality (48), with `4 - r`
         replaced by `d + 1 - r`.  Choose `╬╡` smaller than the coordinate tube
         radius of the open set `BHW.ExtendedTube d n`; then all realized
         perturbations remain in the ordinary extended tube.
      4. `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety` follows
         from the same Schur realization: every source Gram matrix is
         symmetric and has rank at most `d + 1`; conversely every complex
         symmetric matrix of rank `<= d + 1` is congruent to
         `diag(1, ..., 1, 0, ..., 0)` and is realized by `d + 1` standard
         complex source vectors.  The existing checked companion
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le` is always the conjunction of
         symmetry and the rank bound, never the bare rank condition.
      5. `sourceComplexGramVariety_normal` is not a QFT theorem.  The Lean port
         should prove or import the standard symmetric-determinantal package in
         this order:

         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_schurLocalProductModel`: choose a
           rank-`r` principal block by symmetric Gaussian elimination and
           reindex it by `e : Fin n Γëâ r Γèò q`.  On the determinant-unit patch,
           use the checked coordinate equality
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_openCoordinatePatch`:
           membership in `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D` is equivalent to
           the Schur coordinate
           `S = C - Bß╡Ç AΓü╗┬╣ B` being symmetric and having rank
           `<= D-r`.  The inverse chart is
           `Z Γåª (A(Z), B(Z), S(Z))`; the forward chart is
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph n e A B S`.  The block-recovery lemmas
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_toBlocksΓéüΓéü`,
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_toBlocksΓéüΓéé`, and
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_schurComplement` give
           `to_inv_on` and `inv_to_on`.  All coordinate maps are holomorphic
           on the determinant-unit patch because matrix inverse is
           holomorphic there and the remaining operations are polynomial.
         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_singularLocus_eq_lowerRank`: in the
           nontrivial range `0 < D < n`, a rank-`D` point has residual cone
           `rank <= 0`, hence the Schur product is smooth.  A rank
           `r < D` point has residual cone `Sym_{n-r}^{<= D-r}` at `0`, with
           `0 < D-r < n-r`; all defining `(D-r+1)`-minors have vanishing
           linear part at `0`, so the Zariski tangent space is the whole
           symmetric ambient space while the cone has smaller dimension.
           Therefore those and only those points are singular.  The cases
           `D = 0` and `n <= D` are separated before this theorem: a point and
           the full symmetric vector space are smooth.
         * `sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum_complexDimension`: the
           determinant-unit Schur chart at rank `D` has free symmetric
           `A`-coordinates and rectangular `B`-coordinates, with
           `S = 0`; its dimension is
           `D*(D+1)/2 + D*(n-D) = n*D - D*(D-1)/2`.  In Lean this reuses the
           already documented checked dimension theorem
           `finrank_sourceSelectedComplexSymCoordSubspace` and the arithmetic
           packet
           `sourceRankExactChartDim_sub_previous`.
         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim`: subtract the same
           dimension formula for ranks `D` and `D-1` to get
           `n - D + 1`; because the singular case has `D < n`, this is at
           least two.  This is the exact proof of
           `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne`, not a phrase
           "singular locus has codimension at least two".
         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay`: use the
           standard symmetric-determinantal straightening-law theorem over
           `Γäé` (equivalently the Jozefiak-Pragacz resolution) to get reduced
           and Cohen-Macaulay local rings.  If the Lean port imports this as a
           standard algebraic-geometry theorem, the imported theorem must be
           stated for symmetric determinantal varieties over an algebraically
           closed field of characteristic zero and specialized to `Γäé`; it may
           not mention OS, Wightman fields, locality, extended tubes, or the
           scalar representative target.
         * Apply `normalAnalyticSubvariety_of_serre` to
           `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_cohenMacaulay` and
           `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne`; finally rewrite
           through
           `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety`.

         The local Schur chart is the same algebraic chart family used in the
         checked rank-exact source infrastructure, but here its role is
         commutative algebra/normality, not analytic continuation.
      6. `sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank` is
         the density/properness input for the removable theorem.  In the hard
         range `d + 1 < n`, it is the checked rank-exact density theorem
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_rankExact`,
         because max rank is `d + 1`; in the easy range `n <= d + 1`, it is
         the elementary density of invertible symmetric matrices inside the
         full symmetric coordinate space.  This theorem is what prevents the
         exceptional analytic set from becoming an unconstrained component.
      7. `sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension` is the analytic Riemann
         extension theorem on normal analytic spaces, specialized to the
         source Gram variety.  Its inputs must include the analytic exceptional
         set and the dense maximal-rank complement, in addition to
         `hphi_cont`, `hphi_bdd`, and the max-rank local representatives.
         Its proof first rewrites `hU_rel` as a relative-open hypothesis
         `BHW.IsRelOpenIn (sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U`; then it calls the
         generic SCV theorem
         `BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension` with
         `V := sourceComplexGramVariety d n` and
         `E := {Z | HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z}`.  The regular input
         on `U \ E` is exactly `hregular`, because
         `HWSourceGramMaxRank` is the complement of
         `HWSourceGramExceptionalRank`.  Local boundedness near the exceptional
         set is supplied by `hphi_bdd`, while continuity and the dense
         complement ensure that the extended section agrees with the original
         scalar value `phi` on exceptional points.  The resulting holomorphic
         section of the normal analytic space is represented locally by an
         ambient holomorphic function because
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic` identifies the source Gram
         variety as a closed analytic subvariety of the finite scalar-coordinate
         space.  Both the regular-locus chart input and the conclusion must
         also carry the local-domain field `U0 Γê⌐ V Γèå U`; otherwise the
         asserted equality with `phi` would mention values outside the
         relatively open scalar domain.

         The generic theorem is itself implemented through the following
         standard SCV support, with no QFT or locality content:

         ```lean
         /-- Weakly holomorphic local extension on a normal analytic subvariety.
         Here `f` is represented by compatible ambient holomorphic charts on
         `U \ E`, is locally bounded on `U`, and `U \ E` is dense in `U`.
         Normality converts this weakly holomorphic section into a genuine
         structure-sheaf section near `Z`, chosen inside the relative domain. -/
         theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension
             {n : Nat}
             {V U E : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
             {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
             (hV_normal : BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V)
             (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenIn V U)
             (hE_analytic : BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn V E)
             (hDense : U Γèå closure (U \ E))
             (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
             (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
             (hregular :
               ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U \ E ->
                 Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                   IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
                   DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                   U0 Γê⌐ V Γèå U Γêº
                   Set.EqOn phi ╬¿ (U0 Γê⌐ V))
             {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé}
             (hZU : Z Γêê U) :
             Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
               IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
               DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
               U0 Γê⌐ V Γèå U Γêº
               Set.EqOn phi ╬¿ (U0 Γê⌐ V)

         theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension ... := by
           intro Z hZU
           exact
             BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension
               hV_normal hU_rel hE_analytic hDense
               hphi_cont hphi_bdd hregular hZU
         ```

         Proof transcript for
         `normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension`:

         * Use `hU_rel` to choose an ambient open set `╬⌐U` with
           `U = ╬⌐U Γê⌐ V`; shrink every returned neighborhood inside `╬⌐U`, so
           `U0 Γê⌐ V Γèå U` is a theorem field rather than an informal
           convention.
         * On `U \ E`, the `hregular` charts define a holomorphic section of
           the structure sheaf of the analytic space `V`; overlap equality
           follows because every chart equals the same scalar function `phi`
           on its intersection with `V`.
         * `hphi_bdd` gives local boundedness of this section near points of
           `E`, and `hDense` ensures `U \ E` is the dense complement in every
           relative neighborhood used in the extension argument.
         * Apply the standard normal-space Riemann theorem:
           a locally bounded weakly holomorphic function on a normal analytic
           space extends uniquely across a nowhere-dense analytic subset.
           In Lean this theorem should live in the SCV support layer with a
           statement not mentioning Wightman, OS, BHW, locality, Gram maps, or
           fields.
         * Choose an ambient holomorphic representative of the resulting
           structure-sheaf germ using the closed analytic embedding of `V` in
           the finite coordinate space.  Equality with `phi` at exceptional
           points is obtained from continuity of both sides and equality on
           the dense set `U \ E`; no value is assigned by arbitrary choice.

         Lean-shaped specialization:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension ... := by
           have hV_normal :
               BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
                 (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :=
             BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal d n
           have hU_rel' :
               BHW.IsRelOpenIn (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U :=
             BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.to_isRelOpenIn hU_rel
           have hDense' :
               U Γèå closure
                 (U \ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z}) := by
             have hSet :
                 U Γê⌐ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z} =
                   U \ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z} := by
               ext Z
               constructor
               ┬╖ rintro Γƒ¿hZU, hMaxΓƒ⌐
                 refine Γƒ¿hZU, ?_Γƒ⌐
                 intro hExc
                 exact
                   ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
                     (d := d) (n := n)).1 hExc) hMax
               ┬╖ rintro Γƒ¿hZU, hNotExcΓƒ⌐
                 refine Γƒ¿hZU, ?_Γƒ⌐
                 by_contra hNotMax
                 exact hNotExc
                   ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
                     (d := d) (n := n)).2 hNotMax)
             simpa [hSet] using hMaxDense
           have hregular' :
               ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê U \ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z} ->
                 Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                   IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
                   DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                   U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå U Γêº
                   Set.EqOn phi ╬¿
                     (U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
             intro Z hZ
             have hZU : Z Γêê U := hZ.1
             have hMax : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z :=
               by
                 by_contra hnotMax
                 exact hZ.2
                   ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
                     (d := d) (n := n)).2 hnotMax)
             exact hregular Z hZU hMax
           exact
             BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension
               hV_normal hU_rel' hExceptionalAnalytic hDense'
               hphi_cont hphi_bdd hregular'
         ```
      8. `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAmbientNeighborhood` and
         `cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety` are archived
         Siu/Cartan support for the old total ambient-function API only.  The
         first would be a four-step theorem, not a consequence of relative
         openness alone: an independent theorem would have to supply
         `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_domainOfHolomorphy`; the normality of
         the source Gram variety would upgrade this to
         `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace`; the source domain
         is already relatively open by
         `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected`; Siu's theorem would
         then give a Stein ambient neighborhood `╬⌐` with
         `S'_n = ╬⌐ Γê⌐ sourceComplexGramVariety`.  The second uses the coherent
         ideal sheaf generated by the symmetry equations and Gram minors,
         applies the exact sequence
         `0 -> I_V -> O_╬⌐ -> O_{╬⌐Γê⌐V} -> 0`, and invokes Cartan theorem B on
         the Stein open `╬⌐` to prove surjectivity of
         `╬ô(╬⌐, O_╬⌐) -> ╬ô(╬⌐ Γê⌐ V, O_{╬⌐Γê⌐V})`.
      8. `sourceVariety_holomorphicGerm_cartanAmbientExtension` is not a
         Hall-Wightman theorem and is not active.  It is only the bridge from
         the usual analytic-space output to the repo's stronger total-ambient
         `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` predicate.  The active route instead
         changes `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.Phi_holomorphic` to the germ
         predicate, so this bridge is unnecessary for theorem-2 production
         Lean unless the old strong API is explicitly retained and the
         independent Stein input is proved.

      ```lean
      /-- Rank of a source Gram matrix in Hall-Wightman's scalar-product
      coordinates.  This is a predicate on scalar products, not on the
      dimension of the source-vector span. -/
      def BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank
          (n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) : Nat :=
        (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j).rank

      def BHW.sourceConfigGramMatrixRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) : Nat :=
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      /-- Dimension-general Hall-Wightman orbit-rank condition.  For the
      original four-vector theorem, `d = 3`, so this says rank
      `min 3 n` or higher: rank three or four in the hard arity range, and
      nonsingular scalar matrices in the small-arity range. -/
      def BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) : Prop :=
        min d n <= BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z

      def BHW.HWSourceGramLowRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) : Prop :=
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z < min d n

      def BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) : Prop :=
        BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank d n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      def BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) : Prop :=
        BHW.HWSourceGramLowRank d n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      /-- Hall-Wightman's non-exceptional scalar-rank condition for the
      local holomorphic chart part of Theorem I.  This is a different
      threshold from the Lemma-2 orbit condition: it is maximal possible
      scalar rank in spacetime dimension `d + 1`. -/
      def BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) : Prop :=
        min (d + 1) n <= BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z

      def BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) : Prop :=
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z < min (d + 1) n

      def BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) : Prop :=
        BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      theorem BHW.hwSourceGram_lowRank_iff_not_orbitRank
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé} :
          BHW.HWSourceGramLowRank d n Z Γåö
            ┬¼ BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank d n Z := by
        constructor
        ┬╖ intro hlow horbit
          exact (not_lt_of_ge horbit) hlow
        ┬╖ intro hnot
          exact Nat.lt_of_not_ge hnot

      theorem BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé} :
          BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z Γåö
            ┬¼ BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z := by
        constructor
        ┬╖ intro hlow hmax
          exact (not_lt_of_ge hmax) hlow
        ┬╖ intro hnot
          exact Nat.lt_of_not_ge hnot

      theorem BHW.hwSourceGramOrbitRankAt_of_sourceGram_eq
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z Γåö
            BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n w := by
        simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt, hgram]

      /- These rank-predicate lemmas are only arithmetic and definitional
      bookkeeping.  The hypothesis `hd` on
      `hwSourceGram_lowRank_iff_not_orbitRank` is retained only to match the
      Hall-Wightman theorem surfaces where it is consumed; the proof itself is
      `Nat.lt_of_not_ge` and `not_lt_of_ge`.  Thus none of the real
      Hall-Wightman content is hidden in the rank split: the genuine work
      starts with the high-rank span isometry and the low-rank isotropic
      contraction data below. -/

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma-2 low-rank normal form before taking limits.
      After applying `╬¢0` to the left configuration, the two equal-Gram
      configurations differ by vectors in a common totally isotropic residual
      subspace orthogonal to the common selected span.  In four spacetime
      dimensions this residual subspace is at most one-dimensional, giving the
      printed Hall-Wightman formula with one null vector.  For the repo's
      dimension-general surfaces it must be kept as a finite isotropic frame;
      replacing it by one `q` would be false in higher dimension. -/
      structure BHW.HWLowRankIsotropicNormalForm
          (d n : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) where
        ╬¢0 : ComplexLorentzGroup d
        ╬╛ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        s : Nat
        aLeft : Fin n -> Fin s -> Γäé
        aRight : Fin n -> Fin s -> Γäé
        q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        left_eq :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢0 z =
            fun i ╬╝ => ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, aLeft i c * q c ╬╝
        right_eq :
          w = fun i ╬╝ => ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, aRight i c * q c ╬╝
        base_mem :
          ╬╛ Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        q_pair_zero :
          ΓêÇ c c',
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (q c') = 0
        qDual_pair_zero :
          ΓêÇ c c',
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0
        q_dual :
          ΓêÇ c c',
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (qDual c') =
              if c = c' then (1 : Γäé) else 0
        q_orth :
          ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (╬╛ i) = 0
        qDual_orth :
          ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (╬╛ i) = 0
        contract : Γä¥ -> ComplexLorentzGroup d
        contract_fix_╬╛ :
          ΓêÇ t i ╬╝,
            (Γêæ ╬╜ : Fin (d + 1), (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬╜ * ╬╛ i ╬╜) = ╬╛ i ╬╝
        contract_scale_q :
          ΓêÇ t c ╬╝,
            (Γêæ ╬╜ : Fin (d + 1), (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬╜ * q c ╬╜) =
              Real.exp (-t) * q c ╬╝
        contract_scale_qDual :
          ΓêÇ t c ╬╝,
            (Γêæ ╬╜ : Fin (d + 1),
              (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬╜ * qDual c ╬╜) =
              Real.exp t * qDual c ╬╝
        contracted_left_mem :
          ΓêÇ t,
            (fun i ╬╝ =>
              ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        contracted_right_mem :
          ΓêÇ t,
            (fun i ╬╝ =>
              ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        contracted_left_tendsto :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : Γä¥ =>
              fun i ╬╝ =>
                ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c * q c ╬╝)
            atTop (nhds ╬╛)
        contracted_right_tendsto :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : Γä¥ =>
              fun i ╬╝ =>
                ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c * q c ╬╝)
            atTop (nhds ╬╛)

      /-- Linear algebra inside the low-rank branch: after choosing a nonzero
      principal scalar block, the equal Gram data determine the same
      nondegenerate selected span, scalar-coordinate coefficients against that
      span, and residual radical pairings. -/
      structure BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame
          (d n r : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n) where
        I_injective : Function.Injective I
        principal_minor_ne :
          BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) Γëá 0
        selected_gram_eq :
          ΓêÇ a b,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z (I a) (I b) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w (I a) (I b)
        coeff : Fin n -> Fin r -> Γäé
        left_residual_orth :
          ΓêÇ i a,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (z i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó z (I b))
              (z (I a)) = 0
        right_residual_orth :
          ΓêÇ i a,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (w i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó w (I b))
              (w (I a)) = 0
        residual_pairing_eq :
          ΓêÇ i j,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (z i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó z (I b))
              (z j - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ΓÇó z (I b)) =
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (w i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó w (I b))
              (w j - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ΓÇó w (I b))
        left_residual_pair_zero :
          ΓêÇ i j,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (z i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó z (I b))
              (z j - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ΓÇó z (I b)) = 0
        right_residual_pair_zero :
          ΓêÇ i j,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (w i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó w (I b))
              (w j - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ΓÇó w (I b)) = 0

      def BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) :
          Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Γäé :=
        Matrix.of fun a b => G (I a) (I b)

      noncomputable def BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)
          (i : Fin n) :
          Fin r -> Γäé :=
        fun b =>
