          Γêæ a : Fin r,
            G i (I a) *
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G)Γü╗┬╣ a b

      theorem BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff_projection_eq
          {G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hunit :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G).det)
          (i : Fin n) (a : Fin r) :
          Γêæ b : Fin r,
            BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G i b *
              G (I b) (I a) =
            G i (I a)

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_selected_residual_orthogonal
          [NeZero d]
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hunit :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)).det) :
          ΓêÇ i a,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (z i -
                Γêæ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) i b ΓÇó z (I b))
              (z (I a)) = 0

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_residual_pairing_eq_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hunit :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)).det)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          ΓêÇ i j,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (z i -
                Γêæ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) i b ΓÇó z (I b))
              (z j -
                Γêæ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) j b ΓÇó z (I b)) =
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (w i -
                Γêæ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) i b ΓÇó w (I b))
              (w j -
                Γêæ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) j b ΓÇó w (I b))

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_selected_residual_pairing_zero
          [NeZero d]
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hrank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) = r)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) Γëá 0) :
          ΓêÇ i j,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (z i -
                Γêæ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) i b ΓÇó z (I b))
              (z j -
                Γêæ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) j b ΓÇó z (I b)) = 0

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanFrame_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz : z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzLow : BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          Γêâ r : Nat, Γêâ I : Fin r -> Fin n,
            r = BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) Γêº
            BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I

      def BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace
          (d : Nat)
          (R : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)) : Prop :=
        ΓêÇ x y : R,
          BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
            (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
            (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0

      /-- Data obtained after the selected nondegenerate spans have first been
      matched by a Witt-extension Lorentz transformation.  The common selected
      span is the `w` selected span; only after applying `╬¢sel` to the left
      endpoint do the two projected residual families live in the same
      orthogonal complement. -/
      structure BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment
          (d n r : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I) where
        M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
        ╬¢sel : ComplexLorentzGroup d
        ╬╛ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        leftResidual : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        rightResidual : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        M_eq :
          M = Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range (fun a : Fin r => w (I a)))
        M_nondeg : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M
        selected_mem : ΓêÇ a, w (I a) Γêê M
        ╬¢sel_selected :
          ΓêÇ a,
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢sel (z (I a)) = w (I a)
        ╬╛_eq :
          ΓêÇ i, ╬╛ i = Γêæ b : Fin r, S.coeff i b ΓÇó w (I b)
        ╬╛_mem : ΓêÇ i, ╬╛ i Γêê M
        leftResidual_eq :
          ΓêÇ i,
            leftResidual i =
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢sel (z i) - ╬╛ i
        rightResidual_eq :
          ΓêÇ i, rightResidual i = w i - ╬╛ i
        left_decomp :
          ΓêÇ i,
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢sel (z i) =
              ╬╛ i + leftResidual i
        right_decomp : ΓêÇ i, w i = ╬╛ i + rightResidual i
        left_residual_orth_M :
          ΓêÇ i (m : M),
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (leftResidual i)
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0
        right_residual_orth_M :
          ΓêÇ i (m : M),
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (rightResidual i)
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0
        left_residual_pair_zero :
          ΓêÇ i j,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (leftResidual i) (leftResidual j) = 0
        right_residual_pair_zero :
          ΓêÇ i j,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (rightResidual i) (rightResidual j) = 0

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanAlignment_of_selectedSpanFrame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I) :
          Nonempty
            (BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment d n r z w I S)

      /-- Once the selected spans are aligned, the projected residual vectors
      on both sides lie in the orthogonal complement of the common selected
      span and span totally isotropic subspaces. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_after_selectedAlignment
          [NeZero d]
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          {S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I}
          (A : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment d n r z w I S) :
          Γêâ (Rleft Rright : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)),
            Rleft =
              Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range A.leftResidual) Γêº
            Rright =
              Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range A.rightResidual) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x : Rleft, ΓêÇ m : A.M,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x : Rright, ΓêÇ m : A.M,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0) Γêº
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rleft Γêº
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rright

      /-- Finite-dimensional Witt geometry in the orthogonal complement of the
      selected span: two finite totally isotropic residual families can be put
      into one common totally isotropic frame by a complex Lorentz
      transformation fixing the selected nondegenerate span.  This is the
      dimension-general replacement for Hall-Wightman's one-null-vector
      alignment in four spacetime dimensions. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_alignResidualSubspaces_to_commonIsotropicFrame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {M Rz Rw : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hRz_orth :
            ΓêÇ x : Rz, ΓêÇ m : M,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0)
          (hRw_orth :
            ΓêÇ x : Rw, ΓêÇ m : M,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0)
          (hRz_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rz)
          (hRw_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rw) :
          Γêâ (╬¢fix : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
            (s : Nat)
            (q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
            (ΓêÇ m : M,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢fix
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) =
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)) Γêº
            LinearIndependent Γäé q Γêº
            (ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (q c') = 0) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ c (m : M),
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x : Rz,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢fix
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) Γêê
              Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range q)) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x : Rw,
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) Γêê
              Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range q))

      The proof of this alignment theorem is a finite-dimensional Witt
      construction in the orthogonal complement of `M`; it must not be left
      as a black-box "put both residual spaces in the same null frame".  The
      required support packet is:

      ```lean
      def BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)) :
          Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :=
        { carrier := {v | ΓêÇ m : M,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d v
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0}
          zero_mem' := by simp
          add_mem' := by
            intro x y hx hy m
            simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear_add_left, hx m, hy m]
          smul_mem' := by
            intro c x hx m
            simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear_smul_left, hx m] }

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)

      theorem BHW.subspace_le_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
          {M R : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hR_orth :
            ΓêÇ x : R, ΓêÇ m : M,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0) :
          R Γëñ BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M

      structure BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn
          (d : Nat)
          (N : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)) where
        s : Nat
        q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        q_mem : ΓêÇ c, q c Γêê N
        q_independent : LinearIndependent Γäé q
        q_pair_zero :
          ΓêÇ c c',
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (q c') = 0
        maximal :
          ΓêÇ R : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
            R Γëñ N ->
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R ->
            Module.finrank Γäé R Γëñ s

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_exists
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {N : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hN :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N) :
          Nonempty
            (BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N)

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {N R : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hN :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N)
          (hR_le : R Γëñ N)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R) :
          Γêâ F : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N,
            R Γëñ Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range F.q)

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_frame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {N R : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (F : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N)
          (hR_le : R Γëñ N)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R) :
          Γêâ E : R ->Γéù[Γäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
            Function.Injective E Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x : R,
              E x Γêê Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range F.q)) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x y : R,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (E x)
                (E y)
              =
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé))
      ```

      Proof transcript for this packet: `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule`
      is a submodule because the bilinear form is linear in the first
      variable.  If `M` is nondegenerate in the ambient nondegenerate complex
      Minkowski space, the standard finite-dimensional decomposition
      `ambient = M Γèö Mßù«` gives nondegeneracy of `Mßù«`; in Lean this is the
      same Gram-matrix block diagonal argument used in
      `complexMinkowski_wittExtension`.  A maximal isotropic frame in a
      nondegenerate finite-dimensional complex bilinear space is obtained by
      induction on finrank: if there is no nonzero isotropic vector, take
      `s = 0`; otherwise split off a hyperbolic pair `(q, qDual)` and recurse
      on the orthogonal complement of their span.  Maximality records the
      Witt-index dimension bound for every totally isotropic subspace.  The
      strengthened constructor
      `complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending` starts with a
      basis of the given totally isotropic subspace `R`, splits off dual
      hyperbolic partners for that basis, and then completes the basis to a
      maximal isotropic frame inside the remaining orthogonal complement.  Its
      output is essential here: the right residual subspace `Rw` must already
      lie in the final frame span before the left residual subspace is moved.

      For `complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_frame`, choose a
      basis of `R`.  The maximality field gives
      `finrank R <= F.s`, so inject that basis index into `Fin F.s` and send
      the basis vectors of `R` to the corresponding `F.q` vectors.  Since the
      bilinear form vanishes identically on `R` and on the span of the frame,
      the map preserves pairings automatically; the selected `F.q` subfamily
      is independent, so the map is injective.  The theorem deliberately
      returns an injective map into the frame span, not an equivalence onto the
      full frame span: `R` may have smaller dimension than the chosen maximal
      frame.

      Lean-shaped proof of the residual alignment theorem after the support
      packet exists:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_alignResidualSubspaces_to_commonIsotropicFrame
          ... := by
        let N := BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M
        have hN_nondeg :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N :=
          BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
            (d := d) hd hM
        have hRz_le : Rz Γëñ N :=
          BHW.subspace_le_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
            (d := d) hRz_orth
        have hRw_le : Rw Γëñ N :=
          BHW.subspace_le_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
            (d := d) hRw_orth
        rcases BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending
            (d := d) hd hN_nondeg hRw_le hRw_iso with
          Γƒ¿F, hRw_QΓƒ⌐
        rcases
          BHW.complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_frame
            (d := d) hd F hRz_le hRz_iso with
          Γƒ¿Eleft, hEleft_inj, hEleft_Q, hEleft_preservesΓƒ⌐
        let Qspan : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :=
          Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range F.q)
        let Qleft : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :=
          LinearMap.range Eleft
        have hQleft_le_Qspan : Qleft Γëñ Qspan := by
          rintro v Γƒ¿x, rflΓƒ⌐
          exact hEleft_Q x
        let Domain : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) := M Γèö Rz
        let Target : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) := M Γèö Qleft
        let T : Domain ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] Target :=
          BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding
            (d := d) M Rz Eleft hM hRz_orth hRz_iso
            hEleft_inj hEleft_Q hEleft_preserves
        have hT_preserves :
            ΓêÇ x y : Domain,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                ((T x : Target) : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                ((T y : Target) : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) =
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :=
          BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_preserves
            (d := d) M Rz Eleft hM hRz_orth hRz_iso
            hEleft_Q F.q_mem F.q_pair_zero hEleft_preserves
        rcases BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry
            (d := d) hd T hT_preserves with
          Γƒ¿╬¢fix, h╬¢fixΓƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿╬¢fix, F.s, F.q, ?_, F.q_independent,
          F.q_pair_zero, ?_, ?_, ?_Γƒ⌐
        ┬╖ intro m
          have hmDomain : (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) Γêê Domain :=
            Submodule.mem_sup_left m.2
          exact h╬¢fix Γƒ¿m, hmDomainΓƒ⌐ |> congrArg Subtype.val
        ┬╖ intro c m
          have hqN : F.q c Γêê N := F.q_mem c
          exact hqN m
        ┬╖ intro x
          have hxDomain : (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) Γêê Domain :=
            Submodule.mem_sup_right x.2
          have hTx : ((T Γƒ¿x, hxDomainΓƒ⌐ : Target) :
              Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) Γêê Qleft :=
            BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_maps_right
              (d := d) M Rz Eleft x
          have hTxQ : ((T Γƒ¿x, hxDomainΓƒ⌐ : Target) :
              Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) Γêê Qspan :=
            hQleft_le_Qspan hTx
          simpa [Qspan] using
            (show BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢fix
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) =
              ((T Γƒ¿x, hxDomainΓƒ⌐ : Target) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) from h╬¢fix Γƒ¿x, hxDomainΓƒ⌐) Γû╕ hTxQ
        ┬╖ intro x
          exact hRw_Q x.2
      ```

      The pseudocode uses the proof-local helper
      `directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding` and its `preserves` /
      `maps_right` projections.  These are not new route assumptions: they are
      the finite linear map on `M Γèö Rz` which is the identity on `M` and
      `Eleft` on `Rz`, with codomain `M Γèö LinearMap.range Eleft`, not the
      whole maximal frame span.  The proof that it preserves the bilinear form
      is exactly the block calculation using nondegeneracy of `M`,
      orthogonality of `Rz` and the frame span to `M`, and total isotropy of
      both residual blocks.  If the Lean implementation avoids naming this
      helper, it must still perform the same block calculation before applying
      Witt extension.

      theorem BHW.exists_coefficients_of_mem_span_finite_frame
          {d s : Nat}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {v : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hv : v Γêê Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range q)) :
          Γêâ a : Fin s -> Γäé, v = Γêæ c : Fin s, a c ΓÇó q c

      /-- After the selected nondegenerate spans have been matched by Witt
      extension, the low-rank discrepancy is contained in a finite totally
      isotropic residual frame in the orthogonal complement.  For `d = 3` this
      frame has length at most one, recovering Hall-Wightman's displayed
      vector `w`; for general `d` the frame length may be larger. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_alignedResidualFrame_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz : z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzLow : BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          Γêâ (M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé))
            (╬¢0 : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
            (╬╛ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
            (s : Nat)
            (aLeft aRight : Fin n -> Fin s -> Γäé)
            (q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M Γêº
              (ΓêÇ i, ╬╛ i Γêê M) Γêº
              (ΓêÇ c (m : M),
                BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) =
                  0) Γêº
              LinearIndependent Γäé q Γêº
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢0 z =
                (fun i ╬╝ => ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, aLeft i c * q c ╬╝) Γêº
              w = (fun i ╬╝ => ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, aRight i c * q c ╬╝) Γêº
              ╬╛ Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n Γêº
              (ΓêÇ c c',
                BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (q c') = 0) Γêº
              (ΓêÇ c i,
                BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (╬╛ i) = 0)

      /-- Witt-complement input used by the low-rank contraction.  A finite
      totally isotropic residual frame in the orthogonal complement of the
      common base admits a dual isotropic frame, with the stated pairings,
      after possibly discarding zero residual directions in the normal-form
      construction.  This is the dimension-general replacement for the
      one-null-vector boost in the printed four-dimensional proof. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicDualFrame_of_residualFrame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n s : Nat}
          {╬╛ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hM_nondeg :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (h╬╛_mem : ΓêÇ i, ╬╛ i Γêê M)
          (hq_orth_M :
            ΓêÇ c (m : M),
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_independent :
            LinearIndependent Γäé q) :
          Γêâ qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
            (ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : Γäé) else 0) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ c (m : M),
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (qDual c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ c i,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (╬╛ i) = 0)

      /-- The null boost fixing the selected span and contracting every vector
      in the totally isotropic residual frame.  The proof chooses a dual
      isotropic frame `qDual`, completes
      `selectedSpan Γèò span q Γèò span qDual` to an orthogonal Witt basis, and
      scales `q` by `exp (-t)` and `qDual` by `exp t`.  The dual scaling is
      part of the output so the proof that the family lies in
      `ComplexLorentzGroup d` is visible, not hidden inside a bare existence
      of maps.  In production declaration order, first prove the two support
      theorems displayed immediately below this public surface:
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry` and
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContraction_partialIsometry`. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContractionFamily
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n s : Nat}
          {╬╛ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hqDual_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0)
          (hq_dual :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : Γäé) else 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (╬╛ i) = 0)
          (hqDual_orth :
            ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (╬╛ i) = 0) :
          Γêâ contract : Γä¥ -> ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            (ΓêÇ t i ╬╝,
              (Γêæ ╬╜ : Fin (d + 1),
                (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬╜ * ╬╛ i ╬╜) = ╬╛ i ╬╝) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ t c ╬╝,
              (Γêæ ╬╜ : Fin (d + 1),
                (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬╜ * q c ╬╜) =
                  Real.exp (-t) * q c ╬╝) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ t c ╬╝,
              (Γêæ ╬╜ : Fin (d + 1),
                (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬╜ * qDual c ╬╜) =
                  Real.exp t * qDual c ╬╝)

      /-- Hall-Wightman's second remark after Lemma 2, finite-frame version:
      if a forward-tube point differs from a base configuration only by a
      finite totally isotropic frame orthogonal to the base, then the base
      configuration is still in the forward tube.  This is the load-bearing
      input behind the low-rank limiting argument; it is not a closure
      property of the open tube. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_base_mem_forwardTube_of_endpoint
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {╬╛ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> Γäé}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (h╬╛q :
            (fun i ╬╝ => ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, a i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
              BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (╬╛ i) = 0) :
          ╬╛ Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n

      /-- Hall-Wightman's first consequence of the second and third remarks
      after Lemma 2, finite-frame version.  Once one coefficient tuple along a
      totally isotropic frame lies in the ordinary extended tube, every
      coefficient tuple along the same frame lies in the ordinary extended
      tube, including the zero tuple. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {╬╛ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> Γäé}
          {q qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (h╬╛q :
            (fun i ╬╝ => ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, a i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hqDual_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0)
          (hq_dual :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : Γäé) else 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (╬╛ i) = 0)
          (hqDual_orth :
            ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (╬╛ i) = 0) :
          ╬╛ Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n Γêº
          ΓêÇ b : Fin n -> Fin s -> Γäé,
            (fun i ╬╝ => ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, b i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n

      /-- General finite-dimensional Witt extension for the complex Minkowski
      form.  Unlike the high-rank shortcut statement, the subspace here need
      not be nondegenerate: an isometry between two finite-dimensional
      subspaces of the ambient nondegenerate complex Minkowski space extends to
      a global complex Lorentz transformation.  This is the standard Witt
      lemma used by the low-rank contraction frame. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {M N : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (T : M ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] N)
          (hT :
            ΓêÇ x y : M,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                ((T x : N) : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                ((T y : N) : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) =
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)) :
          Γêâ ╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ΓêÇ x : M,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) =
              ((T x : N) : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)

      /-- The partial finite-frame isometry underlying the low-rank null
      boost.  Its domain is the span of the common base vectors, the residual
      isotropic frame, and the dual residual frame.  It fixes every `╬╛ i`,
      scales `q c` by `exp (-t)`, scales `qDual c` by `exp t`, and preserves
      the complex Minkowski form on that span. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContraction_partialIsometry
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n s : Nat}
          {╬╛ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hqDual_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0)
          (hq_dual :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : Γäé) else 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (╬╛ i) = 0)
          (hqDual_orth :
            ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (╬╛ i) = 0)
          (t : Γä¥) :
          Γêâ K : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
          Γêâ T : K ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] K,
          Γêâ h╬╛ : ΓêÇ i, ╬╛ i Γêê K,
          Γêâ hq : ΓêÇ c, q c Γêê K,
          Γêâ hqDual : ΓêÇ c, qDual c Γêê K,
            (ΓêÇ i,
              ((T Γƒ¿╬╛ i, h╬╛ iΓƒ⌐ : K) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = ╬╛ i) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ c,
              ((T Γƒ¿q c, hq cΓƒ⌐ : K) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) =
                fun ╬╝ => Real.exp (-t) * q c ╬╝) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ c,
              ((T Γƒ¿qDual c, hqDual cΓƒ⌐ : K) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) =
                fun ╬╝ => Real.exp t * qDual c ╬╝) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x y : K,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                ((T x : K) : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                ((T y : K) : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) =
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé))

      Proof transcript for the contraction family.  For each fixed `t`, put
      `X := Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range ╬╛)`, `Q := Submodule.span Γäé
      (Set.range q)`, `Qd := Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range qDual)`, and
      `K := X Γèö Q Γèö Qd`.  The duality equations imply:

      1. `q` is linearly independent, because pairing
         `Γêæ c, a c ΓÇó q c = 0` with `qDual c0` gives `a c0 = 0`.
      2. `qDual` is linearly independent by the same argument, using symmetry
         of `complexMinkowskiBilinear`.
      3. `Q Γê⌐ X = ΓèÑ`: an element of `Q Γê⌐ X` pairs trivially with every
         `qDual c` by `hqDual_orth`, while the duality equation reads off its
         `q`-coordinates.
      4. `Qd Γê⌐ (X Γèö Q) = ΓèÑ`: pair with every `q c` and use `hq_orth`,
         `hq_pair_zero`, and `hq_dual`.

      These four facts give a direct-sum coordinate decomposition of `K`.
      Define `T_t` on `K` by
      `x + qpart + qdpart Γåª x + Real.exp (-t) ΓÇó qpart +
      Real.exp t ΓÇó qdpart`.  The displayed pairings show that `T_t`
      preserves the complex Minkowski form on all pairs of generators:
      the `X`-`X` block is fixed, the `X`-`Q` and `X`-`Qd` blocks are zero,
      the `Q`-`Q` and `Qd`-`Qd` blocks are zero, and the `Q`-`Qd` block is
      multiplied by `Real.exp (-t) * Real.exp t = 1`.  The inverse is the
      same construction with `-t`, so this is a linear equivalence
      `K ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] K`.  This proves
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContraction_partialIsometry`.

      Then apply
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry` to this partial
      isometry for each `t`, and choose the returned Lorentz transformation
      as `contract t`.  The three output formulas of
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContractionFamily` are obtained by
      applying the extension equality to the generators `╬╛ i`, `q c`, and
      `qDual c`.  No continuity of the chosen map `t Γåª contract t` is used
      later; all limits in the low-rank branch are proved from the explicit
      contracted configurations and the checked Mathlib scalar limit
      `Real.tendsto_exp_neg_atTop_nhds_zero`, followed by the continuous
      coercion `Γä¥ -> Γäé` and finite-sum continuity.

      Proof transcript for the tube-stability theorems.  The forward-tube
      theorem is Hall-Wightman's second remark after Lemma 2.  In the chosen
      two-plane normal form for each isotropic direction, write the imaginary
      parts of the forward-tube successive differences of
      `╬╛ i + Γêæ c, a i c ΓÇó q c` in the basis
      `w1 c, w2 c` plus an orthogonal remainder.  The positivity inequalities
      defining the forward tube are unchanged when the coefficient components
      along the isotropic directions are continuously decreased to zero; the
      orthogonal remainders still have positive cone differences.  In Lean,
      this is implemented after transporting the finite isotropic frame to a
      Witt basis by
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry`; the coordinate
      calculation is finite and uses only `ForwardTube`, `InOpenForwardCone`,
      `hq_pair_zero`, and `hq_orth`.

      For the extended-tube theorem, write the endpoint as
      `╬¢0 ΓÇó y` with `y Γêê ForwardTube d n`.  Apply the forward-tube theorem to
      the transformed base and transformed isotropic frame to obtain the
      transformed base in the forward tube.  Given arbitrary coefficients
      `b`, use
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContractionFamily` for the transformed
      frame: for sufficiently large `t`, the configuration with coefficients
      `Real.exp (-t) * b` is inside the forward-tube neighborhood of the
      transformed base; applying the inverse contraction gives the
      transformed target with coefficients `b`, hence the original target is
      in the extended tube.  Taking `b = 0` gives `╬╛ Γêê ExtendedTube d n`.
      This is exactly Hall-Wightman's first consequence after the three
      remarks following Lemma 2, generalized from one null vector to a finite
      totally isotropic frame.

      /-- Hall-Wightman's tube lemma after Lemma 2: because the contraction is
      itself a complex Lorentz transformation, every contracted residual-frame
      configuration remains in the ordinary extended tube and converges to the
      endpoint configuration. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicContraction_staysIn_extendedTube
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {╬╛ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> Γäé}
          {q qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {contract : Γä¥ -> ComplexLorentzGroup d}
          (h╬╛q :
            (fun i ╬╝ => ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, a i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hqDual_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0)
          (hq_dual :
            ΓêÇ c c',
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : Γäé) else 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (╬╛ i) = 0)
          (hqDual_orth :
            ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (qDual c) (╬╛ i) = 0)
          (hcontract_fix_╬╛ :
            ΓêÇ t i ╬╝,
              (Γêæ ╬╜ : Fin (d + 1), (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬╜ * ╬╛ i ╬╜) = ╬╛ i ╬╝)
          (hcontract_scale_q :
            ΓêÇ t c ╬╝,
              (Γêæ ╬╜ : Fin (d + 1), (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬╜ * q c ╬╜) =
                Real.exp (-t) * q c ╬╝) :
          ╬╛ Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n Γêº
          (ΓêÇ t,
            (fun i ╬╝ =>
              ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * a i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
          Tendsto
            (fun t : Γä¥ =>
              fun i ╬╝ =>
                ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * a i c * q c ╬╝)
            atTop (nhds ╬╛)

      /-- The singular alternative in Hall-Wightman Lemma 2, packaged as the
      actual limiting data used in the proof.  In the high scalar-rank
      alternative the output is just a complex Lorentz orbit equality.  In the
      low-rank alternative the data remain geometric: both endpoint
      configurations have Lorentz-orbit curves in the ordinary extended tube
      which contract to the same base configuration. -/
      structure BHW.HWSameSourceGramSingularContractionData
          (d n : Nat)
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) where
        base : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        curve_left : Γä¥ -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        curve_right : Γä¥ -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        base_mem : base Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        curve_left_mem : ΓêÇ t, curve_left t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        curve_right_mem : ΓêÇ t, curve_right t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        curve_left_tendsto_base : Tendsto curve_left atTop (nhds base)
        curve_right_tendsto_base : Tendsto curve_right atTop (nhds base)
        curve_left_orbit :
          ΓêÇ t, Γêâ ╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            curve_left t = BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z
        curve_right_orbit :
          ΓêÇ t, Γêâ ╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            curve_right t = BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ w

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz : z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzLow : BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          BHW.HWLowRankIsotropicNormalForm d n z w

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_to_contractionData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz : z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (N : BHW.HWLowRankIsotropicNormalForm d n z w) :
          BHW.HWSameSourceGramSingularContractionData d n z w

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 2 in the exact form used by the branch law:
      equal source Gram data give either a complex Lorentz orbit equality or
      the singular null-contraction data from the paper. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_fiberAlternative
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz : z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          (Γêâ ╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            w = BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) Γê¿
          BHW.HWSameSourceGramSingularContractionData d n z w

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 2, high scalar-rank case.  This deliberately uses
      the scalar-product rank predicate above.  It is not the repo's
      `SourceComplexGramRegularAt`, which is a local source-map regularity
      predicate used elsewhere for Gram-image charts. -/
      def BHW.sourceCoefficientEval
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          (Fin n -> Γäé) ΓåÆΓéù[Γäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :=
        { toFun := fun a ╬╝ => Γêæ i : Fin n, a i * z i ╬╝
          map_add' := by
            intro a b
            ext ╬╝
            simp [add_mul, Finset.sum_add_distrib]
          map_smul' := by
            intro c a
            ext ╬╝
            simp [mul_assoc, Finset.mul_sum] }

      def BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap
          (n : Nat)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) :
          (Fin n -> Γäé) ΓåÆΓéù[Γäé] (Fin n -> Γäé) :=
        { toFun := fun a j => Γêæ i : Fin n, a i * G i j
          map_add' := by
            intro a b
            ext j
            simp [add_mul, Finset.sum_add_distrib]
          map_smul' := by
            intro c a
            ext j
            simp [mul_assoc, Finset.mul_sum] }

      def BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel
          (n : Nat)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) :
          Submodule Γäé (Fin n -> Γäé) :=
        LinearMap.ker (BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n G)

      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_ker_le_gramKernel
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          LinearMap.ker (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z) Γëñ
            BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      /-- The left-adjoint map of the complex Minkowski form restricted to a
      finite source-vector span. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)) :
          M ΓåÆΓéù[Γäé] (M ΓåÆΓéù[Γäé] Γäé)

      /-- Radical of the complex Minkowski form restricted to a finite
      source-vector span. -/
      def BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)) : Submodule Γäé M :=
        LinearMap.ker (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap d M)

      /-- Rank of the restricted complex Minkowski form on `M`, expressed as
      `finrank M - finrank radical`. -/
      def BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)) : Nat :=
        Module.finrank Γäé M -
          Module.finrank Γäé (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M)

      /-- The rank of the scalar Gram matrix of a source configuration equals
      the rank of the complex Minkowski form restricted to the span generated
      by that configuration.  This is the quotient-rank bridge between the
      coefficient Gram kernel and the geometry of the source-vector span. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))

      /-- If the restricted form on the source-vector span is degenerate, the
      scalar Gram rank is below Hall-Wightman's direct-orbit threshold
      `min d n`.  The proof uses both facts: the span is generated by `n`
      vectors, and a proper subspace of the `(d+1)`-dimensional nondegenerate
      ambient space has dimension at most `d`. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_orbitThreshold_of_range_degenerate
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
          (hdeg :
            ┬¼ BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))) :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) <
            min d n

      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hzRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
            (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))

      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_eval_ker_eq_gramKernel
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hzRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z) :
          LinearMap.ker (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z) =
            BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_sourceCoefficientEval_ker_eq_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hzRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          LinearMap.ker (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z) =
            LinearMap.ker (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n w)

      /-- The well-defined span isometry in Hall-Wightman's high-rank branch.
      The map is induced from the common coefficient quotient; it is not the
      informal assignment `z i Γåª w i` until the coefficient kernels have been
      proved equal. -/
      structure BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData
          (d n : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) where
        M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
        N : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
        z_mem : ΓêÇ i, z i Γêê M
        w_mem : ΓêÇ i, w i Γêê N
        T : M ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] N
        T_preserves :
          ΓêÇ x y : M,
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d x y =
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (T x) (T y)
        T_z :
          ΓêÇ i, T Γƒ¿z i, z_mem iΓƒ⌐ = Γƒ¿w i, w_mem iΓƒ⌐

      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_spanIsometryData_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hzRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w

      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz : z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzOrbit : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          Γêâ ╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            w = BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 2, singular scalar-rank case.  This is the
      residual-frame contraction normal form of the paper: after reducing by a
      complex Lorentz frame, the discrepancy lies in a totally isotropic
      subspace orthogonal to the source span, and a one-parameter complex
      Lorentz family contracts that subspace. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singular_contractionData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz : z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzLow : BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          BHW.HWSameSourceGramSingularContractionData d n z w

      /-- Lemma 1 / local repo support: the ordinary extended-tube
      continuation is holomorphic and invariant under complex Lorentz
      transformations.  The theorem-2-facing variant carries both forward-tube
      holomorphy and forward-tube complex Lorentz invariance; the extended
      invariance is not available from `hF_cinv` alone. -/
      theorem BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ΓêÇ (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) = F z)
          (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz : z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          BHW.extendF F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) =
            BHW.extendF F z

      /-- Lemma 2, singular/low-rank case: equal source Gram data have the
      Hall-Wightman residual-frame normal form: after one complex Lorentz
      transform the discrepancy is contained in a totally isotropic subspace
      orthogonal to the source span, together with the one-parameter complex
      Lorentz contraction used in the paper.  The output is stated directly as
      value equality because the proof is a continuity/limit argument, not a
      single orbit equality. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singularLimit_extendF_eq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ΓêÇ (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) = F z)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz : z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (hsingular :
            BHW.HWSameSourceGramSingularContractionData d n z w) :
          BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the Hall-Wightman Lemma-2 alternative:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_fiberAlternative ... := by
        by_cases hzOrbit : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z
        ┬╖ exact Or.inl
            (BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit
              (d := d) hd (n := n) hz hw hzOrbit hgram)
        ┬╖ exact Or.inr
            (BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singular_contractionData
              (d := d) hd (n := n) hz hw
              (by
                simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt,
                  BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt] using
                  ((BHW.hwSourceGram_lowRank_iff_not_orbitRank
                    (d := d) (n := n) hd).2 hzOrbit))
              hgram)
      ```

      Rank convention audit: Hall-Wightman is written for four-vectors.  In
      the outline of Lemma 2 the direct orbit case is scalar-product matrix
      rank three or four in the hard arity range, with the corresponding
      nonsingular-matrix exception for small arity; ranks below that threshold
      require the limiting argument.  Lemma 3 repeats the same rank split for
      neighborhoods.  In the repo notation the spacetime vector dimension is
      `d + 1`, so the dimension-general direct orbit threshold is scalar rank
      at least `min d n`.  Equivalently: if `n >= d`, the orbit cases are rank
      `d` or `d + 1`; if `n < d`, the orbit case is rank `n`.  The lower branch
      is rank `< min d n`.  This is why the proof-doc predicate is
      `HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt`; it must not be replaced by
      `SourceComplexGramRegularAt`.  The latter is still correct for the later
      local-image theorem that chooses a regular source point, but it is not
      Hall-Wightman's Lemma-2 scalar-rank split.

      The proof of `hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit` is the
      finite-dimensional complex orthogonal linear algebra in Hall-Wightman
      Lemma 2, with the coefficient quotient made explicit.  Let
      `evalZ` and `evalW` be the coefficient-evaluation maps
      `a Γåª Γêæ i, a i ΓÇó z i` and `a Γåª Γêæ i, a i ΓÇó w i`.  Equal source Gram
      matrices give the same coefficient Gram bilinear form.  In the high
      scalar-rank range, Hall-Wightman's rank computation proves
      `ker evalZ = sourceCoefficientGramKernel G = ker evalW`; this is the
      theorem `hw_highRank_sourceCoefficientEval_ker_eq_of_sameSourceGram`.
      Therefore the assignment induced by the identity map on coefficient
      space descends to a linear equivalence between `range evalZ` and
      `range evalW`, sends each `z i` to `w i`, and preserves the complex
      Minkowski bilinear form.  This is packaged as
      `HWHighRankSpanIsometryData`.  Applying
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension` to that span isometry gives a full
      `ComplexLorentzGroup d` element carrying every `z i` to `w i`.  No
      source-map regularity theorem is used in this high-rank branch.

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate`: let
      `M := LinearMap.range (sourceCoefficientEval d n z)`.  The source Gram
      matrix is the matrix of the restricted complex Minkowski form pulled
      back along the surjection `sourceCoefficientEval d n z : (Fin n -> Γäé) ->
      M`.  Therefore the first required bridge is
      `sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range`: quotient the
      coefficient space by `ker evalZ`, identify the quotient with `M` by
      `evalZ.quotKerEquivRange`, and observe that the pulled-back Gram
      bilinear form has radical
      `sourceCoefficientGramKernel`; its quotient rank is exactly
      `finrank M - finrank (restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M)`.

      The first bridge is implemented through three explicit support facts,
      not by identifying two different notions of rank informally:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap
          (n : Nat)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G =
            Module.finrank Γäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n G))

      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel_eq_eval_preimage_radical
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
            ((BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))).map
              (Submodule.subtype
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)))).comap
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)

      theorem BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_restrictedRank
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          Module.finrank Γäé
            (LinearMap.range
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))) =
          BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d
            (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))
      ```

      Proof transcript for the first bridge:
      `sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap` is the
      standard finite-matrix identification of the rank of the matrix
      `(G i j)` with the finrank of the range of the associated linear map
      `a Γåª (j Γåª Γêæ i, a i * G i j)`.  For the preimage-radical theorem, unfold
      all three maps.  A coefficient vector `a` is in the scalar Gram kernel
      exactly when, for every coefficient vector `b`,
      `Γêæ i j, a i * b j * sourceMinkowskiGram d n z i j = 0`; after
      rearranging finite sums, this says that the vector
      `sourceCoefficientEval d n z a`, viewed as an element of
      `M := LinearMap.range (sourceCoefficientEval d n z)`, pairs to zero
      with every element of `M`, which is precisely membership in
      `restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M`.

      Lean-shaped proof of the quotient rank computation:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_restrictedRank
          ... := by
        let E := Fin n -> Γäé
        let evalZ := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
        let M := LinearMap.range evalZ
        let gramMap :=
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        have hker_pre :
            LinearMap.ker gramMap =
              ((BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M).map
                (Submodule.subtype M)).comap evalZ :=
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel_eq_eval_preimage_radical
            (d := d) n z
        have hquot :
            (E Γº╕ LinearMap.ker evalZ) ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] M :=
          evalZ.quotKerEquivRange
        have hrad_quot :
            ((LinearMap.ker gramMap).map
              (Submodule.mkQ (LinearMap.ker evalZ))) ΓëâΓéù[Γäé]
              BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M := by
          -- Use `hker_pre` and `hquot`: the scalar Gram radical modulo
          -- `ker evalZ` is exactly the restricted radical in `M`.
          exact BHW.scalarGramKernel_quot_equiv_restrictedRadical
            (d := d) n z hker_pre hquot
        have hrank :
            Module.finrank Γäé (LinearMap.range gramMap) =
              Module.finrank Γäé M -
                Module.finrank Γäé
                  (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M) := by
          -- Rank-nullity for `gramMap`, then replace
          -- `finrank E - finrank ker evalZ` by `finrank M` and
          -- replace the extra quotient radical by `hrad_quot`.
          exact
            BHW.rank_sourceCoefficientGramMap_via_restrictedRadical
              (d := d) n z hker_pre hquot hrad_quot
        simpa [BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank] using hrank

      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range
          ... := by
        rw [BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap]
        exact
          BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_restrictedRank
            (d := d) n z
      ```

      The two helper names
      `scalarGramKernel_quot_equiv_restrictedRadical` and
      `rank_sourceCoefficientGramMap_via_restrictedRadical` are proof-local
      linear algebra helpers if the implementation needs them; they are not
      new mathematical route surfaces.  Their content is exactly quotienting
      by `ker evalZ`, applying rank-nullity, and transporting the radical
      through `evalZ.quotKerEquivRange`.

      The second bridge is
      `sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_orbitThreshold_of_range_degenerate`.  If the
      restricted radical is nonzero, then `M` is not the whole ambient space:
      otherwise ambient nondegeneracy of complex Minkowski space would force
      the radical vector to be zero.  Since the ambient vector space has
      dimension `d + 1`, a proper subspace has dimension at most `d`.  Also,
      `M` is generated by `n` source vectors, so `finrank M <= n`.  Hence a
      nonzero radical gives
      `restrictedMinkowskiRank d M <= finrank M - 1 < min d n`, and the rank
      bridge gives
      `sourceGramMatrixRank n (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < min d n`.
      This contradicts `HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z`.  Thus the restricted
      radical is zero, which is exactly
      `ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M`.  This is the
      finite-dimensional rank-radical step in Hall-Wightman Lemma 2; it is
      not the later source-map regularity theorem.

      The rank-radical bridge is itself decomposed into the following
      finite-dimensional support facts:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical_nontrivial_of_degenerate
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hdeg :
            ┬¼ BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          0 < Module.finrank Γäé
            (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M)

      theorem BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank_lt_finrank_of_degenerate
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hdeg :
            ┬¼ BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d M <
            Module.finrank Γäé M

      theorem BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientEval_le
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          Module.finrank Γäé
            (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) <= n

      theorem BHW.finrank_restrictedSpan_le_d_of_degenerate
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {M : Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hdeg :
            ┬¼ BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          Module.finrank Γäé M <= d
      ```

      Proof transcript for these support facts:
      `restrictedMinkowskiRadical_nontrivial_of_degenerate` unfolds
      `ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace` and
      `restrictedMinkowskiRadical`; the negated universal statement gives
      `x : M` with `x Γëá 0` and zero pairing against every `y : M`, hence a
      nonzero element of the radical and positive finrank.  The range bound is
      the standard finite-dimensional inequality
      `finrank (LinearMap.range evalZ) <= finrank (Fin n -> Γäé) = n`.
      `restrictedMinkowskiRank_lt_finrank_of_degenerate` is then the arithmetic
      statement
      `finrank M - finrank radical < finrank M`: the radical is a subspace of
      `M`, so its positive finrank is at most `finrank M`, and `Nat.sub_lt`
      applies.
      For `finrank_restrictedSpan_le_d_of_degenerate`, first prove
      `M Γëá Γèñ`; if `M = Γèñ`, transport the ambient nondegeneracy of the complex
      Minkowski form through the subtype equivalence and contradict `hdeg`.
      A proper submodule of the `(d + 1)`-dimensional ambient space has
      finrank `< d + 1`, hence `<= d`.

      Lean-shaped proof of the rank-drop theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_orbitThreshold_of_range_degenerate
          ... := by
        let M := LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)
        have hrad_pos :
            0 < Module.finrank Γäé
              (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M) :=
          BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical_nontrivial_of_degenerate
            (d := d) hdeg
        have hM_le_n : Module.finrank Γäé M <= n :=
          BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientEval_le
            (d := d) n z
        have hM_le_d : Module.finrank Γäé M <= d :=
          BHW.finrank_restrictedSpan_le_d_of_degenerate
            (d := d) hd hdeg
        have hrank_lt_M :
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d M <
              Module.finrank Γäé M :=
          BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank_lt_finrank_of_degenerate
            (d := d) hdeg
        have hrank_lt_d :
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d M < d :=
          lt_of_lt_of_le hrank_lt_M hM_le_d
        have hrank_lt_n :
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d M < n :=
          lt_of_lt_of_le hrank_lt_M hM_le_n
        rw [BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range
          (d := d) n z]
        exact lt_min hrank_lt_d hrank_lt_n
      ```

      The displayed proof is meant to be implemented with Mathlib's actual
      finite-dimensional submodule lemmas for `Module.finrank`; if a helper
      name differs, the obligation is still exactly the three support facts
      above.  No analyticity, connectedness, or BHW permutation machinery is
      involved in this rank-drop theorem.

      Lean-shaped proof of the nondegeneracy theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate ... := by
        by_contra hdeg
        have hlt :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) <
              min d n :=
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_orbitThreshold_of_range_degenerate
            (d := d) hd n z hdeg
        exact not_lt_of_ge hzRank hlt
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the basic coefficient-kernel inclusion:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_ker_le_gramKernel ... := by
        intro a ha
        change
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a = 0
        ext j
        have hEval_zero :
            BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z a = 0 :=
          LinearMap.mem_ker.mp ha
        have hpair :
            BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z a) (z j) = 0 := by
          rw [hEval_zero]
          simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear]
        -- Expanding the pairing of `Γêæ i, a i ΓÇó z i` with `z j`
        -- gives exactly the `j`th coordinate of the scalar Gram-kernel map.
        simpa [BHW.sourceCoefficientEval,
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap,
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel,
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram,
          BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear,
          Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, Finset.sum_comm,
          mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm] using hpair
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the high-rank coefficient-kernel equality:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_eval_ker_eq_gramKernel ... := by
        let evalZ := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
        apply le_antisymm
        ┬╖ exact BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_ker_le_gramKernel (d := d) n z
        ┬╖ intro a ha
          have hNondeg :
              BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
                (LinearMap.range evalZ) :=
            BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate
              (d := d) hd (n := n) hzRank
          have horth :
              ΓêÇ v : LinearMap.range evalZ,
                BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                  (evalZ a) (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) = 0 := by
            intro v
            rcases v with Γƒ¿_, b, rflΓƒ⌐
            have ha0 :
                BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a = 0 :=
              LinearMap.mem_ker.mp ha
            have ha0_coord :
                ΓêÇ j,
                  BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a j = 0 :=
              fun j => congrFun ha0 j
            -- Expand the bilinear pairing of the two coefficient sums.
            -- It is a finite linear combination of the zero coordinates
            -- of the scalar Gram map applied to `a`.
            calc
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (evalZ a) (evalZ b)
                  =
                Γêæ j : Fin n,
                  b j *
                    BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a j := by
                    simp [evalZ, BHW.sourceCoefficientEval,
                      BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap,
                      BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram,
                      BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear,
                      Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, Finset.sum_comm,
                      mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
              _ = 0 := by simp [ha0_coord]
          have hEval_mem : evalZ a Γêê LinearMap.range evalZ := Γƒ¿a, rflΓƒ⌐
          have hEval_zero :
              (Γƒ¿evalZ a, hEval_memΓƒ⌐ :
                LinearMap.range evalZ) = 0 :=
            hNondeg Γƒ¿evalZ a, hEval_memΓƒ⌐ horth
          simpa [LinearMap.mem_ker] using
            congrArg Subtype.val hEval_zero
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the kernel transport theorem used by the quotient
      span isometry:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_sourceCoefficientEval_ker_eq_of_sameSourceGram
          ... := by
        let evalZ := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
        let evalW := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n w
        have hwRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n w :=
          (BHW.hwSourceGramOrbitRankAt_of_sourceGram_eq
            (d := d) (n := n) hgram).1 hzRank
        calc
          LinearMap.ker evalZ
              =
            BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) :=
                BHW.hw_highRank_eval_ker_eq_gramKernel
                  (d := d) hd (n := n) hzRank
          _ =
            BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) := by
                rw [hgram]
          _ = LinearMap.ker evalW :=
                (BHW.hw_highRank_eval_ker_eq_gramKernel
                  (d := d) hd (n := n) hwRank).symm
      ```

      Thus the assignment on coefficient classes is genuinely well-defined:
      the identity map on coefficient space descends only after the two kernel
      equalities have been proved.  The proof does not use local source-map
      regularity, connectedness of the scalar domain, or any adjacent
      `S'_n`/PET information.

      The proof of
      `hw_sameSourceGram_singular_contractionData` is the paper's
      null-contraction construction: put the common lower-rank span into a
      standard frame, write the remaining discrepancy in a totally isotropic
      residual frame orthogonal to that span, choose a complex Lorentz
      one-parameter family contracting the whole residual frame, and define the
      two curves so that each parameter is in the ordinary extended tube and
      each curve stays in the complex Lorentz orbit of its own endpoint.  In
      four spacetime dimensions this residual frame has length at most one,
      matching Hall-Wightman's displayed `a_i w`; the dimension-general Lean
      surface must not assume that collapse.  No analytic equality is part of
      either geometric theorem.

      Lean-shaped proof of the high-rank orbit and low-rank geometric
      theorems:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_spanIsometryData_of_sameSourceGram ... := by
        let evalZ := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
        let evalW := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n w
        have hker : LinearMap.ker evalZ = LinearMap.ker evalW :=
          BHW.hw_highRank_sourceCoefficientEval_ker_eq_of_sameSourceGram
            (d := d) hd (n := n) hzRank hgram
        let QZ : ((Fin n -> Γäé) Γº╕ LinearMap.ker evalZ) ΓëâΓéù[Γäé]
            LinearMap.range evalZ :=
          evalZ.quotKerEquivRange
        let QW : ((Fin n -> Γäé) Γº╕ LinearMap.ker evalW) ΓëâΓéù[Γäé]
            LinearMap.range evalW :=
          evalW.quotKerEquivRange
        let hkerEquiv :
            ((Fin n -> Γäé) Γº╕ LinearMap.ker evalZ) ΓëâΓéù[Γäé]
              ((Fin n -> Γäé) Γº╕ LinearMap.ker evalW) :=
          Submodule.quotEquivOfEq _ _ hker
        let T : LinearMap.range evalZ ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] LinearMap.range evalW :=
          QZ.symm.trans (hkerEquiv.trans QW)
        refine
          { M := LinearMap.range evalZ
            N := LinearMap.range evalW
            z_mem := ?_
            w_mem := ?_
            T := T
            T_preserves := ?_
            T_z := ?_ }
        ┬╖ intro i
          exact Γƒ¿Pi.single i (1 : Γäé), by
            ext ╬╝
            simp [evalZ, BHW.sourceCoefficientEval]Γƒ⌐
        ┬╖ intro i
          exact Γƒ¿Pi.single i (1 : Γäé), by
            ext ╬╝
            simp [evalW, BHW.sourceCoefficientEval]Γƒ⌐
        ┬╖ intro x y
          rcases x with Γƒ¿xv, hxvΓƒ⌐
          rcases hxv with Γƒ¿ax, haxΓƒ⌐
          rcases y with Γƒ¿yv, hyvΓƒ⌐
          rcases hyv with Γƒ¿ay, hayΓƒ⌐
          -- Expand both sides through the coefficient representatives
          -- `ax`, `ay`.  The quotient construction of `T` sends the class of
          -- `ax` to the class of the same coefficient vector for `evalW`, and
          -- the bilinear forms are equal because `hgram` identifies every
          -- source Gram coefficient.
          subst xv
          subst yv
          simp [T, QZ, QW, hkerEquiv, evalZ, evalW,
            LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange_apply_mk,
            BHW.sourceCoefficientEval, BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram, hgram,
            Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm,
            mul_comm]
        ┬╖ intro i
          apply Subtype.ext
          ext ╬╝
          simp [T, QZ, QW, hkerEquiv, evalZ, evalW,
            LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange_apply_mk,
            BHW.sourceCoefficientEval]

      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit ... := by
        rcases BHW.hw_highRank_spanIsometryData_of_sameSourceGram
            (d := d) hd (n := n) hzOrbit hgram with
          Γƒ¿M, N, hzM, hwN, T, hT_preserves, hT_zΓƒ⌐
        rcases BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension
            (d := d) hd T hT_preserves with
          Γƒ¿╬¢, h╬¢Γƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿╬¢, ?_Γƒ⌐
        ext i ╬╝
        have h╬¢i :
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢ (z i) = w i := by
          have h╬¢_sub := h╬¢ Γƒ¿z i, hzM iΓƒ⌐
          have hT_sub := hT_z i
          exact Subtype.ext_iff.mp (h╬¢_sub.trans hT_sub)
        simpa [BHW.complexLorentzAction_apply_eq_vectorAction] using
          congrArg (fun v => v ╬╝) h╬¢i

      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singular_contractionData ... := by
        have N :=
          BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_of_sameSourceGram
            (d := d) hd (n := n) hz hw hzLow hgram
        exact BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_to_contractionData
          (d := d) hd hz hw N
      ```

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_of_sameSourceGram`: let
      `r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)`.
      Because the source Gram matrix is symmetric, the checked theorem
      `BHW.exists_sourcePrincipalMinor_ne_zero_of_sourceSymmetricRank` gives an
      injective `I : Fin r -> Fin n` with nonzero principal minor on the common
      Gram matrix.  This is the Lean replacement for the paper's implicit
      "choose independent scalar products" step.  The nonzero principal block
      identifies a nondegenerate rank-`r` selected span; equality of the two
      full Gram matrices gives the same scalar-coordinate coefficients and the
      same residual radical pairings for the remaining vectors.  Use the
      selected-span Gram equality to build the isometry of the two
      nondegenerate spans, then extend it to a full element of
      `ComplexLorentzGroup d` by `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension`.

      Lean-shaped selected-span coefficient construction:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanFrame_of_sameSourceGram ... := by
        let G := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z
        let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G
        have hGsym : G Γêê BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n := by
          intro i j
          exact BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_symm d n z i j
        have hrank :
            (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => G i j).rank = r := by
          rfl
        rcases BHW.exists_sourcePrincipalMinor_ne_zero_of_sourceSymmetricRank
            hGsym hrank with
          Γƒ¿I, hI_inj, hminorΓƒ⌐
        let A := BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G
        have hunit : IsUnit A.det := by
          simpa [A, BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix, BHW.sourceMatrixMinor]
            using hminor
        let coeff : Fin n -> Fin r -> Γäé :=
          fun i => BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G i
        have hsel :
            ΓêÇ a b,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z (I a) (I b) =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w (I a) (I b) := by
          intro a b
          simpa [G] using congrFun (congrFun hgram (I a)) (I b)
        have hleft_orth :
            ΓêÇ i a,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (z i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó z (I b))
                (z (I a)) = 0 := by
          simpa [coeff, G] using
            BHW.hw_lowRank_selected_residual_orthogonal
              (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (I := I)
              hunit
        have hright_orth :
            ΓêÇ i a,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (w i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó w (I b))
                (w (I a)) = 0 := by
          -- Same coefficient formula, with the principal matrix rewritten by
          -- `hgram`.
          have hunitW :
              IsUnit
                (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)).det := by
            simpa [G, hgram] using hunit
          simpa [coeff, G, hgram] using
            BHW.hw_lowRank_selected_residual_orthogonal
              (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := w) (I := I)
              hunitW
        have hres_pair :
            ΓêÇ i j,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (z i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó z (I b))
                (z j - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ΓÇó z (I b)) =
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (w i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó w (I b))
                (w j - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ΓÇó w (I b)) := by
          simpa [coeff, G] using
            BHW.hw_lowRank_residual_pairing_eq_of_sameSourceGram
              (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (w := w)
              (I := I) hunit hgram
        have hleft_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ i j,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (z i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó z (I b))
                (z j - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ΓÇó z (I b)) = 0 := by
          simpa [coeff, G] using
            BHW.hw_lowRank_selected_residual_pairing_zero
              (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (I := I)
              (by rfl) hminor
        have hright_pair_zero :
            ΓêÇ i j,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d
                (w i - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ΓÇó w (I b))
                (w j - Γêæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ΓÇó w (I b)) = 0 := by
          intro i j
          exact (hres_pair i j).symm.trans (hleft_pair_zero i j)
        refine Γƒ¿r, I, rfl, ?_Γƒ⌐
        exact
          { I_injective := hI_inj
            principal_minor_ne := by simpa [G] using hminor
            selected_gram_eq := hsel
            coeff := coeff
            left_residual_orth := hleft_orth
            right_residual_orth := hright_orth
            residual_pairing_eq := hres_pair
            left_residual_pair_zero := hleft_pair_zero
            right_residual_pair_zero := hright_pair_zero }
      ```

      The support theorem
      `BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff_projection_eq` is just
      `row_i * AΓü╗┬╣ * A = row_i` for the selected principal Gram matrix
      `A`; `BHW.hw_lowRank_selected_residual_orthogonal` expands the
      bilinear pairing and applies that identity.  The residual pairing
      equality theorem expands both residual pairings into a finite polynomial
      in the common source Gram entries and the common coefficient formula,
      then rewrites by `hgram`.  Thus no source regularity, connectedness, or
      analytic continuation is used in this selected-span step.
      The zero residual-pairing theorem is the Schur-complement rank step:
      with the selected principal block invertible and the full source Gram
      matrix having rank exactly `r`, the Schur complement of that block is
      zero.  After expanding the projection coefficients, that Schur
      complement is precisely the residual-residual Gram matrix.  In Lean it
      should use the checked Schur theorem
      `BHW.rank_eq_card_iff_reindexed_schur_complement_eq_zero` (or its
      source-specialized corollary) plus `BHW.sourceMatrixMinor` to identify
      the determinant-unit selected block.

      The aligned residual-frame theorem is then a finite-dimensional Witt
      alignment statement, not a notation choice, and it has two separate
      stages.

      First prove
