      `BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanAlignment_of_selectedSpanFrame`.  Let
      `Mz := Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range fun a : Fin r => z (I a))` and
      `Mw := Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range fun a : Fin r => w (I a))`.  The
      nonzero principal minor gives linear independence of the selected
      vectors on both sides and nondegeneracy of the restricted form on both
      selected spans.  The field `S.selected_gram_eq` defines the linear
      isometry `Tsel : Mz ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] Mw` by
      `Tsel Γƒ¿z (I a), _Γƒ⌐ = Γƒ¿w (I a), _Γƒ⌐`; well-definedness and preservation of
      the form are proved by expanding in the selected basis and rewriting by
      `S.selected_gram_eq`.  Witt extension gives
      `╬¢sel : ComplexLorentzGroup d` with
      `╬¢sel ΓÇó z (I a) = w (I a)`.  Now set
      `M := Mw`,
      `╬╛ i := Γêæ b : Fin r, S.coeff i b ΓÇó w (I b)`,
      `leftResidual i := ╬¢sel ΓÇó z i - ╬╛ i`, and
      `rightResidual i := w i - ╬╛ i`.  The orthogonality fields in
      `HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame`, transported by `╬¢sel` on the left, prove
      both residual families are orthogonal to `M`; the Schur-zero fields,
      again transported by `╬¢sel` on the left, prove both residual families
      have zero mutual pairings.  This is why
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_after_selectedAlignment` takes the
      alignment object as input: before `╬¢sel` is applied, there is no common
      selected span in which the statement is true.

      Lean-shaped selected-alignment skeleton:

      ```lean
      rcases BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanAlignment_of_selectedSpanFrame
          (d := d) hd (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (w := w)
          (I := I) S with
        Γƒ¿AΓƒ⌐
      have hleft_residual_mem :
          ΓêÇ i,
            A.leftResidual i Γêê
              Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range A.leftResidual) := by
        intro i
        exact Submodule.subset_span Γƒ¿i, rflΓƒ⌐
      have hright_residual_mem :
          ΓêÇ i,
            A.rightResidual i Γêê
              Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range A.rightResidual) := by
        intro i
        exact Submodule.subset_span Γƒ¿i, rflΓƒ⌐
      ```

      Second prove the common residual-frame theorem
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_alignResidualSubspaces_to_commonIsotropicFrame`.
      Apply `BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_after_selectedAlignment A` to
      get `Rleft` and `Rright` inside `A.Mßù«`.  The proof of the alignment
      theorem is the standard Witt-extension argument in the nondegenerate
      quotient/complement of `A.M`: choose a maximal totally isotropic
      subspace `Q` of `A.Mßù«` containing `Rright`; choose an injective linear
      map from `Rleft` into `Q` (possible because both are totally isotropic
      and `Q` has maximal Witt dimension in the complement); the direct sum of
      the identity on `A.M` and this zero-form isometry on `Rleft` preserves
      the complex Minkowski form on its domain, because both residual
      subspaces are orthogonal to `A.M` and have zero internal form.  Extending
      this partial isometry by
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry` gives `╬¢fix`,
      fixing `A.M`, with `╬¢fix(Rleft) Γèå Q` and `Rright Γèå Q`.  Choose a finite
      independent frame `q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé` for `Q`.  Membership
      in `Submodule.span Γäé (Set.range q)` is converted to coefficient
      functions by `BHW.exists_coefficients_of_mem_span_finite_frame`; this
      step is explicit so that `aLeft` and `aRight` are not hidden choices.

      Lean-shaped residual-frame extraction:

      ```lean
      rcases BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_after_selectedAlignment
          (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (w := w)
          (I := I) (S := S) A with
        Γƒ¿Rleft, Rright, hRleft_eq, hRright_eq,
          hRleft_orth, hRright_orth, hRleft_iso, hRright_isoΓƒ⌐
      rcases BHW.complexMinkowski_alignResidualSubspaces_to_commonIsotropicFrame
          (d := d) hd
          (M := A.M) (Rz := Rleft) (Rw := Rright)
          A.M_nondeg hRleft_orth hRright_orth hRleft_iso hRright_iso with
        Γƒ¿╬¢fix, s, q, h╬¢fix_M, hq_independent, hq_pair_zero, hq_orth_M,
          hleft_span, hright_spanΓƒ⌐
      have hq_orth :
          ΓêÇ c i, BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q c) (A.╬╛ i) = 0 := by
        intro c i
        exact hq_orth_M c Γƒ¿A.╬╛ i, A.╬╛_mem iΓƒ⌐
      choose aLeft h_aLeft using
        fun i =>
          BHW.exists_coefficients_of_mem_span_finite_frame
            (d := d) (s := s) (q := q)
            (v := BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬¢fix (A.leftResidual i))
            (hleft_span Γƒ¿A.leftResidual i, by
              simpa [hRleft_eq] using hleft_residual_mem iΓƒ⌐)
      choose aRight h_aRight using
        fun i =>
          BHW.exists_coefficients_of_mem_span_finite_frame
            (d := d) (s := s) (q := q)
            (v := A.rightResidual i)
            (hright_span Γƒ¿A.rightResidual i, by
              simpa [hRright_eq] using hright_residual_mem iΓƒ⌐)
      let ╬¢0 : ComplexLorentzGroup d := ╬¢fix * A.╬¢sel
      ```

      The equations
      `(BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢0 z) i =
      A.╬╛ i + Γêæ c, aLeft i c * q c` and
      `w i = A.╬╛ i + Γêæ c, aRight i c * q c` then follow by expanding
      `BHW.complexLorentzAction_mul`, using `A.left_decomp`,
      `A.right_decomp`, the fact that `╬¢fix` fixes `A.╬╛ i Γêê A.M`, and the
      coefficient equations `h_aLeft` and `h_aRight`.  This is the
      dimension-general version of Hall-Wightman's four-dimensional line
      saying the singular residuals lie along null directions orthogonal to
      the common base.  Choose a dual isotropic frame `qDual` by the Witt
      decomposition of `A.Mßù«`; in Lean this is exactly
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicDualFrame_of_residualFrame`, applied to
      `A.M`, its nondegeneracy proof, the membership `A.╬╛ i Γêê A.M`, the
      orthogonality of `q` to all `m : A.M`, and the recorded
      `LinearIndependent Γäé q` after zero residual directions have been
      removed.  The returned dual fields `qDual_pair_zero`, `q_dual`, and
      `qDual_orth` are stored in `HWLowRankIsotropicNormalForm`; otherwise the
      later contraction family would be an unexplained existence of Lorentz
      transformations.  Complete `A.M Γèò span q Γèò span qDual` to an orthogonal
      basis, and define the complex Lorentz boost which fixes the common span,
      sends every `q c` to `Real.exp (-t) ΓÇó q c`, and sends every corresponding
      dual vector to `Real.exp t ΓÇó qDual c`.  Applying this boost to the two
      endpoint configurations `╬¢0 z` and `w` proves both contracted
      configurations remain in the extended tube; the scalar `Real.exp (-t)`
      tends to zero, so both contracted configurations tend to `A.╬╛`.

      The `base_mem`, `contracted_left_mem`, and `contracted_right_mem` fields
      of `HWLowRankIsotropicNormalForm` are not independent assumptions.  They
      are filled from
      `BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube`, once for the
      endpoint `BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢0 z` with coefficients `aLeft`, and
      once for the endpoint `w` with coefficients `aRight`.  The base obtained
      from the two applications is the same `╬╛`; the contracted configurations
      are the special coefficient choices
      `fun i c => Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c` and
      `fun i c => Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c`.

      Lean-shaped final assembly inside
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_of_sameSourceGram` after the
      selected span, residual frame, dual frame, and contraction family have
      been constructed:

      ```lean
      have hleft_endpoint :
          (fun i ╬╝ => ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, aLeft i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
            BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        simpa [left_eq] using
          BHW.complexLorentzAction_mem_extendedTube
            (d := d) n ╬¢0 hz
      have hright_endpoint :
          (fun i ╬╝ => ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, aRight i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
            BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        simpa [right_eq] using hw
      rcases BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          (d := d) hd hleft_endpoint
          q_pair_zero qDual_pair_zero q_dual q_orth qDual_orth with
        Γƒ¿hbase_mem, hleft_allΓƒ⌐
      rcases BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          (d := d) hd hright_endpoint
          q_pair_zero qDual_pair_zero q_dual q_orth qDual_orth with
        Γƒ¿_, hright_allΓƒ⌐
      have hleft_contract_mem :
          ΓêÇ t,
            (fun i ╬╝ =>
              ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        intro t
        simpa [mul_assoc] using
          hleft_all (fun i c => Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c)
      have hright_contract_mem :
          ΓêÇ t,
            (fun i ╬╝ =>
              ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c * q c ╬╝) Γêê
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        intro t
        simpa [mul_assoc] using
          hright_all (fun i c => Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c)
      have hleft_tendsto :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : Γä¥ =>
              fun i ╬╝ =>
                ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c * q c ╬╝)
            atTop (nhds ╬╛) := by
        -- finite-coordinate continuity plus
        -- `Real.tendsto_exp_neg_atTop_nhds_zero`
        exact BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg
          (a := aLeft) (q := q)
      have hright_tendsto :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : Γä¥ =>
              fun i ╬╝ =>
                ╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c * q c ╬╝)
            atTop (nhds ╬╛) := by
        exact BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg
          (a := aRight) (q := q)
      exact
        { ╬¢0 := ╬¢0
          ╬╛ := ╬╛
          s := s
          aLeft := aLeft
          aRight := aRight
          q := q
          qDual := qDual
          left_eq := left_eq
          right_eq := right_eq
          base_mem := hbase_mem
          q_pair_zero := q_pair_zero
          qDual_pair_zero := qDual_pair_zero
          q_dual := q_dual
          q_orth := q_orth
          qDual_orth := qDual_orth
          contract := contract
          contract_fix_╬╛ := contract_fix_╬╛
          contract_scale_q := contract_scale_q
          contract_scale_qDual := contract_scale_qDual
          contracted_left_mem := hleft_contract_mem
          contracted_right_mem := hright_contract_mem
          contracted_left_tendsto := hleft_tendsto
          contracted_right_tendsto := hright_tendsto }
      ```

      The helper `BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg` is pure finite
      topology: each coordinate is a finite sum of constants multiplied by
      `Real.exp (-t)`, so it tends to zero; adding the constant coordinate
      `╬╛ i ╬╝` gives convergence to `╬╛`.  It may be private in Lean.

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_to_contractionData`: define
        `base := N.╬╛`,
        `curve_left t :=
          fun i ╬╝ =>
            N.╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin N.s,
              Real.exp (-t) * N.aLeft i c * N.q c ╬╝`,
        and
        `curve_right t :=
          fun i ╬╝ =>
            N.╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin N.s,
              Real.exp (-t) * N.aRight i c * N.q c ╬╝`.
        The left and right curves are in `BHW.ExtendedTube d n` by
        `N.contracted_left_mem t` and `N.contracted_right_mem t`.  The base
        point is in the extended tube by the `N.base_mem` field, which was
        produced upstream by
        `BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube`; it is not
        inferred from closedness of `ExtendedTube`.  The two curves tend to
        `base` by `N.contracted_left_tendsto` and
        `N.contracted_right_tendsto`.  For each `t`, the Lorentz transform
        `N.contract t * N.╬¢0` carries `z` to `curve_left t`, and
        `N.contract t` carries `w` to `curve_right t`, by `N.left_eq`,
        `N.right_eq`, `N.contract_fix_╬╛`, and `N.contract_scale_q`.  These are
        exactly the two orbit fields consumed by the analytic limiting proof.

      Lean-shaped proof of
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_to_contractionData`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_to_contractionData ... := by
        let curve_left : Γä¥ -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé :=
          fun t i ╬╝ =>
            N.╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin N.s,
              Real.exp (-t) * N.aLeft i c * N.q c ╬╝
        let curve_right : Γä¥ -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé :=
          fun t i ╬╝ =>
            N.╬╛ i ╬╝ + Γêæ c : Fin N.s,
              Real.exp (-t) * N.aRight i c * N.q c ╬╝
        refine
          { base := N.╬╛
            curve_left := curve_left
            curve_right := curve_right
            base_mem := N.base_mem
            curve_left_mem := N.contracted_left_mem
            curve_right_mem := N.contracted_right_mem
            curve_left_tendsto_base := by
              simpa [curve_left] using N.contracted_left_tendsto
            curve_right_tendsto_base := by
              simpa [curve_right] using N.contracted_right_tendsto
            curve_left_orbit := ?_
            curve_right_orbit := ?_ }
        ┬╖ intro t
          refine Γƒ¿N.contract t * N.╬¢0, ?_Γƒ⌐
          ext i ╬╝
          calc
            curve_left t i ╬╝
                =
              BHW.complexLorentzAction (N.contract t)
                (BHW.complexLorentzAction N.╬¢0 z) i ╬╝ := by
                  -- Expand `N.left_eq`, distribute the finite sum through
                  -- the matrix action, and use `N.contract_fix_╬╛` plus
                  -- `N.contract_scale_q`.
                  simp [curve_left, BHW.complexLorentzAction, N.left_eq,
                    N.contract_fix_╬╛, N.contract_scale_q,
                    Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc,
                    mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
            _ =
              BHW.complexLorentzAction (N.contract t * N.╬¢0) z i ╬╝ := by
                  simpa using
                    (BHW.complexLorentzAction_mul
                      (d := d) (n := n) (N.contract t) N.╬¢0 z).symm
        ┬╖ intro t
          refine Γƒ¿N.contract t, ?_Γƒ⌐
          ext i ╬╝
          -- Same finite-coordinate calculation, now using `N.right_eq`.
          simp [curve_right, BHW.complexLorentzAction, N.right_eq,
            N.contract_fix_╬╛, N.contract_scale_q,
            Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc,
            mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
      ```

      Proof transcript for `BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv`:
      unfold the ordinary extended tube representation
      `z = complexLorentzAction ╬¢ΓéÇ wΓéÇ` with `wΓéÇ Γêê ForwardTube d n`; then
      `complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z =
      complexLorentzAction (╬¢ * ╬¢ΓéÇ) wΓéÇ`.  Unfold `BHW.extendF` at both
      points and use the checked preimage-independence lemma for `extendF`
      (`extendF_preimage_eq` in the current BHW core support) with `hF_cinv`.
      This is the same proof shape as the existing restricted-Lorentz theorem
      `extendF_complex_lorentz_invariant`, but using the already-complex
      invariance hypothesis directly.

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (_hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ΓêÇ (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) = F z)
          (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz : z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          BHW.extendF F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) =
            BHW.extendF F z := by
        classical
        rcases Set.mem_iUnion.mp hz with Γƒ¿╬¢0, w0, hw0, hz_eqΓƒ⌐
        have hex_z :
            Γêâ (w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              w Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n Γêº
                Γêâ ╬ô : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
                  z = BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ô w :=
          Γƒ¿w0, hw0, ╬¢0, hz_eqΓƒ⌐
        have hex_Lz :
            Γêâ (w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              w Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n Γêº
                Γêâ ╬ô : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
                  BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z =
                    BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ô w := by
          refine Γƒ¿w0, hw0, ╬¢ * ╬¢0, ?_Γƒ⌐
          rw [hz_eq, BHW.complexLorentzAction_mul]
        simp only [BHW.extendF]
        rw [dif_pos hex_Lz, dif_pos hex_z]
        rcases hex_Lz.choose_spec with Γƒ¿hwL, ╬ôL, h╬ôLΓƒ⌐
        rcases hex_z.choose_spec with Γƒ¿hwZ, ╬ôZ, h╬ôZΓƒ⌐
        exact
          BHW.extendF_preimage_eq (d := d) n F hF_cinv
            hwL hwZ
            (╬¢Γéü := ╬ôL) (╬¢Γéé := ╬¢ * ╬ôZ)
            (by
              calc
                BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ôL hex_Lz.choose
                    = BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z := h╬ôL.symm
                _ =
                  BHW.complexLorentzAction (╬¢ * ╬ôZ) hex_z.choose := by
                    rw [h╬ôZ, BHW.complexLorentzAction_mul])
      ```

      The hypotheses `hd` and `_hF_holo` are present because the theorem is
      consumed beside `extendF_holomorphicOn` in the Hall-Wightman branch-law
      proof; the invariance calculation itself only needs `hF_cinv`,
      `ExtendedTube` as the complex Lorentz orbit of `ForwardTube`, and
      `extendF_preimage_eq`.

      Lean-shaped proof of the branch law after these Lemma-2 subfacts:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singularLimit_extendF_eq ... := by
        have hExt_holo :
            DifferentiableOn Γäé (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
          BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n F hF_holo hF_cinv
        have hExt_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
          hExt_holo.continuousOn
        have hleft_base :
            Tendsto (fun t => BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_left t))
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          hExt_cont.tendsto hsingular.curve_left_tendsto_base
            (eventually_of_forall hsingular.curve_left_mem)
            hsingular.base_mem
        have hright_base :
            Tendsto (fun t => BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_right t))
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          hExt_cont.tendsto hsingular.curve_right_tendsto_base
            (eventually_of_forall hsingular.curve_right_mem)
            hsingular.base_mem
        have hleft_value :
            ΓêÇ t,
              BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_left t) =
                BHW.extendF F z := by
          intro t
          rcases hsingular.curve_left_orbit t with Γƒ¿╬¢, h╬¢Γƒ⌐
          calc
            BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_left t)
                = BHW.extendF F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) := by
                    rw [h╬¢]
            _ = BHW.extendF F z := by
                    exact BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
                      (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv ╬¢ hz
        have hright_value :
            ΓêÇ t,
              BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_right t) =
                BHW.extendF F w := by
          intro t
          rcases hsingular.curve_right_orbit t with Γƒ¿╬¢, h╬¢Γƒ⌐
          calc
            BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_right t)
                = BHW.extendF F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ w) := by
                    rw [h╬¢]
            _ = BHW.extendF F w := by
                    exact BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
                      (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv ╬¢ hw
        have hz_base :
            BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F hsingular.base :=
          tendsto_nhds_unique tendsto_const_nhds
            (hleft_base.congr' (eventually_of_forall hleft_value))
        have hw_base :
            BHW.extendF F w = BHW.extendF F hsingular.base :=
          tendsto_nhds_unique tendsto_const_nhds
            (hright_base.congr' (eventually_of_forall hright_value))
        exact hz_base.trans hw_base.symm

      theorem BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq ... := by
        classical
        rcases BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_fiberAlternative
            (d := d) hd (n := n) hz hw hgram with horbit | hsingular
        ┬╖ rcases horbit with Γƒ¿╬¢, hw_eqΓƒ⌐
          calc
            BHW.extendF F z
                = BHW.extendF F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) := by
                    exact (BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
                      (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv ╬¢ hz).symm
            _ = BHW.extendF F w := by simpa [hw_eq]
        ┬╖ exact BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singularLimit_extendF_eq
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hz hw hgram hsingular
      ```

      The singular-limit proof itself is not optional.  Its transcript is:
      put the two equal-Gram configurations into Hall-Wightman Lemma-2 normal
      form; obtain the finite totally isotropic residual frame `q`, the two
      coefficient families `aLeft` and `aRight`, a common base `╬╛`, and a
      one-parameter complex Lorentz family `A ╬╕` with `A ╬╕ q_c -> 0` for every
      residual-frame vector while `A ╬╕` fixes `╬╛`.  Build the two extended-tube
      curves in the Lorentz orbits of `z` and `w`, both tending to `╬╛`; use
      `BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv` to show each curve has the
      same value as its endpoint; pass both limits to the common base using
      continuity from `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn`.  This is the low-rank
      continuity step in Hall-Wightman, not a new theorem route.

      The scalar-domain geometry is split similarly:

      ```lean
      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 / Section 2: every scalar point in `S'_n`
      has a relatively open scalar neighborhood inside the Gram image of the
      ordinary extended tube. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_at
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé}
          (hZ : Z Γêê BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :
          Γêâ O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen O Γêº Z Γêê O Γêº
            O Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n

      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_subset_sourceComplexGramVariety
          [NeZero d] (n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Γèå
            BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n

      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)

      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_connected
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat) :
          IsConnected (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_subset_sourceComplexGramVariety
          [NeZero d] (n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Γèå
            BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
        intro Z hZ
        rcases hZ with Γƒ¿z, hzET, rflΓƒ⌐
        exact Γƒ¿z, rflΓƒ⌐

      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_connected ... := by
        simpa [BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain] using
          (BHW.isConnected_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)).image
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n)
            (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous.continuousOn

      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected ... := by
        exact Γƒ¿
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen (d := d) hd n,
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_connected (d := d) hd nΓƒ⌐
      ```

      The subset proof uses only the repo definitions:
      `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n` is the image of `ExtendedTube d n`
      under `sourceMinkowskiGram d n`, while
      `sourceComplexGramVariety d n` is the full range of that same Gram map.
      The witness `hzET` is deliberately unused; extended-tube membership is
      needed for scalar-domain membership, not for variety membership.  This
      is a definitional support lemma, not Hall-Wightman content.

      The proof of `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen` is the
      Hall-Wightman neighborhood theorem: for
      `Z = sourceMinkowskiGram d n z`, Lemma 3 gives an ambient scalar
      neighborhood `O` of `Z` such that
      `O Γê⌐ sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå sourceMinkowskiGram d n ''
      ExtendedTube d n`; taking the union of these `O` over `Z Γêê S'_n`
      gives the relative-open witness.  This is where singular scalar-rank
      fibers are handled; no openness of a continuous image is used.

      Lean-facing local realization theorem:

      ```lean
      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 3, source-coordinate form.  If `z0` is an
      ordinary extended-tube realization of a scalar point and `Vsrc` is any
      vector neighborhood of `z0` inside the ordinary extended tube, then a
      scalar neighborhood of the Gram point inside the source Gram variety is
      realized by vectors in `Vsrc`. -/
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {Vsrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hVsrc_open : IsOpen Vsrc)
          (hz0Vsrc : z0 Γêê Vsrc)
          (hVsrc_sub : Vsrc Γèå BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          Γêâ O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen O Γêº
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Γêê O Γêº
            O Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Vsrc

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization_orbitRank
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z0)
          {Vsrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hVsrc_open : IsOpen Vsrc)
          (hz0Vsrc : z0 Γêê Vsrc)
          (hVsrc_sub : Vsrc Γèå BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          Γêâ O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen O Γêº
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Γêê O Γêº
            O Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Vsrc

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization_lowRank
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt d n z0)
          {Vsrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hVsrc_open : IsOpen Vsrc)
          (hz0Vsrc : z0 Γêê Vsrc)
          (hVsrc_sub : Vsrc Γèå BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          Γêâ O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen O Γêº
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Γêê O Γêº
            O Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Vsrc

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization ... := by
        by_cases hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z0
        ┬╖ exact BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization_orbitRank
            (d := d) hd n hz0 hRank hVsrc_open hz0Vsrc hVsrc_sub
        ┬╖ exact BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization_lowRank
            (d := d) hd n hz0
            (by
              simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt,
                BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt] using
                ((BHW.hwSourceGram_lowRank_iff_not_orbitRank
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd).2 hRank))
            hVsrc_open hz0Vsrc hVsrc_sub
      ```

      The exported local theorem is obtained from the quantitative
      small-perturbation theorem by a finite-dimensional open-ball shrink:

      ```lean
      /-- Finite-product topology helper: every open neighborhood of a source
      configuration contains a coordinate sup-norm perturbation box.  The
      output is phrased for additive perturbations because that is the exact
      shape returned by Hall-Wightman's quantitative Lemma 3. -/
      theorem BHW.exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen
          {d n : Nat}
          {x0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hx0U : x0 Γêê U) :
          Γêâ ╬╡ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬╡ Γêº
            ΓêÇ v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
              (ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûv i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡) ->
              (fun i ╬╝ => x0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) Γêê U

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_smallPerturbation_to_localVectorRealization
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {Vsrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hVsrc_open : IsOpen Vsrc)
          (hz0Vsrc : z0 Γêê Vsrc)
          (hVsrc_sub : Vsrc Γèå BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          Γêâ O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            IsOpen O Γêº
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Γêê O Γêº
            O Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Vsrc
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_smallPerturbation_to_localVectorRealization
          ... := by
        rcases BHW.exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen
            (x0 := z0) hVsrc_open hz0Vsrc with
          Γƒ¿╬╡, h╬╡_pos, hball_subΓƒ⌐
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization_smallPerturbation
            (d := d) hd n hz0 h╬╡_pos with
          Γƒ¿O, hO_open, hZO, hO_realizeΓƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿O, hO_open, hZO, ?_Γƒ⌐
        intro Z hZ
        rcases hO_realize Z hZ with Γƒ¿v, hv_small, hET, hGramΓƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝, ?_, hGramΓƒ⌐
        exact hball_sub hv_small
      ```

      Here `exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen` is only the standard
      finite-product metric fact that an open neighborhood of `z0` contains a
      coordinate sup-norm ball around `z0`.  It is the only topology used to
      pass from Hall-Wightman's quantitative `╬╡` statement to an arbitrary
      source neighborhood `Vsrc`.  Lean proof: use `Metric.mem_nhds_iff` to
      choose an ordinary norm ball in the Pi space, then use finite
      norm-equivalence (or the existing Pi sup-norm lemma, if available in
      the local imports) to shrink the coordinate bound so that
      `ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûv i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡` implies
      `ΓÇû(fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) - z0ΓÇû < ╧ü`.

      The quantitative theorem
      `BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization_smallPerturbation` is
      the real Hall-Wightman Lemma-3 content.  Its implementation must be
      decomposed into the following finite-dimensional sublemmas, with
      `D := d + 1`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedBlock_sqrt_near_identity
          (r : Nat)
          {╬╡ : Γä¥} (h╬╡ : 0 < ╬╡) :
          Γêâ ╬┤ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬┤ Γêº
            ΓêÇ B : Fin r -> Fin r -> Γäé,
              (ΓêÇ i j, B i j = B j i) ->
              (ΓêÇ i j, ΓÇûB i jΓÇû < ╬┤) ->
              Γêâ P : Fin r -> Fin r -> Γäé,
                (ΓêÇ i j, P i j = P j i) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ i j,
                  ΓÇûP i j - (if i = j then (1 : Γäé) else 0)ΓÇû < ╬╡) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ i j,
                  Γêæ k : Fin r, P i k * P j k =
                    (if i = j then (1 : Γäé) else 0) + B i j)

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_schurComplement_rank_bound
          (D r m : Nat)
          {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Γäé}
          {B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin r) Γäé}
          {C : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Γäé}
          (hA_inv : IsUnit A.det)
          (hRank :
            Matrix.rank
              (Matrix.fromBlocks A B.transpose B C) <= D) :
          Matrix.rank (C - B * AΓü╗┬╣ * B.transpose) <= D - r

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (m k : Nat)
          {╬╡ : Γä¥} (h╬╡ : 0 < ╬╡) :
          Γêâ ╬┤ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬┤ Γêº
            ΓêÇ S : Fin m -> Fin m -> Γäé,
              (ΓêÇ i j, S i j = S j i) ->
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.of fun i j => S i j) <= k ->
              k <= d + 1 ->
              (ΓêÇ i j, ΓÇûS i jΓÇû < ╬┤) ->
              Γêâ q : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
                (ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûq i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡) Γêº
                ΓêÇ i j,
                  BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q i) (q j) = S i j

      /-- Finite-dimensional Takagi/Bargmann factorization in the standard
      complex symmetric form, with the smallness estimate needed in
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 3.  This is pure matrix linear algebra: if a complex
      symmetric matrix has rank at most `k` and all entries are sufficiently
      small, then it is `A * Aß╡Ç` with all entries of `A` small. -/
      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_takagi_rankLE
          (m k : Nat)
          {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Γäé}
          (hSym : S.transpose = S)
          (hRank : Matrix.rank S <= k) :
          Γêâ A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Γäé,
            S = A * A.transpose

      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE
          (m k : Nat)
          {╬╡ : Γä¥} (h╬╡ : 0 < ╬╡) :
          Γêâ ╬┤ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬┤ Γêº
            ΓêÇ S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Γäé,
              S.transpose = S ->
              Matrix.rank S <= k ->
              (ΓêÇ i j, ΓÇûS i jΓÇû < ╬┤) ->
              Γêâ A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Γäé,
                (ΓêÇ i a, ΓÇûA i aΓÇû < ╬╡) Γêº
                S = A * A.transpose

      /-- The tail subspace in the diagonalized complex Minkowski model:
      vectors whose first `r` standard orthogonal coordinates vanish.  This is
      the orthogonal complement used after the selected Hall-Wightman block
      has been normalized to the first `r` standard coordinates. -/
      def BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace
          (d r : Nat) :
          Submodule Γäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)

      theorem BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace_orthogonal
          (d r : Nat) {v w : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hv : v Γêê BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace d r)
          (hw :
            ΓêÇ ╬╝, r <= ╬╝.val ->
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel d w) ╬╝ = 0) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d v w = 0

      theorem BHW.exists_finTailEmbedding
          (D r k : Nat)
          (hkr : k <= D - r) :
          Γêâ ╬╣ : Fin k -> Fin D,
            Function.Injective ╬╣ Γêº
            ΓêÇ a, r <= (╬╣ a).val

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel_symm_coord_bound
          (d : Nat) :
          Γêâ C : Γä¥, 0 < C Γêº
            ΓêÇ v : Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
              (ΓêÇ ╬╝, ΓÇû(BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel d).symm v ╬╝ΓÇû <=
                C * Γêæ ╬╜ : Fin (d + 1), ΓÇûv ╬╜ΓÇû)

      /-- Tail version of the residual realization theorem.  This is the form
      consumed by Lemma 3 after Schur complement reduction: the residual
      vectors are small and lie in the orthogonal complement of the selected
      normalized block, so adding them changes only the residual Schur
      complement scalar products. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (r m k : Nat)
          (hr : r <= d + 1)
          {╬╡ : Γä¥} (h╬╡ : 0 < ╬╡) :
          Γêâ ╬┤ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬┤ Γêº
            ΓêÇ S : Fin m -> Fin m -> Γäé,
              (ΓêÇ i j, S i j = S j i) ->
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.of fun i j => S i j) <= k ->
              k <= d + 1 - r ->
              (ΓêÇ i j, ΓÇûS i jΓÇû < ╬┤) ->
              Γêâ q : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
                (ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûq i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ i, q i Γêê
                  BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace d r) Γêº
                ΓêÇ i j,
                  BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q i) (q j) = S i j

      def BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource
          (d n r : Nat) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé

      def BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram
          (n r : Nat) :
          Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé

      theorem BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_hwLemma3CanonicalSource
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n) (hrD : r <= d + 1) :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
            (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r) =
          BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram n r

      /-- Finite-dimensional normal-form transport used in Hall-Wightman
      Lemma 3.  It records the source-index linear change and ambient
      Minkowski isometry that carry the chosen base tuple to the canonical
      `r`-block tuple, together with the corresponding congruence map on
      source Gram matrices and the smallness estimates needed to transport
      perturbations back. -/
      structure BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport
          [NeZero d]
          (d n r : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) where
        toNormalVec :
          (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) ΓëâΓéù[Γäé]
            (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
        toNormalGram :
          (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) ΓëâΓéù[Γäé] (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)
        z0_to_canonical :
          toNormalVec z0 = BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r
        gram_commute :
          ΓêÇ z,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (toNormalVec z) =
              toNormalGram (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        gram_variety_iff :
          ΓêÇ Z,
            toNormalGram Z Γêê BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γåö
              Z Γêê BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n
        gram_z0 :
          toNormalGram (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) =
            BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram n r
        gram_ball_to_normal_ball :
          ΓêÇ {╬╖N : Γä¥}, 0 < ╬╖N ->
            Γêâ ╬╖ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬╖ Γêº
              ΓêÇ Z,
                (ΓêÇ i j,
                  ΓÇûZ i j - BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 i jΓÇû < ╬╖) ->
                (ΓêÇ i j,
                  ΓÇûtoNormalGram Z i j -
                    BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram n r i jΓÇû < ╬╖N)
        perturb_back_small :
          ΓêÇ {╬╡ : Γä¥}, 0 < ╬╡ ->
            Γêâ ╬╡N : Γä¥, 0 < ╬╡N Γêº
              ΓêÇ vN : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
                (ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûvN i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡N) ->
                Γêâ v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
                  (ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûv i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡) Γêº
                  toNormalVec (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) =
                    fun i ╬╝ =>
                      BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r i ╬╝ + vN i ╬╝

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormTransportData
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (r : Nat)
          (hr :
            r = BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0)) :
          Γêâ T : BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r z0, True

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalizedSchurSurjective
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n r : Nat)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (hrD : r <= d + 1)
          {╬╡ : Γä¥} (h╬╡ : 0 < ╬╡) :
          Γêâ ╬╖ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬╖ Γêº
            ΓêÇ Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé,
              Z Γêê BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              (ΓêÇ i j, ΓÇûZ i j - BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram n r i jΓÇû < ╬╖) ->
              Γêâ v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
                (ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûv i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡) Γêº
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r + v) = Z

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_transport_from_normalForm
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          {╬╡ : Γä¥} (h╬╡ : 0 < ╬╡) :
          Γêâ ╬╖ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬╖ Γêº
            ΓêÇ Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé,
              Z Γêê BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              (ΓêÇ i j,
                ΓÇûZ i j - BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 i jΓÇû < ╬╖) ->
              Γêâ v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
                (ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûv i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡) Γêº
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) = Z

      /-- Finite source-Gram coordinate ball used to turn the quantitative
      Lemma-3 estimate into an open scalar neighborhood. -/
      def BHW.sourceGramCoordBall
          (n : Nat)
          (G0 : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)
          (╬╖ : Γä¥) : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) :=
        {Z | ΓêÇ i j, ΓÇûZ i j - G0 i jΓÇû < ╬╖}

      theorem BHW.isOpen_sourceGramCoordBall
          (n : Nat)
          (G0 : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)
          {╬╖ : Γä¥} (h╬╖ : 0 < ╬╖) :
          IsOpen (BHW.sourceGramCoordBall n G0 ╬╖)

      /-- The final quantitative Lemma-3 bridge: choose the perturbation
      radius small enough that the vector perturbation produced by the
      normal-form realization remains in the ordinary extended tube. -/
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {╬╡ : Γä¥} (h╬╡ : 0 < ╬╡) :
          Γêâ ╬╖ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬╖ Γêº
            ΓêÇ Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé,
              Z Γêê BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              Z Γêê BHW.sourceGramCoordBall n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ╬╖ ->
              Γêâ v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
                (ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûv i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡) Γêº
                (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) Γêê
                  BHW.ExtendedTube d n Γêº
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) = Z
      ```

      Proof transcript for the residual small-realization theorem:

      1. Prove `complexSymmetric_takagi_rankLE` by the standard
         Autonne-Takagi/Bargmann factorization for complex symmetric matrices:
         choose unitary `U` and nonnegative singular values `╧â a` with
         `S = U * diagonal ╧â * U.transpose`; discard zero singular values and
         pad to `Fin k` using the rank hypothesis; set
         `A i a = U i a * Real.sqrt (╧â a)`.
      2. Prove `complexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE` from the same
         factorization with estimates.  Since
         `ΓÇûSΓÇûop <= m * sup_{i,j} ΓÇûS i jΓÇû`, choosing
         `╬┤ < ╬╡^2 / max 1 m` gives `ΓÇûA i aΓÇû < ╬╡`.  This step is finite
         matrix linear algebra only; it has no BHW, OS, or locality content.
      3. Define `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace d r` by transporting
         the coordinate tail `{u | ΓêÇ ╬╝, ╬╝.val < r -> u ╬╝ = 0}` through
         `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel d`.  Orthogonality to the selected
         block is then the ordinary standard-bilinear calculation
         `Γêæ ╬╝, u ╬╝ * w ╬╝ = 0`, because the supports are disjoint, transported
         back by `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel_preserves_bilinear`.
      4. For
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail`, first
         apply `complexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE` with
         `k <= d + 1 - r`.  Embed the resulting `Fin k` columns into the tail
         coordinates using `exists_finTailEmbedding`; all other diagonalized
         coordinates are zero.  Transport the vectors back by
         `(complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel d).symm`.  Finite-dimensional norm
         equivalence for this fixed linear equivalence is exposed as
         `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalModel_symm_coord_bound`, so the Takagi
         factorization is requested with `╬╡ / C`; after transport every
         ambient coordinate is `< ╬╡`.  The support proof is exactly the
         inequality `r <= (╬╣ a).val` from `exists_finTailEmbedding`.
      5. The older theorem
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE` is the special case
         `r = 0` of the tail theorem.  The normalized Schur-surjectivity proof
         must use the tail theorem with `k = d + 1 - r`; otherwise it would
         realize the residual matrix in directions that might not be
         orthogonal to the selected block.

      Normalized Schur-surjectivity is the block construction in
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 3.  After fixing `hrn : r <= n`, use the order
      equivalence
      `Fin n Γëâ Fin r Γèò Fin (n - r)` to write a nearby scalar matrix as
      `Z = [[A, Bß╡Ç], [B, C]]`, where the canonical base is
      `[[1, 0], [0, 0]]`.  The theorem is implemented by the following
      proof-local block lemma, not by an unspecified inverse-function
      theorem:

      ```lean
      def BHW.finSourceHead {n r : Nat} (hrn : r <= n) :
          Fin r -> Fin n :=
        fun a => Γƒ¿a.val, lt_of_lt_of_le a.isLt hrnΓƒ⌐

      def BHW.finSourceTail {n r : Nat} (hrn : r <= n) :
          Fin (n - r) -> Fin n :=
        fun u => Γƒ¿r + u.val, by omegaΓƒ⌐

      theorem BHW.finSourceHead_tail_cases
          {n r : Nat} (hrn : r <= n)
          (i : Fin n) :
          (Γêâ a : Fin r, i = BHW.finSourceHead hrn a) Γê¿
          (Γêâ u : Fin (n - r), i = BHW.finSourceTail hrn u)

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalizedSchurBlockRealization
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n r : Nat)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (hrD : r <= d + 1)
          {╬╡ : Γä¥} (h╬╡ : 0 < ╬╡) :
          Γêâ ╬╖ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬╖ Γêº
            ΓêÇ Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé,
              Z Γêê BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              (ΓêÇ i j, ΓÇûZ i j - BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram n r i jΓÇû < ╬╖) ->
              Γêâ (P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Γäé)
                (L : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Γäé)
                (q : Fin (n - r) -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
                (v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
                  (ΓêÇ a b, P a b = P b a) Γêº
                  (ΓêÇ a b,
                    Γêæ c : Fin r, P a c * P b c =
                      Z (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)
                        (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b)) Γêº
                  (ΓêÇ u a,
                    Γêæ b : Fin r, L u b *
                      Z (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b)
                        (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a) =
                      Z (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u)
                        (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)) Γêº
                  (ΓêÇ u v',
                    BHW.complexMinkowskiBilinear d (q u) (q v') =
                      Z (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u)
                        (BHW.finSourceTail hrn v') -
                      Γêæ a : Fin r, Γêæ b : Fin r,
                        L u a *
                          Z (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)
                            (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b) *
                          L v' b) Γêº
                  (ΓêÇ u, q u Γêê
                    BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace d r) Γêº
                  (ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûv i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡) Γêº
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                    (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r + v) = Z
      ```

      Here `BHW.finSourceHead hrn : Fin r -> Fin n` and
      `BHW.finSourceTail hrn : Fin (n - r) -> Fin n` are the standard finite
      order embeddings selecting the first `r` and last `n-r` source
      indices.  They are proof-local indexing helpers; their only content is
      the equivalence `Fin n Γëâ Fin r Γèò Fin (n-r)`.

      Proof transcript for `hwLemma3_normalizedSchurBlockRealization`:

      1. Let `A` be the selected block of `Z` and write
         `A = 1 + B1`.  Since `Z` is `╬╖`-close to the canonical Gram matrix,
         `B1` is small and symmetric.  Apply
         `hwLemma3_selectedBlock_sqrt_near_identity` to obtain a symmetric
         `P` close to `1` with `P * Pß╡Ç = A`.  The first `r` source vectors
         are `x_a := Γêæ b, P a b ΓÇó e_b`, where `e_b` are the first `r`
         standard orthogonal coordinates in the diagonalized complex
         Minkowski model.
      2. Because `A` is close to `1`, shrink `╬╖` so `A.det` is a unit and
         `AΓü╗┬╣` is close to `1`.  Set
         `L u b := Γêæ a, Z (tail u) (head a) * (AΓü╗┬╣) a b`.  Then
         `Γêæ b, L u b * A b a = Z (tail u) (head a)`, so
         `p_u := Γêæ b, L u b ΓÇó x_b` has the required selected cross
         scalar products.
      3. Define the residual Schur complement
         `S u v := Z (tail u) (tail v) -
           Γêæ a b, L u a * A a b * L v b`.  By
         `hwLemma3_schurComplement_rank_bound`, using
         `Z Γêê sourceComplexGramVariety d n` and
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le`, `rank S <= d + 1 - r`.
         Smallness of `S` follows from smallness of `Z - Gcan`,
         `P - 1`, `AΓü╗┬╣ - 1`, and `L`.
      4. Apply
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail` to
         realize `S` by small `q_u` in the orthogonal tail.  Tail membership
         gives `<q_u, x_a> = 0`, while the theorem gives
         `<q_u, q_v> = S u v`.
      5. For a tail source index set
         `y_u := p_u + q_u`; for a head index use `x_a`.  The perturbation
         `v` is `x_a - e_a` on the head and `y_u` on the tail.  The three
         displayed identities prove the selected-selected,
         tail-selected, and tail-tail entries of
         `sourceMinkowskiGram d n (hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r + v) = Z`.
         Shrinking `╬╖` one final time makes all coordinates of `v` smaller
         than `╬╡`.

      Lean-shaped proof of normalized Schur-surjectivity:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalizedSchurSurjective ... := by
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_normalizedSchurBlockRealization
            (d := d) hd n r hrn hrD h╬╡ with
          Γƒ¿╬╖, h╬╖_pos, hblockΓƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿╬╖, h╬╖_pos, ?_Γƒ⌐
        intro Z hZvar hZsmall
        rcases hblock Z hZvar hZsmall with
          Γƒ¿P, L, q, v, hP_symm, hP_block, hL_cross,
            hq_schur, hq_tail, hv_small, hgramΓƒ⌐
        exact Γƒ¿v, hv_small, hgramΓƒ⌐
      ```

      Proof transcript for the quantitative theorem:

      1. Let `G0 := sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0` and
         `r := sourceGramMatrixRank n G0`.  By finite matrix rank bounds and
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le`, `r <= n` and `r <= D`.
      2. Choose a nonzero principal `r ├ù r` minor using the checked
         `exists_sourcePrincipalMinor_ne_zero_of_sourceSymmetricRank`; after
         a finite permutation of labels, treat this block as the upper-left
         block.  The `r = 0` case is the same argument with the selected block
         empty and the whole nearby matrix handled by the residual realization
         theorem.
      3. Use the selected block to put the base configuration in
         Hall-Wightman normal form: the first `r` source vectors have Gram
         matrix `1`, the remaining source vectors have selected coordinates
         removed, and the base Gram becomes
         `hwLemma3CanonicalGram n r`.  The transformations used here are
         invertible finite-dimensional linear maps on source coordinates and
         congruences on symmetric Gram matrices, so they send neighborhoods to
         neighborhoods and preserve the rank-bounded source variety.
      4. For a nearby scalar matrix `Z = G0 + E`, write the normalized block
         decomposition as `[[1 + B1, Bß╡Ç], [B, C]]`.  For `E` small,
         `1 + B1` is invertible and its analytic square root is supplied by
         `hwLemma3_selectedBlock_sqrt_near_identity`; this chooses small
         perturbations of the first `r` source vectors.
      5. Orthogonalize the remaining perturbations against the first `r`
         perturbed vectors exactly as in Hall-Wightman's equation (46).  The
         coefficients are `B * (1 + B1)Γü╗┬╣`, and the inverse is close to `1`;
         hence this change and its inverse preserve the chosen smallness
         bounds after shrinking `╬╖`.
      6. The lower-right residual is the Schur complement
         `S := C - B * (1 + B1)Γü╗┬╣ * Bß╡Ç`.  Because the full block matrix has
         rank at most `D` and the selected block has rank `r`,
         `hwLemma3_schurComplement_rank_bound` gives `rank S <= D - r`.
      7. Realize this small symmetric residual by small vectors in the
         normalized orthogonal tail using
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail`.
         This is the dimension-general form of Hall-Wightman's estimate (48),
         with `4 - r` replaced by `D - r`.  The tail membership is
         load-bearing: it ensures that the residual vectors do not change the
         selected block or the selected/residual cross scalar products after
         the orthogonalization step.
      8. Add the selected-block perturbations and residual vectors, undo the
         orthogonalization and the initial normal-form transport, and shrink
         `╬╖` one final time so every coordinate of the resulting perturbation
         is `< ╬╡`.

      The normal-form transport in steps 2--3 is exposed as
      `BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport`, not hidden in prose.  Its proof is
      finite-dimensional: choose the nonzero principal `r ├ù r` minor, apply an
      invertible source-index change so the selected block is first, apply the
      symmetric square-root normalization from
      `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedBlock_sqrt_near_identity` at the base block, and
      use Witt extension to carry the normalized selected source vectors to
      the first `r` standard orthogonal coordinates.  The induced congruence
      on Gram matrices is `toNormalGram`; `gram_commute` is the direct
      bilinear calculation, and `gram_variety_iff` follows because both the
      source-index change and the ambient Lorentz isometry are invertible.
      The two estimate fields are finite-dimensional continuity/norm
      equivalence for the fixed linear equivalences.

      Lean-shaped transport proof of
      `BHW.hwLemma3_transport_from_normalForm`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_transport_from_normalForm ... := by
        let G0 := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0
        let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G0
        have hr : r = BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G0 := rfl
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormTransportData
            (d := d) hd n (z0 := z0) r hr with
          Γƒ¿T, _Γƒ⌐
        have hr_le_n : r <= n := by
          exact BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_le_arity
            (n := n) G0
        have hr_le_D : r <= d + 1 := by
          -- From `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le` applied to `G0`.
          exact BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_le_spacetime_of_sourceGram
            (d := d) (n := n) z0
        rcases T.perturb_back_small h╬╡ with
          Γƒ¿╬╡N, h╬╡N_pos, hbackΓƒ⌐
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_normalizedSchurSurjective
            (d := d) hd n r hr_le_n hr_le_D h╬╡N_pos with
          Γƒ¿╬╖N, h╬╖N_pos, hnormΓƒ⌐
        rcases T.gram_ball_to_normal_ball h╬╖N_pos with
          Γƒ¿╬╖, h╬╖_pos, h╬╖_to_normalΓƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿╬╖, h╬╖_pos, ?_Γƒ⌐
        intro Z hZvar hZsmall
        have hZnorm_var :
            T.toNormalGram Z Γêê BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
          (T.gram_variety_iff Z).2 hZvar
        have hZnorm_small :
            ΓêÇ i j,
              ΓÇûT.toNormalGram Z i j -
                BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram n r i jΓÇû < ╬╖N :=
          h╬╖_to_normal Z hZsmall
        rcases hnorm (T.toNormalGram Z) hZnorm_var hZnorm_small with
          Γƒ¿vN, hvN_small, hgramNΓƒ⌐
        rcases hback vN hvN_small with Γƒ¿v, hv_small, hvec_backΓƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿v, hv_small, ?_Γƒ⌐
        apply T.toNormalGram.injective
        calc
          T.toNormalGram
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝))
              =
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
              (T.toNormalVec (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝)) := by
                rw [T.gram_commute]
                rfl
          _ =
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
              (fun i ╬╝ =>
                BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r i ╬╝ + vN i ╬╝) := by
                rw [hvec_back]
          _ = T.toNormalGram Z := hgramN.symm
      ```

      The public small-perturbation Lemma-3 theorem has one additional
      topological shrink that must be explicit in Lean.  Since
      `BHW.ExtendedTube d n` is open and `hz0 : z0 Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n`,
      `BHW.exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen` gives an
      `╬╡ET > 0` such that every coordinate perturbation smaller than `╬╡ET`
      stays inside the extended tube.  Apply
      `BHW.hwLemma3_transport_from_normalForm` with
      `╬╡' := min ╬╡ ╬╡ET / 2`, then use
      `BHW.sourceGramCoordBall` as the scalar neighborhood.  This is the only
      place where extended-tube membership is recovered from the quantitative
      normal-form algebra.

      Lean-shaped final quantitative assembly:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube ... := by
        rcases BHW.exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen
            (x0 := z0)
            (U := BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
            (BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)) hz0 with
          Γƒ¿╬╡ET, h╬╡ET_pos, hET_ballΓƒ⌐
        let ╬╡' : Γä¥ := min ╬╡ ╬╡ET / 2
        have h╬╡'_pos : 0 < ╬╡' := by
          positivity
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_transport_from_normalForm
            (d := d) hd n (z0 := z0) h╬╡'_pos with
          Γƒ¿╬╖, h╬╖_pos, hrealizeΓƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿╬╖, h╬╖_pos, ?_Γƒ⌐
        intro Z hZvar hZball
        rcases hrealize Z hZvar hZball with Γƒ¿v, hv_small, hgramΓƒ⌐
        have hv_target : ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûv i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡ := by
          intro i ╬╝
          exact lt_of_lt_of_le (hv_small i ╬╝) (by
            dsimp [╬╡']; nlinarith [min_le_left ╬╡ ╬╡ET])
        have hv_ET : ΓêÇ i ╬╝, ΓÇûv i ╬╝ΓÇû < ╬╡ET := by
          intro i ╬╝
          exact lt_of_lt_of_le (hv_small i ╬╝) (by
            dsimp [╬╡']; nlinarith [min_le_right ╬╡ ╬╡ET])
        exact Γƒ¿v, hv_target, hET_ball v hv_ET, hgramΓƒ⌐

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization_smallPerturbation
          ... := by
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube
            (d := d) hd n hz0 h╬╡ with
          Γƒ¿╬╖, h╬╖_pos, hrealizeΓƒ⌐
        let O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) :=
          BHW.sourceGramCoordBall n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ╬╖
        refine Γƒ¿O, BHW.isOpen_sourceGramCoordBall n _ h╬╖_pos, ?_, ?_Γƒ⌐
        ┬╖ intro i j
          simp [O, BHW.sourceGramCoordBall]
          exact h╬╖_pos
        ┬╖ intro Z hZ
          exact hrealize Z hZ.2 hZ.1
      ```

      The orbit-rank and low-rank wrappers are only organizational views of
      this same Lemma-3 theorem, matching Hall-Wightman's discussion after
      Lemma 2.  The low-rank branch is the delicate Schur-complement/residual
      realization above; it is not a source-map regularity theorem.  Existing
      local rank-exact Schur charts may be used only inside this realization
      theorem after the ordinary extended-tube preimage has been produced;
      they cannot replace Lemma 3.

      The pointwise relative-open theorem is now mechanical:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_at ... := by
        rcases hZ with Γƒ¿z0, hz0, rflΓƒ⌐
        rcases mem_nhds_iff.mp
            ((BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)).mem_nhds hz0) with
          Γƒ¿Vsrc, hVsrc_sub, hVsrc_open, hz0VsrcΓƒ⌐
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
            (d := d) hd n hz0 hVsrc_open hz0Vsrc hVsrc_sub with
          Γƒ¿O, hO_open, hZO, hO_sub_imageΓƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿O, hO_open, hZO, ?_Γƒ⌐
        intro W hW
        rcases hO_sub_image hW with Γƒ¿z, hzVsrc, rflΓƒ⌐
        exact Γƒ¿z, hVsrc_sub hzVsrc, rflΓƒ⌐
      ```

      Lean-shaped relative-open assembly:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen ... := by
        classical
        choose O hO_open hZO hO_sub using
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_at (d := d) hd (n := n)
        refine Γƒ¿Γïâ Z : BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n, O Z.1 Z.2,
          isOpen_iUnion (fun Z => hO_open Z.1 Z.2), ?_Γƒ⌐
        ext W
        constructor
        ┬╖ intro hWdomain
          exact Γƒ¿Γƒ¿Γƒ¿W, hWdomainΓƒ⌐, hZO W hWdomainΓƒ⌐,
            BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_subset_sourceComplexGramVariety
              (d := d) (n := n) hWdomainΓƒ⌐
        ┬╖ rintro Γƒ¿hWU0, hWvarΓƒ⌐
          rcases Set.mem_iUnion.mp hWU0 with Γƒ¿Z, hWOΓƒ⌐
          exact hO_sub Z.1 Z.2 Γƒ¿hWO, hWvarΓƒ⌐
      ```

      Finally, the descent theorem must be sourced from the analytic part of
      Hall-Wightman Theorem I:

      ```lean
      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7 in the exact active form: the
      single-valued scalar function on `S'_n` becomes a holomorphic
      analytic-space germ on the source Gram variety.  The output is the germ
      predicate; no Stein-neighborhood or total ambient extension is used. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ΓêÇ (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ΓêÇ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé},
              z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w) :
          Γêâ Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
            BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ w, w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) = BHW.extendF F w)
      ```

      The descent theorem is itself split into the local Hall-Wightman chart
      theorem and the gluing/removable-singularity theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ΓêÇ (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ΓêÇ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé},
              z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé}
          (hZU : Z Γêê BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :
          Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
            IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
            DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
            U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Γêº
            (ΓêÇ w, w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Γêê U0 ->
                ╬¿ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w)

      /-- Optional proof-local compatible scalar-product germ atlas produced by
      Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7.  If used in Lean, this packet should be
      `private` or inlined inside
      `sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent`; it is not an additional
      public source theorem surface.  The `overlap_eq` field is equality of
      analytic germs on the scalar Gram variety.  It is deliberately not full
      ambient-open equality: arbitrary ambient representatives of the same
      analytic-space germ can differ by functions vanishing on the variety. -/
      structure BHW.HallWightmanScalarGermAtlas
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé) where
        U0 :
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) ->
            Z Γêê BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)
        ╬¿ :
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) ->
            Z Γêê BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé
        U0_open :
          ΓêÇ Z hZ, IsOpen (U0 Z hZ)
        center_mem :
          ΓêÇ Z hZ, Z Γêê U0 Z hZ
        ╬¿_diff :
          ΓêÇ Z hZ, DifferentiableOn Γäé (╬¿ Z hZ) (U0 Z hZ)
        U0_sub :
          ΓêÇ Z hZ,
            U0 Z hZ Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n
        branch_eq :
          ΓêÇ Z hZ w, w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Γêê U0 Z hZ ->
              ╬¿ Z hZ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                BHW.extendF F w
        overlap_eq :
          ΓêÇ Z hZ W hW,
            Set.EqOn (╬¿ Z hZ) (╬¿ W hW)
              ((U0 Z hZ Γê⌐ U0 W hW) Γê⌐
                BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Compatibility of two Hall-Wightman scalar charts is not an extra
      off-variety uniqueness theorem.  Once each chart's variety slice is
      contained in `S'_n` and each chart agrees with `extendF F` on every
      realized extended-tube point in that slice, equality on the overlap of
      the source Gram variety follows by choosing an ordinary extended-tube
      realization of the overlap scalar point. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_overlap_eq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          {U V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          {╬¿ ╬º : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          (hU_sub :
            U Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (hV_sub :
            V Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (h╬¿_branch :
            ΓêÇ w, w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Γêê U ->
                ╬¿ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w)
          (h╬º_branch :
            ΓêÇ w, w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Γêê V ->
                ╬º (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w) :
          Set.EqOn ╬¿ ╬º
            ((U Γê⌐ V) Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      Lean-shaped proof of the overlap helper:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_overlap_eq ... := by
        intro G hG
        have hG_Uvar : G Γêê U Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
          Γƒ¿hG.1.1, hG.2Γƒ⌐
        have hG_domain : G Γêê BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n :=
          hU_sub hG_Uvar
        rcases hG_domain with Γƒ¿w, hw, rflΓƒ⌐
        exact
          (h╬¿_branch w hw hG.1.1).trans
            (h╬º_branch w hw hG.1.2).symm
      ```

      /-- Optional proof-local sheaf-style gluing on the source Gram analytic
      subspace.  This formalizes choice-independence of compatible chart
      representatives, but the production route should not expose it as a
      second public descent theorem: the direct descent proof below uses the
      branch-defined `Phi` from
      `hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded` and proves the germ
      predicate for that same `Phi`. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_scalarGermAtlas_glue
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanScalarGermAtlas hd n F) :
          Γêâ phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé) -> Γäé,
            (ΓêÇ Z, Z Γêê BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
                IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
                DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
                Set.EqOn phi ╬¿
                  (U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) Γêº
                U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
                  BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ w, w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) = BHW.extendF F w)
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of `BHW.hallWightman_scalarGermAtlas_glue`: let
      `I := ╬ú Z, Z Γêê BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n`, set
      `U i := A.U0 i.1 i.2` and `╬¿i i := A.╬¿ i.1 i.2`, and let
      `╬⌐V := Γïâ i : I, U i Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n`.  Define
      `phi W := ╬¿i (Classical.choose hW) W` if `hW : W Γêê ╬⌐V`, and `0`
      otherwise.  If
      `W Γêê (U i Γê⌐ U j) Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n`, then
      `A.overlap_eq i.1 i.2 j.1 j.2` proves `╬¿i i W = ╬¿i j W`, so `phi` is
      choice-independent on the scalar variety and
      `Set.EqOn phi (╬¿i i) (U i Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)`.  The
      local fields `A.U0_open`, `A.center_mem`, `A.╬¿_diff`, and `A.U0_sub`
      give the analytic-space local representative clause.  The branch
      equality is `A.branch_eq`
      applied to the index
      `Γƒ¿BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w, Γƒ¿w, hw, rflΓƒ⌐Γƒ⌐`.

      `BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_at` is Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7 in
      local form.  It must not reuse the Lemma-2 orbit/low split.  The
      power-series chart is at non-exceptional scalar points, i.e. maximal
      possible scalar rank `min (d + 1) n`; the exceptional lower-rank stratum
      uses boundedness/continuity plus the removable-singularity theorem on
      the scalar-product variety.  The overlap
      equality for two local scalar charts is not an extra assumption: on any
      common scalar point realized by extended-tube configurations, `hBranch`
      equates the two values; analytic continuation on the connected
      relatively open chart overlap extends equality as a germ on the scalar
      Gram variety.  A private gluing theorem can define a scalar-space
      function by these compatible variety germs, but the active public
      descent theorem must use the already branch-defined `Phi` from
      `hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded`.  It must not
      introduce a second scalar value function and then silently identify it
      with the branch-law choice.

      The API-critical point is now separated cleanly.  Hall-Wightman gives
      equality of scalar-product germs on `S'_n`, i.e. equality on the source
      Gram variety.  That does not imply equality of arbitrary ambient
      extensions on full open overlaps.  Therefore the active production API
      must consume `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn`; keeping the old
      `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` field would require the archived
      Cartan/Oka bridge and an independent Stein-domain theorem.

      If the implementation uses the optional atlas as local proof
      organization, its construction is the following private Hall-Wightman
      chart-packet producer:

      ```lean
      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7 plus the scalar-product power-series
      overlap uniqueness on the scalar Gram analytic subspace. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.hallWightman_consistentScalarGermAtlas
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ΓêÇ (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ΓêÇ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé},
              z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w) :
          BHW.HallWightmanScalarGermAtlas hd n F
      ```

      Proof transcript for the private
      `BHW.hallWightman_consistentScalarGermAtlas`:

      1. For each `Z Γêê sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n`, split by
         `BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z`.  In the max-rank case, use
         `BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at`; in the exceptional
         case, use
         `BHW.hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at`, whose proof
         is the removable-singularity construction below.  These choices fill
         `U0`, `╬¿`, `U0_open`, `center_mem`, `╬¿_diff`, `U0_sub`, and
         `branch_eq`.
      2. Prove `overlap_eq` uniformly with
         `BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_overlap_eq`.  Given a scalar point
         in `(U0 Z hZ Γê⌐ U0 W hW) Γê⌐ sourceComplexGramVariety d n`, the first
         chart's `U0_sub` field realizes it as `sourceMinkowskiGram d n w`
         for some `w Γêê ExtendedTube d n`; the two `branch_eq` fields then
         identify both chart values with `extendF F w`.  This is equality on
         the source Gram variety only, exactly the germ-overlap field.
      3. If the private glue theorem is used, it is purely formal once
         variety-germ `overlap_eq` is a field of the atlas; it may not re-prove
         or assume equality of arbitrary ambient extensions.  The public
         descent theorem still returns the branch-defined `Phi`, not an
         unrelated glued choice.

      Lean-facing local-chart split:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_localScalarChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ΓêÇ (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ΓêÇ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé},
              z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé}
          (hZU : Z Γêê BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z) :
          Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
            IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
            DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
            U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Γêº
            (ΓêÇ w, w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Γêê U0 ->
                ╬¿ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w)

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ΓêÇ (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ΓêÇ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé},
              z Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé}
          (hZU : Z Γêê BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z) :
          Γêâ U0 ╬¿,
            IsOpen U0 Γêº Z Γêê U0 Γêº
            DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬¿ U0 Γêº
            U0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Γèå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Γêº
            (ΓêÇ w, w Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Γêê U0 ->
                ╬¿ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w)

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_at ... := by
        by_cases hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z
        ┬╖ exact BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_localScalarChart_at
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hZU hRank
        ┬╖ exact BHW.hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hZU
            ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
              (d := d) (n := n)).2 hRank)
      ```

      The max-rank chart is proved from the Hall-Wightman invariant
      power-series theorem near a non-exceptional scalar point.  The
      exceptional-rank chart is not a formal gluing wrapper: it is the
      theorem's removable singularity step, using boundedness/continuity from
      Lemmas 1--7 and the analytic structure of the exceptional locus in the
      scalar-product variety.  Existing repo rank-exact identity-principle
      lemmas may help with the final analytic continuation on relatively open
      scalar charts, but they do not replace the Hall-Wightman bounded
      extension at the exceptional locus.

      The proof of the two chart lemmas is itself part of the source route.
      It must be decomposed as follows; otherwise
      `hallWightman_localScalarChart_at` is still only a name for the missing
      Hall-Wightman theorem.

      The predicates `BHW.LocallyBoundedOn` and
      `BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn` are declared in the active
      standard-geometry block above, before they are consumed by
      `sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension`; the chart packet below only
      adds the continuity and PDE support lemmas used to prove those inputs.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.continuousOn_of_open_nhdsWithin_control
          {X Y : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] [TopologicalSpace Y]
          {f : X -> Y} {U : Set X}
          (h :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê U ->
              ΓêÇ T : Set Y, IsOpen T -> f x Γêê T ->
                Γêâ O : Set X, IsOpen O Γêº x Γêê O Γêº
                  ΓêÇ y, y Γêê O -> y Γêê U -> f y Γêê T) :
          ContinuousOn f U

      theorem BHW.continuousOn_openDomain_preimage_nhds
          {X Y : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] [TopologicalSpace Y]
          {f : X -> Y} {╬⌐ : Set X}
          (h╬⌐_open : IsOpen ╬⌐)
          (hf : ContinuousOn f ╬⌐)
          {x : X} (hx╬⌐ : x Γêê ╬⌐)
          {T : Set Y} (hT_open : IsOpen T)
          (hxT : f x Γêê T) :
          Γêâ V : Set X, IsOpen V Γêº x Γêê V Γêº
            ΓêÇ y, y Γêê ╬⌐ -> y Γêê V -> f y Γêê T

      /-- Lean-facing structure for the concrete finite linear PDE system in
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 4.  Its single field is the infinitesimal generator;
      the equations themselves are the vanishing conditions in
      `SatisfiesLorentzInvariantPDE`, indexed by these generators and obtained
      by differentiating the complex Lorentz action at the identity. -/
      structure BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator (d : Nat) where
        val : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Γäé
        skew :
          ΓêÇ ╬╝ ╬╜,
            (MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature d ╬╝ : Γäé) * val ╬╝ ╬╜ +
            (MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature d ╬╜ : Γäé) * val ╬╜ ╬╝ = 0

      def BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent
