      ```lean
        exact
          BHW.os45SPrime_permutedWickExtendF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart
            ╧å h╧å_comp h╧å_supp
      ```

      The checked geometric transport from raw adjacent Wick coordinates to
      the canonical Figure-2-4 lift is then:

      ```lean
      have hcanonical_to_raw :
          Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
              BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * ╧å x
            =
          Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
              BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (BHW.permAct (d := d) ╧ä
                  (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * ╧å x := by
        refine integral_congr_ae ?_
        filter_upwards with x
        by_cases hx : x Γêê hChart.V0
        ┬╖ have hnorm :
              BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
                =
              BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (BHW.permAct (d := d) ╧ä
                  (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) :=
            BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_extendF_eq_permutedWick_zero
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart x hx
          rw [hnorm]
        ┬╖ have h╧åx : ╧å x = 0 := by
            exact
              (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                (fun hxSupp => hx (h╧å_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
          simp [h╧åx]
      exact hcanonical_to_raw.trans hBHWJost_raw
      ```

      This downstream transport is the only allowed way to use the raw
      adjacent Wick section after the source equality has been proved.  In
      particular, the already implemented
      `BHW.extendF_eq_boundary_value` and
      `BHW.extendF_eq_boundary_value_ET` helpers are insufficient here:
      their conclusions concern real-embedded boundary configurations
      `BHW.realEmbed x`, while the adjacent Wick section
      `BHW.permAct ╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))` is a complex
      ordinary-extended-tube point selected by the Figure-2-4/BHW route.  A
      proof that applies `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube`,
      `BHW.extendF_eq_boundary_value`, or
      `BHW.extendF_eq_boundary_value_ET` directly at `wick (x Γêÿ ╧ä)` or
      `permAct ╧ä (wick x)` is therefore a route error unless it first proves
      the missing adjacent branch membership and boundary theorem above,
      which is exactly the OS-I ┬º4.5 content.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V -> x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          let ╬ª╧ä :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
          ΓêÇ (╧å : SchwartzNPoint d n)
            (_h╧å_comp :
              HasCompactSupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé))
            (h╧å_supp :
              tsupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γèå hChart.V0),
            let ╧åZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              Γƒ¿╧å, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) ╧å
                h╧å_suppΓƒ⌐
            let ╧êZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä.symm ╧åZ
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * ╧å x
              =
            OS.S n ╧êZ
      ```

      Mathematical role of this theorem: it is the source-coordinate form of
      the canonical-lift boundary gate.  It says that the adjacent
      source-coordinate branch
      `Z Γåª hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z)`, pulled back along the
      Wick real section, has the compact boundary distribution selected by
      the Euclidean permuted test `╧êZ`.  It is derived from the
      canonical-lift theorem by source-Gram rewriting, not used to prove that
      theorem.

      The implementation-level derivation obligations are:

      1. Build `╧åZ` and
         `╧êZ := permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä.symm ╧åZ`.
         `OS.E3_symmetric` supplies only the Euclidean symmetry
         `OS.S n ╧åZ = OS.S n ╧êZ`; it is not used as pointwise permutation
         invariance of `bvt_F`.
      2. Prove the source branch is holomorphic on the selected chart:
         `hRep.Phi_holomorphic.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram ╧ä`, restricted
         to the chart domain by `hChart.double_mem`, then composed with
         `sourceMinkowskiGram` on `hChart.Usrc` by
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram`.
      3. Rewrite the source pullback to the canonical lift on `hChart.V0`
         using `hRep.branch_eq` and `hChart.adjLift_sourceGram`; outside
         `hChart.V0`, the compact-support hypothesis gives `╧å x = 0`.
      4. Apply the canonical-lift source-boundary theorem
         `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`.
      5. Use `bvt_euclidean_restriction` only to rewrite the selected ACR(1)
         compact germ as `OS.S n ╧êZ`.  The finite permutation change of
         variables remains the checked one from
         `os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector`.
      6. The proof does not evaluate
         `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` at `wick (x Γêÿ ╧ä)` or
         `permAct ╧ä (wick x)`.  The adjacent Wick section is not obtained from
         ordered-positive-time forward-tube membership; it enters the ordinary
         extended tube only through the Figure-2-4 canonical lift.

      The canonical-lift theorem above is the mathematically primitive
      boundary statement in this block.  For Lean bookkeeping it is useful to
      isolate the source-coordinate ACR-kernel form below: the right hand side is
      exactly the `bvt_euclidean_restriction` pairing for the permuted
      zero-diagonal test `╧êZ`.  Thus this theorem and
      `BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger` differ
      only by the already checked OS-II ACR boundary-value rewrite.  Neither
      theorem may be treated as proved by that rewrite alone; the hard
      content is still the canonical Figure-2-4 lift pairing, transported to
      the source branch on the left by `hRep.branch_eq` and
      `hChart.adjLift_sourceGram`.

      The isolated ACR-kernel form is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_acrPermutedBoundary
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V -> x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          let ╬ª╧ä :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
          ΓêÇ (╧å : SchwartzNPoint d n)
            (_h╧å_comp :
              HasCompactSupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé))
            (h╧å_supp :
              tsupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γèå hChart.V0),
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * ╧å x
              =
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  ╧å (fun k => x (╧ä k))
      ```

      Proof transcript required for this theorem:

      1. Define
         `╧åZ := Γƒ¿╧å, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap ...Γƒ⌐` and
         `╧êZ := permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä.symm ╧åZ`.
         The right side is exactly `OS.S n ╧êZ` rewritten by the checked
         theorem `bvt_euclidean_restriction`; this uses only the definitional
         equation
         `(permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz ╧ä.symm ╧åZ).1 x =
           ╧å (fun k => x (╧ä k))`.
      2. The left side is the compact Wick real-section pairing of the
         Hall-Wightman source branch
         `Z Γåª hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z)` restricted to
         `hChart.Usrc`.  Its holomorphy is obtained by
         `hRep.Phi_holomorphic.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram ╧ä`,
         `hChart.double_mem`, and
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram`.
      3. On `hChart.V0`, the deterministic Figure-2-4 lift gives the
         ordinary extended-tube realization of that same scalar point:
         `hChart.adjLift_sourceGram x hx 0` rewrites
         `sourceMinkowskiGram (hChart.adjLift x 0)` to
         `sourcePermuteComplexGram ╧ä (sourceMinkowskiGram (wick x))`.
         Therefore `hRep.branch_eq` rewrites the left integrand to
         `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (hChart.adjLift x 0) * ╧å x`.  Outside
         `hChart.V0`, `╧å x = 0` by `h╧å_supp`, so the rewrite is valid under
         the integral without choosing any existential `╬ö_x`.
      4. The remaining equality

         ```lean
         Γê½ x, BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * ╧å x
           =
         OS.S n ╧êZ
         ```

         is the genuine OS-I ┬º4.5 Figure-2-4 `S'_n` boundary theorem.  It
         says that the rotated adjacent ordinary-extended-tube realization
         carried by the selected chart is the BHW continuation of the same
         symmetric Euclidean compact-test branch selected by E3.  This is not
         local EOW, not final Wightman locality, not global PET branch
         independence, and not a pointwise permutation-symmetry shortcut.
      5. Step 4 is supplied directly by
         `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`.  The
         ACR-kernel theorem is therefore derived from the canonical compact
         theorem by `hRep.branch_eq`, `hChart.adjLift_sourceGram`, and the
         Euclidean restriction for `╧êZ`; it does not call the raw pointwise
         branch theorem or any raw compact theorem.

      The current adjacent proof-document gap is therefore the canonical-lift
      OS-I ┬º4.5/Bargmann-Hall-Wightman/Jost compact-boundary transcript, not a
      specialized raw adjacent-Wick pointwise comparison.  The displayed raw
      comparison and raw Schwinger-valued theorems are downstream
      deterministic transports after the local scalar source equality exists.
      Adding any of these statements to Lean as a wrapper, `sorry`, `admit`,
      or unapproved axiom before the canonical compact proof is audited would
      violate the implementation gate.

      Once the canonical-lift compact theorem is proved, the ACR-kernel form
      is mechanical:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_acrPermutedBoundary ... := by
        classical
        let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
        let ╬ª╧ä :=
          fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé =>
            hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
        intro ╧å h╧å_comp h╧å_supp
        let ╧åZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          Γƒ¿╧å, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
            (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
            (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) ╧å h╧å_suppΓƒ⌐
        let ╧êZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä.symm ╧åZ
        have hsource_to_lift :
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * ╧å x
              =
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * ╧å x := by
          refine integral_congr_ae ?_
          filter_upwards with x
          by_cases hx : x Γêê hChart.V0
          ┬╖ have hET :
                hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) Γêê
                  BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
              hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube x hx (0 : unitInterval)
            have hGram :
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                    (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
                  =
                BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) := by
              simpa [╧ä, BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero] using
                hChart.adjLift_sourceGram x hx (0 : unitInterval)
            have hbranch :
                hRep.Phi
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                      (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)))
                  =
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) :=
              hRep.branch_eq _ hET
            calc
              ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * ╧å x
                  =
                hRep.Phi
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                      (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))) * ╧å x := by
                    simp [╬ª╧ä, hGram]
              _ =
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * ╧å x := by
                    rw [hbranch]
          ┬╖ have h╧åx : ╧å x = 0 := by
              exact
                (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                  (fun hxSupp => hx (h╧å_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
            simp [h╧åx]
        have hlift :
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * ╧å x
              =
            OS.S n ╧êZ :=
          BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart
            ╧å h╧å_comp h╧å_supp
        have h╧ê :
            OS.S n ╧êZ =
              Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  ╧å (fun k => x (╧ä k)) := by
          simpa [╧êZ, ╧åZ, ╧ä] using
            bvt_euclidean_restriction (d := d) OS lgc n ╧êZ
        exact hsource_to_lift.trans (hlift.trans h╧ê)
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof skeleton for the source-pullback theorem after that
      comparison is available:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger ... := by
        classical
        let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
        let ╬ª╧ä :=
          fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé =>
            hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
        intro ╧å h╧å_comp h╧å_supp
        let ╧åZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          Γƒ¿╧å, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
            (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
            (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) ╧å h╧å_suppΓƒ⌐
        let ╧êZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä.symm ╧åZ
        have hsrc_acr :
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * ╧å x
              =
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  ╧å (fun k => x (╧ä k)) := by
          exact
            BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_acrPermutedBoundary
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
              hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hRep hChart
              ╧å h╧å_comp h╧å_supp
        have h╧ê :
            OS.S n ╧êZ =
              Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  ╧å (fun k => x (╧ä k)) := by
          simpa [╧êZ, ╧åZ, ╧ä] using
            bvt_euclidean_restriction (d := d) OS lgc n ╧êZ
        exact hsrc_acr.trans h╧ê.symm
      ```

      The theorem
      `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger` is the
      upstream OS-I ┬º4.5/BHW/Jost compact-boundary proof obligation for the
      adjacent trace theorem.  It is deliberately earlier than the raw
      adjacent-Wick pointwise branch theorem; deriving it from that pointwise
      theorem would be circular.  The displayed
      `BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_acrPermutedBoundary` theorem
      is a genuine derived theorem, not a wrapper: it removes only the
      already-checked Euclidean functional rewrite and the
      `hRep.branch_eq`/Figure-2-4 source-Gram rewrite from the
      Hall-Wightman comparison.

      The theorem
      `BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24`
      is then a short composition:

      1. apply
         `BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger` to get
         the source-side pairing as `OS.S n ╧êZ`;
      2. use `bvt_euclidean_restriction` for `╧êZ`;
      3. change variables by the checked finite permutation invariance of
         product Lebesgue measure, the same `integral_perm_npoint_volume`
         calculation used in
         `os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector`, and
         `Equiv.swap_inv`, to rewrite the result as the raw adjacent Wick
         pairing.  That helper is currently private in
         `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45.lean`; the Lean port must either expose it
         under a public support name or reprove the same
         `MeasureTheory.volume_measurePreserving_piCongrLeft` one-liner at
         the call site.

      Lean-shaped proof of the composition:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24 ... := by
        classical
        let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
        intro ╧å h╧å_comp h╧å_supp
        let ╧åZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          Γƒ¿╧å, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
            (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
            (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) ╧å h╧å_suppΓƒ⌐
        let ╧êZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä.symm ╧åZ
        have hsrc :
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * ╧å x =
              OS.S n ╧êZ :=
          BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hRep hChart
            ╧å h╧å_comp h╧å_supp
        have h╧ê :
            OS.S n ╧êZ =
              Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  ╧å (fun k => x (╧ä k)) := by
          simpa [╧êZ, ╧åZ, ╧ä] using
            bvt_euclidean_restriction (d := d) OS lgc n ╧êZ
        have hchange :
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  ╧å (fun k => x (╧ä k))
              =
            Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n
                  (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (╧ä k))) * ╧å x := by
          simpa [╧ä, BHW.permAct_wickRotatePoint, Equiv.swap_inv] using
            (integral_perm_npoint_volume
              (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä
              (fun x =>
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  ╧å (fun k => x (╧ä k)))).symm
        exact hsrc.trans (h╧ê.trans hchange)
      ```

      Until this distributional theorem has a complete proof transcript, the
      adjacent `S'_n` scalarization chart is not Lean-ready.  Any proof that
      replaces it by `hRep.branch_eq` followed by
      `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` at `wick (x Γêÿ ╧ä)` is rejected: it proves
      the wrong membership statement.

      Lean-shaped pseudocode for the scalarization chart:

      The helper named below,
      `BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at`, is the Lean-facing packaging of the
      already selected Figure-2-4 source patch at `x0`.  It is not an analytic
      equality theorem.  Its fields are exactly: an open `V0 Γèå V` with
      `x0 Γêê V0`, an open connected nonempty source neighborhood `Usrc`, Wick
      membership of `V0` in `Usrc`, the reverse Wick real-section
      equivalence
      `ΓêÇ x, (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê Usrc Γåö x Γêê V0`,
      double-scalar-domain membership of every source Gram point in `Usrc`,
      forward-tube membership of the identity Wick configuration on `V0`, and
      the deterministic canonical Figure-2-4 adjacent lift on `V0`.
      It deliberately does not carry adjacent raw-Wick forward-tube
      membership.  The lift fields are what prevent the distributional proof
      from integrating arbitrary existential `╬ö_x` witnesses.  The continuity
      input used below must be made a
      public finite-coordinate helper
      `BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig (d := d) (n := n) :
      Continuous (fun x : NPointDomain d n => fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))`;
      the same proof currently exists only as the private helper
      `continuous_wickRotateRealConfig` in
      `OSToWightmanTubeIdentity.lean`.  During the Lean port, either export
      that coordinate proof under the `BHW` support namespace or reprove it
      immediately by `continuous_pi`, `continuous_apply_apply`, and the
      coordinate definition of `wickRotatePoint`.

      Lean-facing support surfaces for the helper:

      ```lean
      def BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          NPointDomain d n :=
        fun k ╬╝ => if h╬╝ : ╬╝ = 0 then (z k 0).im else (z k ╬╝).re

      theorem BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse_wickRotateRealConfig
          (d n : Nat) (x : NPointDomain d n) :
          BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) = x

      theorem BHW.continuous_wickRealSectionLeftInverse
          (d n : Nat) :
          Continuous (BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n)

      theorem BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig
          (d n : Nat) :
          Continuous
            (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
              fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x k))

      theorem BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage
          (d n : Nat)
          {╬⌐ : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (h╬⌐_open : IsOpen ╬⌐)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hz0╬⌐ :
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) Γêê ╬⌐)
          {V : Set (NPointDomain d n)}
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hx0V : x0 Γêê V) :
          Γêâ Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
            IsOpen Usrc Γêº IsConnected Usrc Γêº
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) Γêê Usrc Γêº
            Usrc Γèå ╬⌐ Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x : NPointDomain d n,
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê Usrc -> x Γêê V)

      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.sourceMinkowskiGram_preimage_open
          (d n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hU : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U) :
          IsOpen {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé |
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Γêê U}

      structure BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (x0 : NPointDomain d n) where
        V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n)
        Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
        V0_open : IsOpen V0
        x0_mem : x0 Γêê V0
        V0_sub : V0 Γèå V
        Usrc_open : IsOpen Usrc
        Usrc_connected : IsConnected Usrc
        Usrc_nonempty : Usrc.Nonempty
        wick_mem :
          ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V0 -> (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê Usrc
        wick_realSection_iff :
          ΓêÇ x : NPointDomain d n,
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê Usrc Γåö x Γêê V0
        double_mem :
          ΓêÇ z, z Γêê Usrc ->
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Γêê
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐)
        wick_id_forwardTube :
          ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V0 ->
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n
        adjLift :
          NPointDomain d n -> unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        adjLift_def :
          ΓêÇ x t,
            adjLift x t =
              BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                (d := d) (n := n) hd
                (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐) x t
        adjLift_continuousOn :
          ContinuousOn
            (fun p : NPointDomain d n ├ù unitInterval =>
              adjLift p.1 p.2)
            (V0 ├ù╦ó (Set.univ : Set unitInterval))
        adjLift_mem_extendedTube :
          ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V0 -> ΓêÇ t, adjLift x t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        adjLift_sourceGram :
          ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V0 -> ΓêÇ t,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (adjLift x t) =
              BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n
                (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐)
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t))

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V -> x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              ΓêÇ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä x t Γêê
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Γêê V) :
          BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0
      ```

      The proof of
      `BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage` is pure
      finite-dimensional topology.  Let
      `L := BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n` and
      `z0 := fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)`.  By
      `BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse_wickRotateRealConfig`, `L z0 = x0`.
      The set `╬⌐ Γê⌐ L Γü╗┬╣' V` is open and contains `z0`, using
      `h╬⌐_open`, `hV_open.preimage
      (BHW.continuous_wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n)`, and `hx0V`.
      Choose a metric ball around `z0` contained in this intersection by
      `Metric.mem_nhds_iff`; take `Usrc` to be that ball.  Its openness is
      `Metric.isOpen_ball`, its connectedness is `Metric.isConnected_ball`,
      it contains `z0`, and its inclusions into `╬⌐` and into the Wick preimage
      of `V` are exactly the ball-subset conclusion.  No analytic theorem is
      used in this helper.

      With this helper the source chart is constructed as follows.  Put
      `╧ä := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐`,
      `z0 := fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)`, and
      `╬⌐double := {z | BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Γêê
      BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä}`.  Openness of `╬⌐double`
      is the preimage of the relatively open double scalar domain: use
      `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`
      together with `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected` and
      `(BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous`.  Membership
      `z0 Γêê ╬⌐double` is the Figure-2-4 source realization at the selected
      base point: the identity Wick trace lies in the ordinary forward tube by
      `hV_ordered x0 hx0V`, while the adjacent permuted source Gram is
      realized by the canonical lift
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift hd ╧ä x0 0`, whose extended-tube membership
      is supplied by `hV_adjLift_ET` and whose scalar Gram is identified by
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram`.  Apply
      `BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage` to
      `╬⌐double` and `V`; then define
      `V0 := {x | (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê Usrc}`.  The fields are
      `V0_open` from `BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig` and `Usrc_open`,
      `x0_mem` from `z0 Γêê Usrc`, `V0_sub` from the helper's Wick-preimage
      inclusion, `wick_mem` and `wick_realSection_iff` by the definition of
      `V0`, `double_mem` from `Usrc Γèå ╬⌐double`, and
      `wick_id_forwardTube` from `V0_sub`, `hV_ordered`, and
      `wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector 1`.  The
      lift fields are filled by setting `adjLift :=
      BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift hd ╧ä`, using
      `BHW.continuous_os45Figure24AdjacentLift`, restricting
      `hV_adjLift_ET` along `V0_sub`, and applying
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at ... := by
        classical
        let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
        let z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé :=
          fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)
        let ╬⌐double : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :=
          {z | BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Γêê
            BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä}
        have h╬⌐_open : IsOpen ╬⌐double := by
          have hUrel :
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
                (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected
              (d := d) hd n).1
          have hDoubleRel :
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
                (BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä) :=
            BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain
              (d := d) (n := n) hUrel ╧ä
          simpa [╬⌐double] using
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.sourceMinkowskiGram_preimage_open
              (d := d) (n := n) hDoubleRel
        have hz0╬⌐ : z0 Γêê ╬⌐double := by
          have hleft :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Γêê
                BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
            have hz0FT :
                z0 Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
              simpa [z0] using
                wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector
                  (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
                  (hV_ordered x0 hx0V)
            exact Γƒ¿z0, BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hz0FT, rflΓƒ⌐
          have hright :
              BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) Γêê
                BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
            let ╬ö0 :=
              BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä x0 (0 : unitInterval)
            refine Γƒ¿╬ö0, hV_adjLift_ET x0 hx0V (0 : unitInterval), ?_Γƒ⌐
            have hGram :=
              BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram
                (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä x0 (0 : unitInterval)
            simpa [╬ö0, z0, BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero] using hGram
          exact Γƒ¿hleft, hrightΓƒ⌐
        rcases BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage
            (d := d) (n := n) h╬⌐_open hz0╬⌐ hV_open hx0V with
          Γƒ¿Usrc, hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hz0Usrc,
            hUsrc_sub, hwick_preimage_subΓƒ⌐
        let V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n) :=
          {x | (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê Usrc}
        have hV0_open : IsOpen V0 := by
          simpa [V0] using
            hUsrc_open.preimage
              (BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig (d := d) (n := n))
        have hx0V0 : x0 Γêê V0 := by simpa [V0, z0] using hz0Usrc
        have hV0_sub : V0 Γèå V := by
          intro x hx
          exact hwick_preimage_sub x (by simpa [V0] using hx)
        have hdouble :
            ΓêÇ z, z Γêê Usrc ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Γêê
                BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä := by
          intro z hz
          exact hUsrc_sub hz
        have hwick_id_FT :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V0 ->
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
          intro x hx
          exact wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector
            (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
            (hV_ordered x (hV0_sub hx))
        let adjLift :
            NPointDomain d n -> unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé :=
          BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä
        have hadj_cont :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun p : NPointDomain d n ├ù unitInterval => adjLift p.1 p.2)
              (V0 ├ù╦ó (Set.univ : Set unitInterval)) := by
          exact
            (BHW.continuous_os45Figure24AdjacentLift
              (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä).continuousOn
        have hadj_ET :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V0 -> ΓêÇ t,
              adjLift x t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
          intro x hx t
          exact hV_adjLift_ET x (hV0_sub hx) t
        have hadj_gram :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V0 -> ΓêÇ t,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (adjLift x t) =
                BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                    (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t)) := by
          intro x hx t
          simpa [adjLift] using
            BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram
              (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä x t
        exact
          { V0 := V0, Usrc := Usrc,
            V0_open := hV0_open, x0_mem := hx0V0, V0_sub := hV0_sub,
            Usrc_open := hUsrc_open, Usrc_connected := hUsrc_conn,
            Usrc_nonempty := Γƒ¿z0, hz0UsrcΓƒ⌐,
            wick_mem := by intro x hx; simpa [V0] using hx,
            wick_realSection_iff := by intro x; rfl,
            double_mem := by simpa [╧ä, ╬⌐double] using hdouble,
            wick_id_forwardTube := hwick_id_FT,
            adjLift := adjLift,
            adjLift_def := by intro x t; rfl,
            adjLift_continuousOn := hadj_cont,
            adjLift_mem_extendedTube := hadj_ET,
            adjLift_sourceGram := by simpa [╧ä] using hadj_gram }
      ```

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24 ... := by
        classical
        let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
        let ╬ª0 := fun z => hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        let ╬ª╧ä := fun z =>
          hRep.Phi
            (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
        have hF_holo_BHW :
            DifferentiableOn Γäé (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
          simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
            bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
        have hF_restricted_BHW :
            ΓêÇ (╬¢ : RestrictedLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              z Γêê BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              bvt_F OS lgc n
                (fun k ╬╝ => Γêæ ╬╜, (╬¢.val.val ╬╝ ╬╜ : Γäé) * z k ╬╜) =
              bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
          intro ╬¢ z hz
          exact bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
            (d := d) OS lgc n ╬¢ z
            ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Γû╕ hz)
        -- Figure-2-4 / OS ┬º4.5 source chart around the chosen base point.
        rcases BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_open hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hV_adjLift_ET hx0V with
          Γƒ¿V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
            hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne,
            hwick_mem, hwick_realSection_iff,
            hdouble, hwick_id_FT,
            adjLift, hadj_def, hadj_cont, hadj_ET, hadj_gramΓƒ⌐
        have h╬ª0_diff : DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬ª0 Usrc := by
          have hGramU :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Usrc Γèå hRep.U := by
            intro Z hZ
            rcases hZ with Γƒ¿z, hz, rflΓƒ⌐
            rw [hRep.U_eq]
            exact (hdouble z hz).1
          exact BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram
            (d := d) (n := n) hRep.Phi_holomorphic hGramU
        have h╬ª╧ä_diff : DifferentiableOn Γäé ╬ª╧ä Usrc := by
          have hpre :=
            BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram
              (d := d) (n := n) hRep.Phi_holomorphic ╧ä
          have hGramU :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Usrc Γèå
                {Z | BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z Γêê hRep.U} := by
            intro Z hZ
            rcases hZ with Γƒ¿z, hz, rflΓƒ⌐
            rw [hRep.U_eq]
            exact (hdouble z hz).2
          exact BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram
            (d := d) (n := n) hpre hGramU
        have h╬ª0_wick :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V0 ->
              ╬ª0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) =
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
          intro x hx
          have hwET : (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
            BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube (hwick_id_FT x hx)
          calc
            ╬ª0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))
                = BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
                    simpa [╬ª0, hRep.U_eq] using
                      hRep.branch_eq _ hwET
            _ = bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
                    exact BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube n
                      (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                      hF_holo_BHW hF_restricted_BHW
                      _ (hwick_id_FT x hx)
        have h╬ª╧ä_pairing :
            ΓêÇ ╧å : SchwartzNPoint d n,
              HasCompactSupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) ->
              tsupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γèå V0 ->
                Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * ╧å x
                  =
                Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    bvt_F OS lgc n
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (╧ä k))) * ╧å x := by
          exact
            BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
              hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hRep
              { V0 := V0, Usrc := Usrc,
                V0_open := hV0_open, x0_mem := hx0V0, V0_sub := hV0_sub,
                Usrc_open := hUsrc_open, Usrc_connected := hUsrc_conn,
                Usrc_nonempty := hUsrc_ne, wick_mem := hwick_mem,
                wick_realSection_iff := hwick_realSection_iff,
                double_mem := hdouble,
                wick_id_forwardTube := hwick_id_FT,
                adjLift := adjLift, adjLift_def := hadj_def,
                adjLift_continuousOn := hadj_cont,
                adjLift_mem_extendedTube := hadj_ET,
                adjLift_sourceGram := hadj_gram }
        exact Γƒ¿V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
          hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne,
          hwick_mem, hwick_realSection_iff,
          hdouble, h╬ª0_diff, h╬ª╧ä_diff,
          h╬ª0_wick, by simpa [╧ä] using h╬ª╧ä_pairingΓƒ⌐
      ```

      The source-neighborhood seed theorem is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V -> x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              ΓêÇ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä x t Γêê
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Γêê V)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          Γêâ (V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n))
            (Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)),
              IsOpen V0 Γêº x0 Γêê V0 Γêº V0 Γèå V Γêº
              IsOpen Usrc Γêº IsConnected Usrc Γêº Usrc.Nonempty Γêº
              (ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V0 ->
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê Usrc) Γêº
              (ΓêÇ z, z Γêê Usrc ->
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Γêê
                  BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä) Γêº
              Set.EqOn
                (fun z =>
                  hRep.Phi
                    (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)))
                (fun z => hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
                Usrc
      ```

      Proof transcript:

      1. Apply
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24`, obtaining
         `V0`, `Usrc`, the double-domain field, differentiability of the two
         scalar pullbacks, the identity Wick-section scalarization identity,
         and the adjacent scalar-trace compact-pairing identity.
      2. Invoke the checked compact Wick equality
         `os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector` with
         `╧ü = 1` and with tests supported in `V0`.  The checked theorem is in
         the Wick-rotation namespace-free layer and has production signature
         `OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost ╧ü hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered ╧å
         h╧å_tsupport`; it does not take `hd`, `hV_open`, or a separate
         compactness hypothesis.  Since `V0 Γèå V`, the
         existing `hV_jost`, `hV_ordered`, and `hV_swap_ordered` hypotheses
         restrict directly, with the swapped-order target normalized by
         `Equiv.swap_inv` and `mul_one`: the theorem asks for
         `((Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐).symm * 1)` while the local
         hypothesis is stated for `╧ä`.
      3. Apply
         `SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn`
         on `V0` to recover pointwise equality of the two scalarized
         source pullbacks
         `x Γåª ╬ª0 (wick x)` and `x Γåª ╬ª╧ä (wick x)` from
         `h╬ª0_diff`, `h╬ª╧ä_diff`, and
         `BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig` by Mathlib
         `ContinuousOn.comp'` with the explicit `MapsTo` proof
         `fun x hx => hwick_mem x hx`; `ContinuousOn.comp_continuous` is the
         wrong API here because the Wick map is known to land in `Usrc` only
         on `V0`, not globally.  The integral equality for these two
         scalarized pullbacks is the chain
         adjacent source-pairing theorem, checked compact Wick equality, and
         the identity scalarization `h╬ª0_wick` rewritten under the integral.
         This step deliberately avoids any continuity assumption for the raw
         adjacent total function
         `x Γåª bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (╧ä k)))`.
      4. Apply
         `eqOn_openConnected_of_eqOn_wickRealSection`
         to the connected complex source chart `Usrc`.  The holomorphic
         functions are the scalar pullbacks
         `z Γåª hRep.Phi (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)` and
         `z Γåª hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
         (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))`; their differentiability on `Usrc`
         is supplied by the scalarization chart theorem.

      Lean-shaped pseudocode:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq ... := by
        classical
        let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
        let ╬ª0 := fun z =>
          hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        let ╬ª╧ä := fun z =>
          hRep.Phi
            (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
        rcases BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_open hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hV_adjLift_ET hx0V hRep with
          Γƒ¿V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
            hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne,
            hwick_mem, hwick_realSection_iff,
            hdouble, h╬ª0_diff, h╬ª╧ä_diff,
            h╬ª0_wick, h╬ª╧ä_pairingΓƒ⌐
        have hV0_ne : V0.Nonempty := Γƒ¿x0, hx0V0Γƒ⌐
        have hwick_ne :
            Γêâ x : NPointDomain d n,
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê Usrc :=
          Γƒ¿x0, hwick_mem x0 hx0V0Γƒ⌐
        have hwick_eq :
            ΓêÇ x : NPointDomain d n,
              x Γêê V0 ->
                ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) =
                  ╬ª0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
          have hcont╬ª╧ä_real :
              ContinuousOn
                (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                  ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) V0 :=
            h╬ª╧ä_diff.continuousOn.comp'
              (BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig (d := d) (n := n)).continuousOn
              (by
                intro x hx
                exact hwick_mem x hx)
          have hcont╬ª0_real :
              ContinuousOn
                (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                  ╬ª0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) V0 :=
            h╬ª0_diff.continuousOn.comp'
              (BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig (d := d) (n := n)).continuousOn
              (by
                intro x hx
                exact hwick_mem x hx)
          have hpoint :
              Set.EqOn
                (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                  ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)))
                (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                  ╬ª0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)))
                V0 := by
            refine
              SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn
                hV0_open hcont╬ª╧ä_real hcont╬ª0_real ?compact_eq
            ┬╖ intro ╧å h╧å_comp h╧å_supp
              calc
                Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    ╬ª╧ä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * ╧å x
                    =
                  Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    bvt_F OS lgc n
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (╧ä k))) * ╧å x :=
                    h╬ª╧ä_pairing ╧å h╧å_comp h╧å_supp
                _ =
                  Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    bvt_F OS lgc n
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * ╧å x :=
                    os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector
                      (d := d) OS lgc n i hi V0
                      (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hV0_sub hx))
                      (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
                      (fun x hx => hV_ordered x (hV0_sub hx))
                      (fun x hx => by
                        simpa [╧ä, Equiv.swap_inv] using
                          hV_swap_ordered x (hV0_sub hx))
                      ╧å h╧å_supp
                _ =
                  Γê½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    ╬ª0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * ╧å x := by
                    refine integral_congr_ae ?_
                    filter_upwards with x
                    by_cases hx : x Γêê V0
                    ┬╖ simpa [h╬ª0_wick x hx]
                    ┬╖ have h╧åx : ╧å x = 0 := by
                        exact
                          (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                            (fun hxSupp => hx (h╧å_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
                      simp [h╧åx]
          intro x hx
          exact hpoint x hx
        have hEqOnUsrc : Set.EqOn ╬ª╧ä ╬ª0 Usrc :=
          eqOn_openConnected_of_eqOn_wickRealSection
            (d := d) (n := n) Usrc hUsrc_open hUsrc_conn hwick_ne
            ╬ª╧ä ╬ª0 h╬ª╧ä_diff h╬ª0_diff
            (fun x hxU => by
              -- `hxU` is a Wick point in `Usrc`; shrink-chart construction
              -- supplies the corresponding `x Γêê V0` for the real section used
              -- by the identity theorem.
              have hxV0 : x Γêê V0 := (hwick_realSection_iff x).1 hxU
              exact hwick_eq x hxV0)
        exact Γƒ¿V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
          hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne, hwick_mem,
          hdouble, hEqOnUsrcΓƒ⌐
      ```

      The final real-section membership bridge used in the last lambda must be
      an explicit field of the source chart: the Wick real section of `Usrc`
      used by `eqOn_openConnected_of_eqOn_wickRealSection` is exactly the
      shrunken `V0`, not an unspecified set of Euclidean configurations.  The
      source-equivalence field is consumed only to prove equality on `Usrc`;
      the exported source equality theorem may keep only the forward
      membership field `hwick_mem`.

      The scalar-image extraction theorem used by the adjacent seed is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localConnectedRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular_allArity
          (d n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hreg : BHW.SourceComplexGramRegularAt d n z0)
          {Vsrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hVsrc_open : IsOpen Vsrc)
          (hz0Vsrc : z0 Γêê Vsrc) :
          Γêâ U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
            IsOpen U Γêº IsConnected U Γêº z0 Γêê U Γêº U Γèå Vsrc Γêº
            Γêâ O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Γêê O Γêº
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n O Γêº
              IsConnected O Γêº O.Nonempty Γêº
              O Γèå BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' U Γêº
              (ΓêÇ G, G Γêê O ->
                Γêâ z Γêê U, BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z = G)
      ```

      Proof transcript: if `d + 1 <= n`, this is the checked theorem
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localConnectedRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular`
      with the rank-exact field forgotten.  If `n < d + 1`, first use the
      checked all-arity local image theorem
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular`.
      Since `sourceComplexGramVariety d n =
      sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n` in this easy range by
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace_of_le`,
      pass to full symmetric coordinates with
      `BHW.sourceFullSymCoordMapCLM`,
      `BHW.isOpen_sourceFullSymCoordMap_preimage_of_relOpen_of_le`, and the
      inverse coordinate map used in
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle_easy`; choose a small
      connected coordinate ball inside the relatively open image and push it
      forward by `sourceFullSymCoordMap`.  The resulting `O` is connected,
      relatively open, nonempty, and still contained in the image supplied by
      `sourceComplexGramMap_localRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular`.

      The adjacent scalar seed theorem is then:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_regular_sourcePoint_in_open_neighborhood
          (d n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé}
          (hz0U : z0 Γêê U) :
          Γêâ zreg,
            zreg Γêê U Γêº BHW.SourceComplexGramRegularAt d n zreg
      ```

      This helper is a one-line use of
      `(BHW.dense_sourceComplexGramRegularAt d n).exists_mem_open hU_open
      Γƒ¿z0, hz0UΓƒ⌐`; it is listed only because both the local image theorem and
      the later path theorem need the returned membership proof.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V -> x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              ΓêÇ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä x t Γêê
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Γêê V)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          Γêâ (Wseed : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé))
            (Gseed : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wseed Γêº
            IsConnected Wseed Γêº
            Wseed.Nonempty Γêº
            Gseed Γêê Wseed Γêº
            Wseed Γèå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä Γêº
            Set.EqOn
              (fun Z =>
                hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z))
              hRep.Phi
              Wseed
      ```

      Proof transcript:

      1. Apply
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq`, obtaining
         `V0`, `Usrc`, the source-level scalar equality, and the double-domain
         membership of every scalar image point.
      2. Let `zwick0 := fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)`.  Since
         `x0 Γêê V0`, the chart theorem gives `zwick0 Γêê Usrc`.  Choose a complex
         source-Gram regular point `zreg Γêê Usrc` using
         `BHW.dense_sourceComplexGramRegularAt` inside a small open ball in
         `Usrc` around `zwick0`; record `Gseed :=
         sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg`.
      3. Apply
         `BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localConnectedRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular_allArity`
         to `zreg` inside `Usrc`.  This returns a connected open source ball
         `Ureg Γèå Usrc` and a connected nonempty relatively open scalar image
         `Wseed` with `Gseed Γêê Wseed` and
         `Wseed Γèå sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Ureg`.
      4. Since `Ureg Γèå Usrc`, the source-level equality from step 1 descends
         to `Wseed`: for `G Γêê Wseed`, choose `z Γêê Ureg` with
         `sourceMinkowskiGram d n z = G` and rewrite.  The double-domain
         inclusion of `Wseed` follows from the double-domain field on `Usrc`.
      5. This `Wseed` is the local `S'_n` scalar overlap; it is not the final
         `S''_n` PET branch law and it does not use local EOW, real-edge
         adjacent equality, or final `bvt_W` locality.

      Lean-shaped pseudocode:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq ... := by
        classical
        let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
        rcases BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_open hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hV_adjLift_ET hx0V hRep with
          Γƒ¿V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
            hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne,
            hwick_mem, hdouble, hEq_srcΓƒ⌐
        let zwick0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé :=
          fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)
        have hzwick0 : zwick0 Γêê Usrc := hwick_mem x0 hx0V0
        -- Pick a small open ball inside `Usrc` around the Wick point and use
        -- density of regular complex source configurations.
        rcases BHW.exists_regular_sourcePoint_in_open_neighborhood
            (d := d) (n := n) hUsrc_open hzwick0 with
          Γƒ¿zreg, hzregUsrc, hzreg_regularΓƒ⌐
        let Gseed := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg
        rcases
          BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localConnectedRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular_allArity
            (d := d) (n := n) hzreg_regular hUsrc_open hzregUsrc with
          Γƒ¿Ureg, hUreg_open, hUreg_conn, hzregUreg, hUreg_sub,
            Wseed, hGseedW, hW_rel, hW_conn, hW_ne,
            hW_image, hW_realizeΓƒ⌐
        have hW_double :
            Wseed Γèå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä := by
          intro G hG
          rcases hW_realize G hG with Γƒ¿z, hzUreg, rflΓƒ⌐
          exact hdouble z (hUreg_sub hzUreg)
        have hW_eq :
            Set.EqOn
              (fun Z => hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z))
              hRep.Phi
              Wseed := by
          intro G hG
          rcases hW_realize G hG with Γƒ¿z, hzUreg, hGzΓƒ⌐
          have hzUsrc : z Γêê Usrc := hUreg_sub hzUreg
          have hsrc := hEq_src z hzUsrc
          simpa [hGz] using hsrc
        exact Γƒ¿Wseed, Gseed, hW_rel, hW_conn, hW_ne,
          hGseedW, hW_double, hW_eqΓƒ⌐
      ```

      The helper `BHW.exists_regular_sourcePoint_in_open_neighborhood` is only
      the standard application of `BHW.dense_sourceComplexGramRegularAt` to the
      open neighborhood `Usrc`; it may be inlined in Lean.  It must return the
      membership proof `hzregUsrc`, because that proof is used both for the
      local image theorem and later for the path from `zreg` back to the Wick
      source point.

      The path theorem needs the same proof in a provenance-preserving form.
      This is an implementation structure, not a second mathematical result:

      ```lean
      structure BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeedWithSourceProvenance
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (x0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) where
        V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n)
        Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)
        zreg : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé
        Wseed : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)
        Gseed : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé
        V0_open : IsOpen V0
        x0_mem_V0 : x0 Γêê V0
        Usrc_open : IsOpen Usrc
        Usrc_connected : IsConnected Usrc
        wick_mem :
          ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V0 -> (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê Usrc
        zreg_mem_Usrc : zreg Γêê Usrc
        Gseed_def : Gseed = BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg
        Wseed_relOpen :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wseed
        Wseed_connected : IsConnected Wseed
        Wseed_nonempty : Wseed.Nonempty
        Gseed_mem : Gseed Γêê Wseed
        Wseed_double :
          Wseed Γèå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
            (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐)
        Wseed_eq :
          Set.EqOn
            (fun Z =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐) Z))
            hRep.Phi Wseed
        Usrc_double :
          ΓêÇ z, z Γêê Usrc ->
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Γêê
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐)

      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_with_sourceProvenance
          ... :
          BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeedWithSourceProvenance
            hd OS lgc n i hi V x0 hRep
      ```

      In the eventual Lean file, either return this structure directly and
      derive `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq` by
      projection, or prove the public seed theorem first and keep the same
      local variables in the path proof.  What is forbidden is discarding
      `Usrc`, `zreg`, `hwick_mem`, and `zreg_mem_Usrc` and then trying to
      recover them from the scalar-only seed.

      Since `╧ä` is an adjacent transposition, Lean normalizes the inverse by
      `Equiv.swap_inv`; after `change ╧äΓü╗┬╣ = ╧ä` and `simpa [╧ä]`, this is the
      exact equality `╧ä.symm = ╧ä` needed by the packet whose branch label is
      `╬▓ = ╧ä`.  Thus the scalar function in this theorem,
      `Z Γåª hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z)`, is exactly the
      downstream `╬▓.symm` function when the one-branch theorem is instantiated
      at `╬▓ = ╧ä`.

      The seed used by the corridor must carry its Figure-2-4 path
      provenance.  This provenance is not a bare scalar-domain assertion.
      First isolate the configuration-level realization path supplied by the
      Streater-Wightman Figure-2-4 geometry, then obtain the scalar path by
      applying `sourceMinkowskiGram`.

      The realization-level theorem is the genuine paper-geometry input, and
      it must be produced by the **selected** Figure-2-4 source patch.  It is
      not a theorem about an arbitrary later real-open `V` satisfying only the
      broad Jost, ordered-sector, and horizontal pulled-domain fields.  Those
      fields give the endpoint domain memberships; by themselves they do not
      give a continuous ordinary-extended-tube realization of the adjacent
      permuted Gram point along the whole Wick-to-quarter-turn deformation.

      The Lean producer is therefore a path-stable refinement of the source
      selector.  It must export two related fields.  First, on the open chart
      `V`, it exports the deterministic canonical lift membership
      `hV_adjLift_ET` used by the distributional source-pairing theorem.  This
      field is the restriction of the compact-open `Upath` theorem for
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift`; it is not the same as choosing a
      pointwise witness.  Second, for the later corridor it exports the
      following closure-level path field:

      ```lean
      hV_figPath_closure :
        let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
        let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
        ΓêÇ x, x Γêê closure V ->
          let y :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x
          Γêâ (╬ô ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
            Continuous ╬ô Γêº
            Continuous ╬ö Γêº
            ╬ô (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêº
            ╬ô (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ t, ╬ô t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ t, ╬ö t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ t,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ô t)))
      ```

      This field is not a wrapper assumption.  Its proof belongs to the
      Streater-Wightman Figure-2-4 selector:

      1. Start with the explicit adjacent two-plane real witness already
         formalized in
         `ComplexLieGroups/AdjacentOverlapWitness.lean`.  In current Lean this
         is the `swapWitnessReal`/`swapWitnessRealSwapped` model, the
         `3-4-5` spatial rotation `spatialRotCLG12`, and the checked endpoint
         theorem `BHW.adjacent_overlap_real_jost_witness_exists`.
      2. Let
         `c t := ((t : Γä¥) / 2 : Γäé) +
           (((1 : Γä¥) - (t : Γä¥) / 2 : Γä¥) : Γäé) * Complex.I`
         on `unitInterval`.  For `x Γêê V`, set
         `╬ô t k 0 := c t * (x k 0 : Γäé)` and
         `╬ô t k ╬╝ := (x k ╬╝ : Γäé)` for `╬╝ Γëá 0`.  Then `╬ô 0` is the Wick
         configuration and `╬ô 1 = Q.symm (realEmbed
         (os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 x))` by
         `os45QuarterTurnCLE_symm_apply`.  The identity ordered-sector field
         proves `╬ô t Γêê ForwardTube d n`, hence `╬ô t Γêê ExtendedTube d n`,
         because the imaginary time gaps are the positive scalar
         `(1 - t/2)` times the ordered Euclidean time gaps and the spatial
         imaginary parts are zero.
      3. The adjacent realization is not `fun k => ╬ô t (╧ä k)`.  Instead use
         the Figure-2-4 two-plane Lorentz-rotation witness from the checked
         adjacent-overlap proof.  In Lean terms, promote the private rotation
         data behind `realEmbed_swapWitnessRealSwapped_eq_action_rot` to a
         public support theorem for the whole interpolation.  The support
         theorem must state the actual realization:

         ```lean
         Γêâ ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
           Continuous ╬ö Γêº
           (ΓêÇ t, ╬ö t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
           ΓêÇ t,
             BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
               BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                 (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ô t))
         ```

         It may internally represent `╬ö t` as a complex-Lorentz transform of a
         pre-rotated adjacent interpolation, but the exported conclusion is
         ordinary extended-tube membership, not bare membership of the
         relabelled path in the forward tube.
      4. The previous step is a compact-open argument, not a new analytic
         theorem.  After the bounded ordered perturbation has selected
         `xseed` inside the Figure-2-4 real environment, the explicit
         two-plane inequalities are strict for the displayed one-parameter
         interpolation at `xseed`.  The coordinate expressions are continuous
         in `(x,t)`, and `unitInterval` is compact; shrink the real source
         environment around `xseed` so the same extended-tube realization
         certificate remains valid uniformly for all `t`.  This is why the
         path field belongs to the selected source patch `V`, not to an
         arbitrary open set with similar endpoint memberships.
      5. The Gram identity is exactly Lorentz invariance plus permutation of
         labels:
         `sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction`,
         `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`, and `Equiv.swap_inv` to normalize
         `╧ä.symm` to `╧ä` for adjacent swaps.
         This is the point where the realization proof transports the kernel
         through the local coordinates; no function values, no local EOW
         conclusion, and no final `bvt_W` distribution appear.

      Lean-ready subpacket for `hV_figPath_closure`:

      ```lean
      def BHW.os45Figure24TimeCoeff (t : unitInterval) : Γäé :=
        (((t : Γä¥) / 2 : Γä¥) : Γäé) +
          (((1 : Γä¥) - (t : Γä¥) / 2 : Γä¥) : Γäé) * Complex.I

      def BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
          (x : NPointDomain d n)
          (t : unitInterval) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé :=
        fun k ╬╝ =>
          if ╬╝ = 0 then BHW.os45Figure24TimeCoeff t * (x k 0 : Γäé)
          else (x k ╬╝ : Γäé)

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero
          (x : NPointDomain d n) :
          BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x
              (0 : unitInterval) =
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_one
          (x : NPointDomain d n) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
            (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
          BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x
              (1 : unitInterval) =
            Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y)

      theorem BHW.continuous_os45Figure24IdentityPath
          (x : NPointDomain d n) :
          Continuous (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x)

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_of_time_zero
          (x : NPointDomain d n)
          (hx_time0 : ΓêÇ k : Fin n, x k 0 = 0)
          (t : unitInterval) :
          BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t =
            BHW.realEmbed x

      theorem BHW.continuous_os45Figure24IdentityPath_joint :
          Continuous
            (fun p : NPointDomain d n ├ù unitInterval =>
              BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) p.1 p.2)

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_mem_forwardTube
          {x : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx_ordered :
            x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) :
          ΓêÇ t,
            BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t Γêê
              BHW.ForwardTube d n

      -- These finite algebraic definitions and the inverse Lorentz action
      -- theorem live in
      -- `ComplexLieGroups/AdjacentOverlapWitness.lean`, not in the OS45
      -- companion file, because the public Figure-2-4 witness export needs
      -- them without creating an import cycle.
      def BHW.figure24Axis1 (hd : 2 <= d) : Fin (d + 1) := Γƒ¿1, by omegaΓƒ⌐

      def BHW.figure24Axis2 (hd : 2 <= d) : Fin (d + 1) := Γƒ¿2, by omegaΓƒ⌐

      def BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé :=
        fun k ╬╝ =>
          if ╬╝ = 0 then z k ╬╝
          else if ╬╝ = BHW.figure24Axis1 hd then
            ((3 / 5 : Γä¥) : Γäé) * z k (BHW.figure24Axis1 hd) -
              ((4 / 5 : Γä¥) : Γäé) * z k (BHW.figure24Axis2 hd)
          else if ╬╝ = BHW.figure24Axis2 hd then
            ((4 / 5 : Γä¥) : Γäé) * z k (BHW.figure24Axis1 hd) +
              ((3 / 5 : Γä¥) : Γäé) * z k (BHW.figure24Axis2 hd)
          else z k ╬╝

      theorem BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse
          (hd : 2 <= d) :
          Γêâ ╬¢fig : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ΓêÇ z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé,
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢fig
                (BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd z) =
              z

      theorem BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction
          (╬¢ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
              (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢ z) =
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z

      theorem BHW.continuous_figure24RotatedIdentityPath
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          Continuous
            (fun p : NPointDomain d n ├ù unitInterval =>
              BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd
                (BHW.permAct (d := d) ╧ä
                  (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                    (d := d) (n := n) p.1 p.2)))

      theorem BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlaneRotationSupport
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
            Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          Γêâ (xfig xrot : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γä¥)
            (╬¢fig : ComplexLorentzGroup d),
            (ΓêÇ k : Fin n, xfig k 0 = 0) Γêº
            xfig Γêê BHW.JostSet d n Γêº
            BHW.realEmbed xfig Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n Γêº
            BHW.realEmbed xrot =
              BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd
                (BHW.realEmbed (fun k => xfig (╧ä k))) Γêº
            xrot Γêê BHW.ForwardJostSet d n
              (Nat.succ_le_of_lt (Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le
                (Nat.zero_lt_succ _) hd)) Γêº
            BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬¢fig (BHW.realEmbed xrot) =
              BHW.realEmbed
                (fun k => xfig (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k))

      theorem BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlanePathSupport_at_orderedSeed
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          Γêâ xseed : NPointDomain d n,
            xseed Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector
              (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) Γêº
            (fun k => xseed (╧ä k)) Γêê
              EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä Γêº
            let ╬öseed : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé :=
              fun t =>
                BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) ╧ä
                    (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                      (d := d) (n := n) xseed t))
            Continuous ╬öseed Γêº
              (ΓêÇ t, ╬öseed t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
              (ΓêÇ t,
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬öseed t) =
                  BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                      (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                        (d := d) (n := n) xseed t)))

      theorem BHW.swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          Γêâ (Upath : Set (NPointDomain d n))
            (xfig : NPointDomain d n),
            IsOpen Upath Γêº xfig Γêê Upath Γêº
            (ΓêÇ k : Fin n, xfig k 0 = 0) Γêº
            xfig Γêê BHW.JostSet d n Γêº
            BHW.realEmbed xfig Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n Γêº
            BHW.realEmbed (fun k => xfig (╧ä k)) Γêê
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x Γêê Upath,
              ΓêÇ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä x t Γêê
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x Γêê Upath, ΓêÇ t,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                    (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä x t)
                =
              BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                    (d := d) (n := n) x t)))
      ```

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists`: start from the
      same equal-time witness used by
      `BHW.adjacent_overlap_real_jost_witness_exists`, together with the
      concrete two-plane rotated witness from
      `BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlaneRotationSupport`.  At this equal-time
      anchor, `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath xfig t = BHW.realEmbed xfig` for
      every `t`, so the rotated adjacent path is the constant
      `BHW.realEmbed xrot` and lies in `ExtendedTube` by the checked
      `ForwardJostSet` inclusion.  The map
      `(x,t) Γåª figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd
        (permAct ╧ä (os45Figure24IdentityPath x t))`
      is continuous, so compactness of `unitInterval` and
      `BHW.exists_open_nhds_forall_mem_of_compact_parameter` give one open
      neighborhood `Upath` of the equal-time anchor on which the canonical
      rotated adjacent path stays in `ExtendedTube` for all `t`.  The scalar
      Gram identity is algebraic and holds pointwise in `t` by the exported
      Lorentz inverse and `sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`; it must be exported as a
      theorem about `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift`, not hidden behind an
      existential `╬ö`.

      Crucial order: `Upath` is not an ordered-sector neighborhood; it is a
      Figure-2-4 path-stability neighborhood around the equal-time anchor.
      In `BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch`, first
      intersect the Figure-2-4 real environment `Ufig` with this `Upath`, then
      choose the positive-time perturbation inside `Ufig Γê⌐ Upath`, and only
      after that intersect with the identity and adjacent ordered-sector
      preimages before taking the final precompact ball `V`.  This is what
      makes the closure-level `hV_figPath_closure` field share the same anchor as
      the horizontal pulled-branch-domain fields.

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlanePathSupport_at_orderedSeed`: start from
      `BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlaneRotationSupport`; write
      `╧ä := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐`, and choose the returned
      equal-time witness `xfig`, rotated witness `xrot`, and Lorentz element
      `╬¢fig`.  The support theorem must export the formula
      `realEmbed xrot =
      figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd (realEmbed (fun k => xfig (╧ä k)))`;
      this is not optional, because it is what identifies the path-stability
      base point with the checked Figure-2-4 endpoint calculation.

      The public rotation-support theorem is a wrapper around the concrete
      calculation already present in `AdjacentOverlapWitness.lean`, but the
      wrapper has mathematical content: it exposes exactly the real witness
      needed by the source-patch path.  Instantiate
      `xfig := swapWitnessReal hd i hi` and
      `xrot := swapWitnessRealSwappedRot hd i hi`, with the public axes
      `figure24Axis1 hd = e1 hd` and `figure24Axis2 hd = e2 hd`.  The
      coordinate identity
      `realEmbed xrot =
        figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd (realEmbed (fun k => xfig (╧ä k)))`
      is proved by `ext k ╬╝`, then the four cases
      `╬╝ = 0`, `╬╝ = figure24Axis1 hd`, `╬╝ = figure24Axis2 hd`, and the
      remaining coordinates.  The nonzero/axis-distinct side conditions are
      exactly the existing `e1_ne_zero`, `e2_ne_zero`, and `e2_ne_e1`
      calculations, publicized under the `figure24Axis*` names.

      The forward-Jost field for `xrot` is also a concrete finite inequality
      check, not a topological assertion.  For every consecutive index `k`,
      the time difference of `xrot` is `0`.  The first spatial-axis difference
      is:

      * at `k = i`,
        `(3 / 5) * 2 + (-4 / 5) * 1 = 2 / 5 > 0`;
      * at `k = Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐`,
        `(3 / 5) * (-1) + (-4 / 5) * (-1) = 1 / 5 > 0`;
      * at every other `k`,
