        `(3 / 5) * ╬öe1 + (-4 / 5) * 0 > 0`, using the already checked
        positivity of the swapped witness's first-axis difference.

      Therefore `|╬ötime| = 0 < ╬öaxis1`, which is precisely
      `ForwardJostSet d n hd1`.  Applying
      `forwardJostSet_subset_extendedTube hd1` gives the endpoint
      `realEmbed xrot Γêê ExtendedTube d n`.  The Lorentz element exported by
      `figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse` is the inverse of the
      same `(3,4,5)` spatial rotation, so applying it to
      `realEmbed xrot` recovers `realEmbed (fun k => xfig (╧ä k))`.

      The identity path theorem is where the `0 Γëñ t Γëñ 1` arithmetic enters.
      For `t : unitInterval`, use `t.property.1 : 0 Γëñ (t : Γä¥)` and
      `t.property.2 : (t : Γä¥) Γëñ 1` to prove
      `0 < 1 - (t : Γä¥) / 2` by `nlinarith`.  If
      `x` is in the identity ordered positive-time sector, every consecutive
      Euclidean time gap `╬┤k` is positive.  The imaginary part of the
      corresponding consecutive difference in
      `os45Figure24IdentityPath x t` has time component
      `(1 - (t : Γä¥) / 2) * ╬┤k` and all spatial components zero, while the
      real part is irrelevant for forward-tube membership.  Thus the imaginary
      difference lies in the open forward cone by `mul_pos`; this proves
      `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_mem_forwardTube`.  The endpoint theorems
      are coordinate extensionality: at `t = 0`, the coefficient is `I`; at
      `t = 1`, it is `1 / 2 + (1 / 2) * I`, exactly the inverse OS45
      quarter-turn coordinate of `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 x`.
      The zero-time lemma
      `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_of_time_zero` is also pure coordinate
      extensionality: if `ΓêÇ k, x k 0 = 0`, then the time coordinate is
      `os45Figure24TimeCoeff t * 0 = 0`, and every spatial coordinate is
      already `(x k ╬╝ : Γäé)`, so the whole path is `BHW.realEmbed x` for every
      `t`.

      Define the two-variable path map
      `H x t :=
        figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd
          (permAct ╧ä (os45Figure24IdentityPath x t))`.
      The joint-continuity helper
      `BHW.continuous_os45Figure24IdentityPath_joint` is the missing Lean
      surface here: prove it by `continuous_pi` twice, splitting on
      `╬╝ = 0`; the time branch is
      `(BHW.continuous_os45Figure24TimeCoeff.comp continuous_snd).mul`
      the continuous real coordinate
      `fun p => (p.1 k 0 : Γäé)`, and the spatial branches are just coordinate
      projections through `Complex.continuous_ofReal`.  Then prove
      `BHW.continuous_figure24RotatedIdentityPath` by composing this joint
      path with the finite permutation map `permAct ╧ä` and the checked finite
      linear map `BHW.continuous_figure24RotateAdjacentConfig`.  Since `xfig` has
      zero time coordinates, `os45Figure24IdentityPath xfig t` is
      `realEmbed xfig` for every `t`; hence the exported rotation formula
      gives `H xfig t = realEmbed xrot` for every `t`.  Because
      `xrot Γêê ForwardJostSet`, `forwardJostSet_subset_extendedTube` gives
      `realEmbed xrot Γêê ExtendedTube d n`; `BHW.isOpen_extendedTube` and the
      compactness of `unitInterval` then give an open neighborhood `Upath` of
      `xfig` such that
      `ΓêÇ x Γêê Upath, ΓêÇ t, H x t Γêê ExtendedTube d n`.

      The compact-parameter step is the following reusable topology lemma,
      whose proof is the standard finite-subcover tube lemma and contains no
      BHW-specific mathematics:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_open_nhds_forall_mem_of_compact_parameter
          {X Y Z : Type*}
          [TopologicalSpace X] [TopologicalSpace Y]
          [TopologicalSpace Z] [CompactSpace Y]
          {f : X ├ù Y -> Z} (hf : Continuous f)
          {x0 : X} {U : Set Z} (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hx0 : ΓêÇ y : Y, f (x0, y) Γêê U) :
          Γêâ V : Set X,
            IsOpen V Γêº x0 Γêê V Γêº
              ΓêÇ x Γêê V, ΓêÇ y : Y, f (x, y) Γêê U := by
        have hP : ΓêÇ y Γêê (Set.univ : Set Y),
            ΓêÇß╢á z : X ├ù Y in ≡¥ô¥ (x0, y), f z Γêê U := by
          intro y _hy
          exact hf.continuousAt.preimage_mem_nhds
            (hU_open.mem_nhds (hx0 y))
        have hEventually :
            ΓêÇß╢á x in ≡¥ô¥ x0,
              ΓêÇ y Γêê (Set.univ : Set Y), f (x, y) Γêê U :=
          (isCompact_univ : IsCompact (Set.univ : Set Y)).
            eventually_forall_of_forall_eventually hP
        rcases mem_nhds_iff.mp hEventually with
          Γƒ¿V, hV_sub, hV_open, hx0VΓƒ⌐
        refine Γƒ¿V, hV_open, hx0V, ?_Γƒ⌐
        intro x hx y
        exact hV_sub hx y trivial
      ```

      Apply the checked bounded perturbation theorem
      `exists_ordered_small_time_perturb_in_adjacent_overlap_of_lt` to this
      `Upath`, the equal-time witness `xfig`, and the zero-time field
      `ΓêÇ k, xfig k 0 = 0`, with any fixed positive upper bound, for example
      `a = 1`.  The theorem first obtains a metric ball around `0` in the
      perturbation parameter from continuity of `adjacentTimePerturb`; it then
      chooses `╬╡ = min ╬┤ a / 2`, so `0 < ╬╡`, `╬╡ < a`, and the perturbed point
      is still in `Upath`.  Its ordering conclusions are exactly the checked
      formula `adjacentTimePerturb_mem_identity_sector`: after perturbation,
      the time coordinate is `╬╡ * ((k : Γä¥) + 1)`, so all times are positive
      and strictly increasing; after applying the adjacent swap, the same
      identity-order calculation is rewritten by `Equiv.Perm.mul_apply`.
      Thus the theorem returns `xseed = adjacentTimePerturb xfig ╬╡` with
      `╬╡ > 0`, `xseed Γêê Upath`, `xseed` in the identity ordered positive-time
      sector, and `(fun k => xseed (╧ä k))` in the `╧ä`-ordered sector.  With
      `╬öseed := H xseed`, continuity follows by fixing the first argument of
      the continuous two-variable map `H`, and
      `ΓêÇ t, ╬öseed t Γêê ExtendedTube d n` is the universal field of `Upath`.

      The Gram identity for `╬öseed` is pointwise in `t`.  From
      `figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse`, choose `╬¢fig` with
      `complexLorentzAction ╬¢fig (╬öseed t) =
        permAct ╧ä (os45Figure24IdentityPath xseed t)`.  Then
      `sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction` rewrites the left side to
      the Gram matrix of the relabelled identity path, and
      `sourceMinkowskiGram_perm` rewrites that as
      `sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
        (sourceMinkowskiGram d n
          (os45Figure24IdentityPath xseed t))`.
      This proof never asserts that `permAct ╧ä ╬ô` is in the forward tube:
      for the adjacent swap it has the wrong ordered time orientation at the
      swapped gap.  The route uses only the rotated extended-tube certificate
      and Lorentz invariance of scalar products, exactly as OS I Figure 2-4
      requires.

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction`: extensionality in the
      two source indices reduces the statement to preservation of the complex
      bilinear Minkowski form.  Unfold `sourceMinkowskiGram` and
      `complexLorentzAction`, expand the two finite sums, commute the sums,
      factor
      `Γêæ ╬╝, (metricSignature d ╬╝ : Γäé) * ╬¢.val ╬╝ ╬▒ * ╬¢.val ╬╝ ╬▓`, and replace
      it by `if ╬▒ = ╬▓ then metricSignature d ╬▒ else 0` using
      `╬¢.metric_preserving`; the diagonal collapse is the same finite-sum
      calculation used by
      `MinkowskiSpace.complexQuadratic_lorentz_invariant`, without needing
      polarization or any new analytic input.

      With `hV_figPath_closure` exported by the selected source patch, the
      per-point theorem below is only the Lean projection used by the scalar
      corridor:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.swFigure24_wickToQuarterTurn_doubleETRealizationPath
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V -> x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hV_horiz_swap :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hV_figPath_closure :
            let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê closure V ->
              let y :=
                BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x
              Γêâ (╬ô ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
                Continuous ╬ô Γêº
                Continuous ╬ö Γêº
                ╬ô (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêº
                ╬ô (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ t, ╬ô t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ t, ╬ö t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ t,
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                    BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ô t))))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Γêê V) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
          Γêâ (╬ô ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
            Continuous ╬ô Γêº
            Continuous ╬ö Γêº
            ╬ô (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) Γêº
            ╬ô (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ t, ╬ô t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ t, ╬ö t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ t,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ô t)))
      ```

      Proof transcript for the realization theorem: unfold the two `let`
      binders in `hV_figPath_closure`, prove
      `hx0_closure : x0 Γêê closure V` by `exact subset_closure hx0V`, and apply
      `hV_figPath_closure x0 hx0_closure`.  The mathematical work is the
      producer of `hV_figPath_closure`, displayed above; this projection theorem
      has no function-value equality, no
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData`, no local EOW output, no global PET
      branch independence, and no `bvt_W` term.  If the call site can consume
      `hV_figPath_closure` directly, do not add this projection as a public
      theorem.

      The scalar path is then the mechanical corollary used by the corridor:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.swFigure24_wickToQuarterTurn_scalarPath
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V -> x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hV_horiz_swap :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hV_figPath_closure :
            let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê closure V ->
              let y :=
                BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x
              Γêâ (╬ô ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
                Continuous ╬ô Γêº
                Continuous ╬ö Γêº
                ╬ô (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêº
                ╬ô (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ t, ╬ô t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ t, ╬ö t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ t,
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                    BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ô t))))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Γêê V) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
          Γêâ ╬│fig : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé,
            Continuous ╬│fig Γêº
            ╬│fig (0 : unitInterval) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) Γêº
            ╬│fig (1 : unitInterval) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0)) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ t,
              ╬│fig t Γêê BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä)
      ```

      Proof transcript: apply
      `BHW.swFigure24_wickToQuarterTurn_doubleETRealizationPath` with
      `hV_figPath_closure`, or consume
      `hV_figPath_closure x0 (subset_closure hx0V)` directly, and set
      `╬│fig t := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ô t)`.  Continuity is
      `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n` composed with `h╬ô_cont`.  The
      endpoint equations are the corresponding equations for `╬ô`.  For each
      `t`, prove
      `╬│fig t Γêê BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä` by
      `BHW.mem_sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_iff_exists_realizations`,
      using realizations `╬ô t` and `╬ö t`, the two extended-tube membership
      fields, `rfl` for the left Gram, and the realization theorem's Gram
      identity for the right Gram.  This scalar theorem is therefore geometry
      only: it has no analytic equality field and no `hRep`.

      The non-topological BHW/Jost source package is therefore:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V -> x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              ΓêÇ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd ╧ä x t Γêê
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_horiz_swap :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Γêê V)
          (hfig_x0 :
            let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            let y0 :=
              BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
            Γêâ (╬ô ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
              Continuous ╬ô Γêº
              Continuous ╬ö Γêº
              ╬ô (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) Γêº
              ╬ô (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0) Γêº
              (ΓêÇ t, ╬ô t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
              (ΓêÇ t, ╬ö t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
              (ΓêÇ t,
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                  BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ô t))))
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
          Γêâ (Wseed : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé))
            (╬│ : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wseed Γêº
              IsConnected Wseed Γêº
              Wseed.Nonempty Γêº
              Wseed Γèå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä Γêº
              Set.EqOn
                (fun Z =>
                  hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z))
                hRep.Phi
                Wseed Γêº
              Continuous ╬│ Γêº
              ╬│ (0 : unitInterval) Γêê Wseed Γêº
              ╬│ (1 : unitInterval) =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0)) Γêº
              (ΓêÇ t,
                ╬│ t Γêê BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä)
      ```

      Proof transcript: first construct `Wseed` by
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq`, obtaining
      the distinguished `Gseed Γêê Wseed` constructed as
      `sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg` with `zreg Γêê Usrc`.  Because `Usrc` is an
	      open connected subset of the finite-dimensional source space, use the
	      checked Mathlib theorem
	      `(IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected hUsrc_open).mp hUsrc_conn` to
	      obtain `hUsrc_path : IsPathConnected Usrc`.  This theorem is in
	      `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.LocPathConnected` and uses the local
	      path-connectedness instance for the finite-dimensional source space.
	      Then use
	      `hUsrc_path.joinedIn zreg hzregUsrc zwick0 hzwickUsrc` (i.e.
      `IsPathConnected.joinedIn`) to get
      `JoinedIn Usrc zreg zwick0`, where
      `zwick0 := fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)`.  Extract the actual source
      path as `hjoin.somePath`; its membership proof is
      `hjoin.somePath_mem`.  Applying `sourceMinkowskiGram d n` with
      `Path.map` and the global continuity supplied by
      `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n` gives a scalar path from `Gseed`
      to the Wick scalar point, and the scalarization chart's double-domain
      field keeps this first segment inside
      `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä`.  Concatenate it with the
      scalar path obtained from the pointwise Figure-2-4 realization
      `hfig_x0` using `Path.trans` (method form `╬│seed.trans ╬│fig`).  The
      range/membership proof for the concatenated path is
      `Path.trans_range`, or `JoinedIn.trans` if the proof is kept in
      joined-in form; do not cite an unspecified path-concatenation lemma.
      Local API audit, 2026-05-01: these exact names exist in the local
      Mathlib tree.  `IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected` is in
      `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.LocPathConnected`; `JoinedIn.somePath`,
      `JoinedIn.somePath_mem`, and `JoinedIn.trans` are in
      `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.PathConnected`; `Path.trans` and
      `Path.trans_range` are in `Mathlib.Topology.Path`.  The archived
      connected-union route must use the checked theorem
      `IsConnected.biUnion_of_reflTransGen` from
      `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.Basic`, whose relation is exactly
      `(s i Γê⌐ s j).Nonempty Γêº i Γêê t` and whose connected version also requires
      `t.Nonempty`.
      Its endpoint is the OS45 quarter-turn scalar point.  The
      resulting `╬│` starts in `Wseed`,
      ends at `sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm (realEmbed y0))`, and stays in
      the adjacent double scalar domain.  This theorem is source geometry and
      scalarization only; it must not use local EOW, real-edge adjacent
      equality, global PET branch independence, or final `bvt_W` locality.

      Lean-shaped pseudocode:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq
          ... := by
        classical
        let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
        let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
        let y0 := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
        -- Use the strengthened seed theorem in its implementation form,
        -- retaining the source chart, `zreg`, and `Gseed` provenance.
        rcases BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_with_sourceProvenance
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_open hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hV_adjLift_ET hx0V hRep with
          Γƒ¿V0, Usrc, zreg, Wseed, Gseed,
            hV0_open, hx0V0, hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn,
            hwick_mem, hzregUsrc, hGseed_def, hW_rel, hW_conn, hW_ne,
            hGseedW, hW_double, hW_eq, hUsrc_doubleΓƒ⌐
        let zwick0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé :=
          fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)
        have hzwickUsrc : zwick0 Γêê Usrc :=
          hwick_mem x0 hx0V0
        subst Gseed
        have hUsrc_path : IsPathConnected Usrc :=
          (IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected hUsrc_open).mp hUsrc_conn
        have hjoin : JoinedIn Usrc zreg zwick0 :=
          hUsrc_path.joinedIn zreg hzregUsrc zwick0 hzwickUsrc
        let ╬│src : Path zreg zwick0 := hjoin.somePath
        have h╬│src_mem : ΓêÇ t, ╬│src t Γêê Usrc := hjoin.somePath_mem
        let ╬│seed : Path
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg)
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zwick0) :=
          (╬│src.map (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous)
        have h╬│seed_double :
            ΓêÇ t, ╬│seed t Γêê
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä := by
          intro t
          change BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬│src t) Γêê
            BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä
          exact hUsrc_double (╬│src t) (h╬│src_mem t)
        rcases hfig_x0 with
          Γƒ¿╬ô, ╬ö, h╬ô_cont, h╬ö_cont, h╬ô0, h╬ô1, h╬ô_ET, h╬ö_ET, h╬ö_gramΓƒ⌐
        let ╬│fig : Path
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zwick0)
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0))) :=
          { toFun := fun t => BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ô t)
            source' := by simpa [zwick0] using congrArg
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n) h╬ô0
            target' := by simpa [y0] using congrArg
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n) h╬ô1
            continuous_toFun := (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous.comp h╬ô_cont }
        have h╬│fig_double :
            ΓêÇ t, ╬│fig t Γêê
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä := by
          intro t
          refine
            (BHW.mem_sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_iff_exists_realizations
              (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä (╬│fig t)).2 ?_
          exact Γƒ¿╬ô t, ╬ö t, h╬ô_ET t, h╬ö_ET t, rfl, h╬ö_gram tΓƒ⌐
        let ╬│path : Path
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg)
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0))) :=
          ╬│seed.trans ╬│fig
        have h╬│path_double :
            ΓêÇ t, ╬│path t Γêê
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä := by
          intro t
          have ht :
              ╬│path t Γêê Set.range ╬│seed Γê¬ Set.range ╬│fig := by
            have hmem :
                ╬│path t Γêê Set.range (╬│seed.trans ╬│fig) := by
              exact Γƒ¿t, rflΓƒ⌐
            simpa [╬│path, Path.trans_range ╬│seed ╬│fig] using hmem
          rcases ht with Γƒ¿u, rflΓƒ⌐ | Γƒ¿u, rflΓƒ⌐
          ┬╖ exact h╬│seed_double u
          ┬╖ exact h╬│fig_double u
        exact Γƒ¿Wseed, ╬│path, hW_rel, hW_conn, hW_ne,
          hW_double, hW_eq, ╬│path.continuous,
          by simpa [╬│path] using hGseedW,
          by simp [╬│path, ╬│fig, y0],
          h╬│path_doubleΓƒ⌐
      ```

      The provenance-strengthened helper
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_with_sourceProvenance` is not a
      separate mathematical theorem from
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq`; it is the
      implementation form of the same proof, keeping the source chart and
      regular source point instead of throwing them away before the path step.
      It must carry the Wick-membership field `hwick_mem` (or the specialized
      proof `zwick0 Γêê Usrc`) forward to the path theorem; no later helper may
      reconstruct that membership from only the scalar seed.

      The corridor uses two finite-dimensional topology support theorems.
      They are not QFT theorems: they are local connectedness of the source
      complex Gram variety and the compact-path tube construction derived from
      it.

      The singular-rank branch of the local-basis theorem needs one additional
      rank-`<=` cone input, distinct from the already checked rank-exact cone
      theorem in `SourceComplexConeTransport.lean`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.matrixSymmetricRankLECone_small_connected
          {q : Type*} [Fintype q] [DecidableEq q]
          (r : Nat)
          {N : Set (Matrix q q Γäé)}
          (hN_open : IsOpen N)
          (h0N : (0 : Matrix q q Γäé) Γêê N) :
          Γêâ C : Set (Matrix q q Γäé),
            (0 : Matrix q q Γäé) Γêê C Γêº
            IsOpen C Γêº
            C Γèå N Γêº
            IsConnected
              (C Γê⌐ {S : Matrix q q Γäé | Sß╡Ç = S Γêº S.rank Γëñ r})

      theorem BHW.isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image
          (n D : Nat) {r q : Type*} [Fintype r] [Fintype q]
          [DecidableEq r] [DecidableEq q]
          (e : Fin n Γëâ r Γèò q)
          {Aset : Set (Matrix r r Γäé)}
          {Bset : Set (Matrix r q Γäé)}
          {Sset : Set (Matrix q q Γäé)}
          (hA_conn : IsConnected Aset)
          (hB_conn : IsConnected Bset)
          (hS_conn :
            IsConnected
              (Sset Γê⌐ {S : Matrix q q Γäé |
                Sß╡Ç = S Γêº S.rank Γëñ D - Fintype.card r}))
          (hA_unit : ΓêÇ A, A Γêê Aset -> IsUnit A.det) :
          IsConnected
            ((fun p : Matrix r r Γäé ├ù Matrix r q Γäé ├ù Matrix q q Γäé =>
                BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph n e p.1 p.2.1 p.2.2) ''
              {p | p.1 Γêê Aset Γêº p.2.1 Γêê Bset Γêº
                p.2.2 Γêê Sset Γê⌐ {S : Matrix q q Γäé |
                  Sß╡Ç = S Γêº S.rank Γëñ D - Fintype.card r}})

      theorem BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_openCoordinatePatch
          (n D : Nat) {r q : Type*} [Fintype r] [Fintype q]
          [DecidableEq r] [DecidableEq q]
          (e : Fin n Γëâ r Γèò q)
          {N0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          {Aset : Set (Matrix r r Γäé)}
          {Bset : Set (Matrix r q Γäé)}
          {Sset : Set (Matrix q q Γäé)}
          (hA_unit : ΓêÇ A, A Γêê Aset -> IsUnit A.det)
          (hrD : Fintype.card r <= D)
          (hgraph_N0 :
            ((fun p : Matrix r r Γäé ├ù Matrix r q Γäé ├ù Matrix q q Γäé =>
                BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph n e p.1 p.2.1 p.2.2) ''
              {p | p.1 Γêê Aset Γê⌐ {A : Matrix r r Γäé | Aß╡Ç = A} Γêº
                p.2.1 Γêê Bset Γêº
                p.2.2 Γêê Sset Γê⌐ {S : Matrix q q Γäé |
                  Sß╡Ç = S Γêº S.rank Γëñ D - Fintype.card r}}) Γèå N0) :
          ({Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé |
              Z Γêê N0 Γêº
              IsUnit
                ((((Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j).reindex e e).toBlocksΓéüΓéü).det) Γêº
              (((Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j).reindex e e).toBlocksΓéüΓéü) Γêê
                Aset Γêº
              (((Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j).reindex e e).toBlocksΓéüΓéé) Γêê
                Bset Γêº
              BHW.reindexedRectSchurComplement
                (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j) e e Γêê Sset} Γê⌐
              BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) =
            ((fun p : Matrix r r Γäé ├ù Matrix r q Γäé ├ù Matrix q q Γäé =>
                BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph n e p.1 p.2.1 p.2.2) ''
              {p | p.1 Γêê Aset Γê⌐ {A : Matrix r r Γäé | Aß╡Ç = A} Γêº
                p.2.1 Γêê Bset Γêº
                p.2.2 Γêê Sset Γê⌐ {S : Matrix q q Γäé |
                  Sß╡Ç = S Γêº S.rank Γëñ D - Fintype.card r}})
      ```

      Proof transcript: for
      `matrixSymmetricRankLECone_small_connected`, choose a centered ball
      `C := Metric.ball 0 ╬╡ Γèå N`.  The set
      `C Γê⌐ {S | Sß╡Ç = S Γêº S.rank Γëñ r}` is star-shaped at `0`: for
      `0 Γëñ t Γëñ 1`, the path `S_t := ((1 - t : Γä¥) : Γäé) ΓÇó S` stays in the
      centered ball by `norm_smul` and `1 - t Γëñ 1`, stays symmetric by
      transpose-linearity, and stays rank-`<= r` because nonzero scalar
      multiplication preserves rank while the endpoint has rank `0`.  Thus
      every point is joined to `0`, and connectedness follows by joining
      through `0`.  The transported arbitrary-index version is obtained from
      the `Fin (Fintype.card q)` proof by `Matrix.reindex`, exactly as in the
      checked rank-exact transport theorem.  The two Schur-graph rank-`<=`
      lemmas are the rank-`<=` analogues of the checked rank-exact image and
      coordinate-patch lemmas, using
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_mem_rankLE_iff` in place of
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_mem_rankExact_iff`.

      Lean status (2026-04-30): this rank-`<=` support is checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexCone.lean`,
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexConeTransport.lean`, and
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexSchurGraph.lean`.  The checked names are
      `BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLECone_small_connected`,
      `BHW.matrixSymmetricRankLECone_small_connected`,
      `BHW.isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image`,
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_coordinatePatch`, and
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_openCoordinatePatch`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis
          (d n : Nat)
          {Z0 : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé}
          (hZ0 : Z0 Γêê BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          {N0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hZ0N0 : Z0 Γêê N0) :
          Γêâ V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            Z0 Γêê V Γêº
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n V Γêº
            IsConnected V Γêº
            V Γèå N0 Γê⌐ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n
      ```

      Proof transcript: if `n <= d + 1`, use
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace_of_le` and
      the full symmetric-coordinate equivalence to choose a small connected
      coordinate ball inside `N0`.  If `d + 1 < n`, write
      `D := d + 1` and split by the rank of `Z0`.  At rank `D`, use the
      checked complex-regular realization theorem and
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localConnectedRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular`
      to get a connected relatively open image inside `N0`.  At lower rank,
      use the checked principal Schur product chart around `Z0`: the
      determinant-unit principal block is shrunk to a connected matrix ball,
      the rectangular block to a connected ball, and the Schur-complement
      factor to a small ball intersected with the symmetric rank-`<= D-k`
      cone.  Its connectedness is exactly
      `BHW.matrixSymmetricRankLECone_small_connected`; the connected graph
      image is supplied by
      `BHW.isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image`, and its
      identification with the relatively open coordinate patch is
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_openCoordinatePatch`.
      The graph map is continuous, the patch is relatively open in
      `sourceComplexGramVariety d n` after rewriting
      `sourceComplexGramVariety d n` by
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le`, and the product image is
      connected and contained in `N0`.

      Lean status (2026-04-30): this local connected basis is checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexDensity.lean` as
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis`, with strict
      branch
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis_strict`,
      regular branch
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis_regular`, and
      singular branch
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis_singular`
      checked using the rank-`<=` Schur tube.  The older
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_rankExact_connected_basis` remains a
      separate rank-exact-regular-locus input for the identity-principle layer;
      it is not the lower-rank connected source-variety tube.

      The compact-path tube is implemented through connected components in the
      relatively open source variety, not through an ad hoc finite-chain
      subdivision.  First add the relative-open union and component lemmas:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.iUnion
          (d n : Nat)
          {╬╣ : Type*}
          {U : ╬╣ -> Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hU : ΓêÇ i, BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n (U i)) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n (Γïâ i, U i)

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedComponentIn_relOpen
          (d n : Nat)
          {D : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hD_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n D)
          {Z0 : Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé}
          (hZ0D : Z0 Γêê D) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
            (connectedComponentIn D Z0)
      ```

      Proof transcript for the component lemma: write
      `D = D0 Γê⌐ sourceComplexGramVariety d n`, with `D0` open, and set
      `C := connectedComponentIn D Z0`.  For each `Y : C`, the inclusion
      `C Γèå D` gives `Y Γêê D0` and
      `Y Γêê sourceComplexGramVariety d n`.  Apply
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis d n` to `Y`
      inside the ambient open set `D0`, obtaining a connected relatively open
      neighborhood `VY` with `Y Γêê VY` and
      `VY Γèå D0 Γê⌐ sourceComplexGramVariety d n = D`.  Since `VY` is
      preconnected, contains `Y`, and is contained in `D`,
      `IsPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn` gives
      `VY Γèå connectedComponentIn D Y`; because `Y Γêê C`,
      `connectedComponentIn_eq` identifies this component with `C`.  Hence
      `VY Γèå C`.  Conversely every point of `C` lies in its own `VY`, so
      `C = Γïâ Y : C, VY`.  The preceding relative-open `iUnion` lemma makes
      this union relatively open in `sourceComplexGramVariety d n`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedRelOpenTube_around_compactPath
          (d n : Nat)
          {D : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hD_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n D)
          {╬│ : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé}
          (h╬│_cont : Continuous ╬│)
          (h╬│D : ΓêÇ t, ╬│ t Γêê D)
          {W0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)}
          (hW0_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n W0)
          (hW0_conn : IsConnected W0)
          (hW0_nonempty : W0.Nonempty)
          (hW0D : W0 Γèå D)
          (hstart : ╬│ (0 : unitInterval) Γêê W0) :
          Γêâ Wtube : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wtube Γêº
            IsConnected Wtube Γêº
            W0 Γèå Wtube Γêº
            Wtube Γèå D Γêº
            (ΓêÇ t, ╬│ t Γêê Wtube)
      ```

      Proof transcript for the compact-path tube theorem: let
      `Zstart := ╬│ (0 : unitInterval)` and
      `Wtube := connectedComponentIn D Zstart`.  Relative openness is exactly
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedComponentIn_relOpen d n hD_rel
      (h╬│D 0)`.  Connectedness is
      `isConnected_connectedComponentIn_iff.mpr (h╬│D 0)`, and containment in
      `D` is `connectedComponentIn_subset D Zstart`.  Since `W0` is connected,
      contains `Zstart`, and is contained in `D`,
      `hW0_conn.isPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn hstart hW0D`
      proves `W0 Γèå Wtube`.  Finally, the image `Set.range ╬│` is preconnected
      by continuity of `╬│` and connectedness of `unitInterval`; it contains
      `Zstart` and is contained in `D` by `h╬│D`, so another application of
      `IsPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn` gives
      `ΓêÇ t, ╬│ t Γêê Wtube`.  The theorem name retains the compact-path
      wording for downstream route stability, but the proof only needs
      connectedness of `unitInterval`.

      Lean status (2026-04-30): the relative-open union lemma
      `BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.iUnion`, the component theorem
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedComponentIn_relOpen`, and the
      compact-path tube theorem
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedRelOpenTube_around_compactPath`
      are checked in `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexDensity.lean`.  They use the
      checked local connected basis above and do not introduce any BHW/PET
      branch-independence, local EOW, or final-`bvt_W` shortcut.

      The BHW corridor theorem is the source form of the cited
      Bargmann-Hall-Wightman step in OS I ┬º4.5:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentQuarterTurnScalarCorridor_of_figure24
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V -> x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hV_horiz_swap :
            ΓêÇ x, x Γêê V ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Γêê V)
          (Wseed : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé))
          (hWseed_relOpen :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wseed)
          (hWseed_connected : IsConnected Wseed)
          (hWseed_nonempty : Wseed.Nonempty)
          (hWseed_double :
            Wseed Γèå
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hDouble_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
              (BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐)))
          (╬│ : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé)
          (h╬│_cont : Continuous ╬│)
          (h╬│_start : ╬│ (0 : unitInterval) Γêê Wseed)
          (h╬│_end :
            ╬│ (1 : unitInterval) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                ((BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
                  (BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                      (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0))))
          (h╬│_double :
            ΓêÇ t,
              ╬│ t Γêê
                BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                  (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐)) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
          Γêâ (Wscal : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé))
            (Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)),
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wscal Γêº
              IsConnected Wscal Γêº
              Wseed Γèå Wscal Γêº
              IsOpen Uy Γêº
              BHW.realEmbed y0 Γêê Uy Γêº
              Uy Γèå BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä Γê⌐
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä Γêº
              (ΓêÇ z, z Γêê Uy ->
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z) Γêê Wscal) Γêº
              Wscal Γèå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä
      ```

      Proof transcript:

      1. Put `╧ä := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐`,
         `Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)`, and
         `y0 := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0`.
         The adjacent ACR edge membership is obtained from the swapped ordered
         branch, not from the identity ordered hypothesis alone:

         ```lean
         have hy_acr_swapped :
           BHW.realEmbed
             (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä
               (fun k => x0 (╧ä k))) Γêê
             BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä :=
           BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered
             (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä (hV_swap_ordered x0 hx0V)

         have hedge_eq :
           BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä
             (fun k => x0 (╧ä k)) = y0 := by
           simpa [╧ä, y0] using
             BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq
               (d := d) (n := n) i hi 1 x0
         ```

         Rewriting `hy_acr_swapped` by `hedge_eq` gives
         `BHW.realEmbed y0 Γêê BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain ... ╧ä`.  The pulled BHW
         edge membership is exactly `hV_horiz_swap x0 hx0V`.  The fields
         `hV_open`, `hV_jost`, `hV_ordered`, and `hV_swap_ordered` remain on
         the theorem surface because the upstream Figure-2-4 path supplier
         consumes the same source patch; after the path `╬│` is explicitly
         supplied, this assembly theorem only uses the adjacent ACR and pulled
         BHW edge memberships.
      2. Consume the path `╬│` produced by
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq`.
         Its endpoint is the scalar point

         ```lean
         BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
           (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0))
         ```

         and its image lies in `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä`.
         This path is the local BHW/Jost corridor input; it is not a
         consequence of `SourceScalarRepresentativeData` alone and it is not
         a theorem about an arbitrary subset of the double domain.
      3. The path in step 2 is the only OS-paper geometric input.  Thicken it
         inside the relatively open double scalar domain by
         `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedRelOpenTube_around_compactPath`,
         with
         `D := sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä` and `hD_rel :=
         hDouble_rel`.  In the downstream one-branch source input,
         `hDouble_rel` is supplied by
         `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`
         from `hRep.U_relOpen` and `hRep.U_eq`.
         The output tube, enlarged to include `Wseed`, is the connected
         relatively open set `Wscal`.
      4. From the tube theorem, obtain `Wtube` with relative openness,
         connectedness, `Wseed Γèå Wtube`, `Wtube Γèå D`, and
         `ΓêÇ t, ╬│ t Γêê Wtube`.  The endpoint identity `h╬│_end` gives
         `Zend := sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0)) Γêê
         Wtube`.  Write `Wtube = O Γê⌐ sourceComplexGramVariety d n`, with `O`
         open, and let

         ```lean
         scalarMap z := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z)
         Uy :=
           scalarMap Γü╗┬╣' O Γê⌐
           BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä Γê⌐
           BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä
         ```

         The map `scalarMap` is continuous by
         `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n` and `Q.symm.continuous`, so
         `Uy` is open by the checked branch-domain openness theorems.  The
         base point belongs to `Uy` by `Zend Γêê O`, the adjacent ACR edge
         membership above, and `hV_horiz_swap x0 hx0V`.  For `z Γêê Uy`,
         `scalarMap z Γêê O`; also
         `scalarMap z Γêê sourceComplexGramVariety d n` by the witness
         `Γƒ¿Q.symm z, rflΓƒ⌐`.  Hence `scalarMap z Γêê Wtube`.  Set
         `Wscal := Wtube`; the double-domain field is the tube containment
         `Wtube Γèå D`.

      This theorem is external BHW/Jost source content on the OS ┬º4.5 route.
      It must not use `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`, the downstream
      real-edge equality theorem, global PET branch independence, or final
      `bvt_W` locality.

      Lean status (2026-04-30): the assembly theorem
      `BHW.os45AdjacentQuarterTurnScalarCorridor_of_figure24` is checked in
      `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45TraceMembership.lean`.  Its hypotheses still
      include the Figure-2-4 scalar path `╬│`; the theorem itself performs only
      the connected source-tube thickening and OS45 branch-domain pullback
      described above.

      Finally propagate the seed through the connected scalar corridor:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentScalarEq_on_quarterTurnCorridor
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          {F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé}
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData (d := d) n F)
          (Wseed Wscal : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé))
          (hWscal_relOpen :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wscal)
          (hWscal_connected : IsConnected Wscal)
          (hWseed_relOpen :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wseed)
          (hWseed_nonempty : Wseed.Nonempty)
          (hWseed_subset : Wseed Γèå Wscal)
          (hWscal_double :
            Wscal Γèå
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐))
          (hSeed :
            let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
            Set.EqOn
              (fun Z =>
                hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z))
              hRep.Phi
              Wseed) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          Set.EqOn
            (fun Z =>
              hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z))
            hRep.Phi
            Wscal
      ```

      Proof transcript:

      1. Put `╧ä := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐`,
         `╬ª Z := hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z)`, and
         `╬¿ := hRep.Phi`.  This theorem is deliberately generic in the
         represented holomorphic function `F`; the OS-specific object
         `bvt_F OS lgc n` enters only when constructing the
         `SourceScalarRepresentativeData` packet.  This avoids an
         OS-wrapper theorem whose axiom audit would inherit unrelated
         upstream `bvt_F` obligations.
      2. From `hWscal_double` and `hRep.U_eq`, prove
         `Wscal Γèå hRep.U` and
         `Wscal Γèå {Z | BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z Γêê hRep.U}`.
      3. Restrict `hRep.Phi_holomorphic` to `Wscal` by
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.of_subset_relOpen`, and restrict the
         permutation precomposition by
         `BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram`.
      4. Set `H Z := ╬ª Z - ╬¿ Z` and use
         `BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.sub` to prove
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n H Wscal`.
      5. On `Wseed`, `hSeed` gives `H Z = 0` by `sub_eq_zero.mpr`; use
         `hWseed_subset`.
      6. Apply the checked germ-API theorem
         `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle` in
         `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexDensity.lean`, with
         `U := Wscal`, `W := Wseed`, `H := H`, `hWscal_relOpen`,
         `hWscal_connected`, `hWseed_relOpen`, `hWseed_nonempty`,
         `hWseed_subset`, `hH_holo`, and the zero result from step 5.
      7. Convert `Set.EqOn H 0 Wscal` back to the displayed equality by
         `sub_eq_zero.mp`.

      Lean status (2026-05-01): the generic theorem
      `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarEq_on_quarterTurnCorridor` in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexDensity.lean` has already been ported to
      the germ API.  Its proof builds `h╬¿_holo`, `h╬ª_holo`, and `hH_holo`
      using `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.of_subset_relOpen`,
      `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram`, and
      `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.sub`, then calls
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle`.

      **(C) Pullback assembly.**  Compose the `Set.EqOn` result from
      `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarEq_on_quarterTurnCorridor` with
      `z Γåª sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z)` on `Uy`.  This produces the
      adjacent `hGram` field of
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent`.  No new
      mathematics occurs in this last step.

      The `hGram` production is now entirely inside the active identity and
      adjacent scalar-packet suppliers above.  The assembly theorem
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput` consumes
      `BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket`; it does not manufacture that
      packet from geometry.  The allowed source content for the packet
      producers is only the OS I ┬º4.5 one-branch Hall-Wightman/BHW source
      theorem on the selected Figure-2-4 scalar corridor: construct the
      branch seed
      `z Γåª bvt_F OS lgc n (permAct ╬▓.symm z)` on the appropriate `S'_n`
      ordered/permuted tube side, use OS-II `bvt_F_perm` only to identify that
      seed with the selected symmetric `S'_n` datum there, enlarge that branch
      by the BHW invariant analytic-function theorem, and propagate equality
      through the connected scalar corridor to the OS45 quarter-turn
      neighbourhood.  It must not compare the identity and adjacent pulled BHW
      branches by real-edge locality.

      In particular, a Lean proof of the packet-producing theorem may use:

      - `bvt_F_holomorphic`, `bvt_F_acrOne_package`, `bvt_F_perm`, and
        `bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` for the selected
        OS-II analytic datum;
      - `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn` and the ordinary one-branch BHW
        continuation of that datum;
      - the Figure-2-4 real-environment geometry supplied by
        `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalRealEnvironment` and the
        relabelled source patch;
      - the local Hall-Wightman/BHW scalar corridor and source-variety
        identity principle needed to identify the ACR and pulled BHW
        representatives near the quarter-turn edge.

      It may not use:

      - any `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`;
      - `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarGerm_of_OSII_Figure24`;
      - the real-edge equality theorem
        `BHW.bvt_F_adjacent_extendF_edgeDistribution_eq_of_osEOWDifferenceEnvelope`;
      - global PET branch independence or
        `hallWightman_source_permutedBranch_compatibility_of_distributionalAnchor`;
      - final `bvt_W` locality.

      Therefore the local theorem order is:

      1. prove the pure source-neighborhood geometry from `hU_ordered`,
         `hU_horiz_bhw`, `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered`,
         and openness of `os45ACRBranchDomain ╬▓` and
         `os45PulledRealBranchDomain ╬▓`;
      2. prove the public scalar-packet supplier
         `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id` or
         `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent`;
      3. consume that packet in
         `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput`;
      4. derive `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_sourcePatch` by adding
         the recorded holomorphy fields and restricting the `Set.EqOn`
         equality from `Uy` to the smaller domain requested by the packaging
         theorem;
      5. package `BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt` from that theorem and
         the definitional pulled-branch equality.

      Expanded source-germ proof transcript:

      1. For a fixed branch label `╬▓`, set

         ```lean
         Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
         W╬▓ z := BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)
         A╬▓ z := BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z
         B╬▓ z := BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z
         ```

         The ACR-side domain is `Q.symm z Γêê ForwardTube`; the BHW-side
         domain is `W╬▓ z Γêê ExtendedTube`.
      2. The base point belongs to both branch domains:

         ```lean
         have hy_acr :
           BHW.realEmbed
             (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x0) Γêê
             BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ :=
           BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered
             (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ (hU_ordered x0 hx0U)

         have hy_bhw :
           BHW.realEmbed
             (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x0) Γêê
             BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ :=
           hU_horiz_bhw x0 hx0U
         ```

         Using `BHW.isOpen_os45ACRBranchDomain` and
         `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`, shrink to an open `Uy`
         containing this base point and contained in the intersection of the
         two domains.  This is the pure topology part of the theorem.
      3. `A╬▓` is holomorphic on the ACR-side domain, but not because
         `W╬▓ z` is itself in the forward tube.  On
         `os45ACRBranchDomain ╬▓`, the checked field is
         `Q.symm z Γêê ForwardTube`; for nontrivial `╬▓`, the relabelled point
         `W╬▓ z` can lie outside the ordered forward tube.  The proof must
         first use the total finite-permutation symmetry field
         `bvt_F_perm OS lgc n` to rewrite
         `bvt_F OS lgc n (W╬▓ z)` as `bvt_F OS lgc n (Q.symm z)`, and only
         then use the ordinary holomorphy of `bvt_F` on `ForwardTube`.  This
         is a one-branch OS-II ACR symmetry statement, not an equality of two
         BHW real-edge branches.
      4. `B╬▓` is holomorphic on the pulled BHW domain by the checked
         `BHW.os45PulledRealBranch_holomorphicOn`.
      5. The local common representative for this branch is

         ```lean
         H╬▓ z := BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z)
         ```

         on a small complex neighborhood `Uy` of `BHW.realEmbed y0`.
         On `Uy Γê⌐ os45PulledRealBranchDomain ╬▓`, agreement with `B╬▓` is
         definitional after unfolding `BHW.os45PulledRealBranch`.
      6. On `Uy`, agreement with `A╬▓` is the
         one-branch OS I ┬º4.5/BHW source input.  For `╬▓ = 1`, use
         `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id` and the algebraic
         reduction
         `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_scalarGramEq`.  For
         `╬▓ = ╧ä`, use
         `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent` with the
         pointwise Figure-2-4 path `hfig_x0` coming from
         `hV_figPath_closure`.  That packet is based at the identity common
         edge `y0`, so the adjacent supplier rewrites it to the relabelled
         common edge before calling the generic one-branch source-patch
         assembly, and rewrites the packaged source-germ predicate back to
         `y0` afterward.  This is not the downstream adjacent scalar equality;
         it never compares `H1` with `H╧ä`.
      7. The source theorem must output `Uy` small enough that `W╬▓ z` remains
         in the branch's extended-tube/scalar-product source domain.  The
         membership at `BHW.realEmbed y0` is supplied by `hUfig_source` for the
         chosen Figure-2-4 branch, and openness is supplied by
         `BHW.isOpen_extendedTube`, `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`,
         and continuity of `Q.symm`, `BHW.permAct ╬▓.symm`, and `BHW.realEmbed`.
      8. Package `Uy`, the inclusion `W╬▓ '' Uy Γèå ExtendedTube`, the
         differentiability of the branch-specific `H╬▓`, and the two agreement
         fields as `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt OS lgc n ╬▓ y0`.

      The forbidden move is to replace step 5 by a theorem that the identity
      and adjacent BHW branches are already equal, or by using a scalar
      equality produced from `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`.  The branch
      source predicate is upstream of the branch-difference EOW argument; the
      adjacent scalar equality below is downstream of it.

      Downstream scalar-germ consequence:

      After `BHW.os45_adjacent_commonBoundaryEnvelope` has been proved from
      the branchwise packets and the checked local EOW theorem, one may derive
      a local Hall-Wightman scalar equality.  This theorem is useful as a
      source-side audit/export of the real-edge equality, but it is not an
      input to `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt`, `os45BranchHorizontalCommonGerm`,
      or `os45_adjacent_commonBoundaryEnvelope`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentScalarGerm_of_OSII_Figure24
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Γäò) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_nonempty : V.Nonempty)
          (hV_jost : ΓêÇ x Γêê V, x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ΓêÇ x Γêê V, BHW.realEmbed x Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                  BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (E :
            BHW.AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope
              (d := d) OS lgc n
              (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐) V)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) :
          let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
          Γêâ Wscal : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Γäé),
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wscal Γêº
            Wscal.Nonempty Γêº
            Wscal Γèå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╧ä Γêº
            Set.EqOn
              (fun Z =>
                hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z))
              hRep.Phi Wscal
      ```

      Proof transcript: choose a regular `x0 Γêê V`; construct `Oenv` by
      `sourceRealGramMap_realEnvironmentAt_of_regular`; use `E` and
      `bvt_F_adjacent_extendF_edgeDistribution_eq_of_osEOWDifferenceEnvelope`
      to obtain compact real-edge equality on `V`; convert compact equality
      to pointwise equality on `V` by the checked compact-support
      DuBois-Reymond/continuity argument used in
      `sourceAnchor_compactBranchEq_pointwise_on_realPatch`; convert that
      pointwise equality to scalar equality on `Oenv` by `hRep.branch_eq` and
      `sourceRealGramComplexify_perm`; then apply
      `sourceVariety_localChart_totallyReal_zero` to the holomorphic
      difference on the double domain.  The double-domain relative openness is
      derived inside the proof from `hRep.U_relOpen`, `hRep.U_eq`, and
      `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`.
      This theorem is the required Wick-to-real-to-source germ.  It is not a
      shortcut around local EOW, and it is not a boundary-functional demotion:
      the only real-edge equality input is the concrete
      `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope` produced by the OS45 local EOW
      construction.

      Lean proof skeleton:

      1. Put `╧ä := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐` and
         `F := bvt_F OS lgc n`.  Derive `hF_holo` from
         `bvt_F_holomorphic` and `hF_lorentz` from
         `bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube`.
      2. Get compact real-edge equality on `V`:

         ```lean
         hcompact :
           ΓêÇ ╧å : SchwartzNPoint d n,
             HasCompactSupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) ->
             tsupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γèå V ->
             Γê½ x, BHW.extendF F (BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (╧ä k))) * ╧å x =
             Γê½ x, BHW.extendF F (BHW.realEmbed x) * ╧å x
         ```

         by `BHW.bvt_F_adjacent_extendF_edgeDistribution_eq_of_osEOWDifferenceEnvelope`.
      3. Convert `hcompact` to

         ```lean
         hreal :
           ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
             BHW.extendF F (BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (╧ä k))) =
             BHW.extendF F (BHW.realEmbed x)
         ```

         using `SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn`.
         The two continuity hypotheses are exactly the `extendF`-continuity
         proof already checked inside
         `BHW.sourceAnchor_compactBranchEq_pointwise_on_realPatch`, with
         `hV_ET` and `hV_swapET` supplying the two `ExtendedTube` inclusions.
      4. Choose a regular real seed:

         ```lean
         rcases (BHW.dense_sourceGramRegularAt d n).exists_mem_open
             hV_open hV_nonempty with Γƒ¿x0, hx0reg, hx0VΓƒ⌐
         ```

         and build `Oenv`:

         ```lean
         rcases BHW.sourceRealGramMap_realEnvironmentAt_of_regular
             (d := d) n hx0reg (hV_jost x0 hx0V) V hV_open hx0V with
           Γƒ¿Oenv, hOenv_sub, hOenvΓƒ⌐
         ```

      5. Prove `sourceRealGramComplexify n G Γêê D╧ä` for every `G Γêê Oenv` by
         realizing `G` as `sourceRealMinkowskiGram d n x` with `x Γêê V`, then
         using `hV_ET`, `hV_swapET`, `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_realEmbed`,
         `BHW.sourceRealGramComplexify_perm`, and
         `BHW.mem_sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_iff_exists_realizations`.
      6. Define `Hscal Z := hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä Z) -
         hRep.Phi Z`.  Prove
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Hscal D╧ä` from
         `hRep.Phi_holomorphic`, the permutation precomposition theorem, and
         subtraction.
      7. For each `G Γêê Oenv`, choose `x Γêê V` realizing it.  Use `hreal x hx`
         and the two `hRep.branch_eq` rewrites to prove
         `Hscal (sourceRealGramComplexify n G) = 0`.  The right-hand branch
         rewrite uses `sourceRealGramComplexify_perm`; no Euclidean value or
         `bvt_W` term appears here.
      8. Apply `BHW.sourceVariety_localChart_totallyReal_zero` with `hOenv`,
         the relative openness of `D╧ä`, the membership result from step 5, and
         the zero result from step 7.  It returns the desired `Wscal`; convert
         `Set.EqOn Hscal 0 Wscal` to the displayed equality by
         `sub_eq_zero.mp`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalCommonGerm
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Γäò) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (E : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hE_precompact : IsCompact (closure E))
          (hsource :
            ΓêÇ y Γêê closure E,
              BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
                (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ y) :
          Γêâ (U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé))
            (H╬▓ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé),
            IsOpen U Γêº
            BHW.realEmbed '' closure E Γèå U Γêº
            DifferentiableOn Γäé H╬▓ U Γêº
            (ΓêÇ z Γêê U,
              z Γêê BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ ->
                H╬▓ z =
                  BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ z Γêê U,
              z Γêê BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ ->
                H╬▓ z =
                  BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
      ```

      This theorem is the precise compact-gluing consumer for the one-branch
      OS I ┬º4.5 / BHW input.  It is not an adjacent-branch equality theorem.
      The non-topological source content is exactly `hsource`; the rest of
      the proof only transports compactness of `closure E` and chooses a
      finite cover on which the same branch-specific canonical germ is valid.
      Jost/order/extended-tube fields live upstream: they are used to build
      `hsource`, `hlocal`, and the selected edge `E`, not to glue the already
      available germs.
      Precompactness of `E` is what lets the final neighborhood be chosen
      around all of `closure E`.

      Proof transcript for `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalCommonGerm`:

      1. Define the canonical branch formula

         ```lean
         Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
         W╬▓ z := BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)
         H╬▓ z :=
           BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z)
         ```

         The compact common-germ theorem must use this single formula for the
         fixed branch label `╬▓`, not glue arbitrary existential functions.  It
         also must not use the unpermuted identity-branch formula for every
         branch.
      2. At each real edge point `y Γêê closure E`, apply `hsource y hy`.  This
         gives an open neighborhood `Uy` of `BHW.realEmbed y` on which
         `W╬▓ z Γêê ExtendedTube`, `H╬▓` is holomorphic, and `H╬▓` agrees with the
         ACR representative on the ACR branch domain and with the pulled BHW
         representative on the pulled branch domain.
      3. The neighborhoods `Uy` cover the compact set
         `BHW.realEmbed '' closure E`; compactness follows from
         `hE_precompact` and continuity of `BHW.realEmbed`.  Choose a finite
         subcover `UyΓéü, ..., Uy_m` and set `U = Γïâ j, Uy_j`.
      4. `U` is open and contains `BHW.realEmbed '' closure E`.  The function
         `H╬▓` is differentiable on `U` because every point of `U` lies in one
         selected `Uy_j`, and the source predicate gives differentiability of
         the same canonical formula on that open neighborhood.  The agreement
         fields on `U Γê⌐ os45ACRBranchDomain ╬▓` and
         `U Γê⌐ os45PulledRealBranchDomain ╬▓` are proved by choosing a covering
         index `j` for the given point and applying the corresponding
         `hsource` fields.  No identity theorem is needed at this compact
         gluing stage.
      5. Restricting `H╬▓` to the real slice gives a smooth function on the real
         open set `Ureal := {y | BHW.realEmbed y Γêê U}`.  Since `closure E` is
         compact and contained in `Ureal`, the cutoff lemma can choose a
         Schwartz cutoff supported in `Ureal` and equal to one on `closure E`.

      Exact branch-boundary theorem derived from the common germ:

      The only new cutoff infrastructure needed for this derivation is the
      finite-dimensional smooth Urysohn lemma in Schwartz form.  The currently
      checked theorem
      `SCV.exists_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open` is the
      support-in-open projection of this construction; the branch-BV route
      exposes the compact-support field as the following pure real-analysis
      theorem, using the same proof:

      ```lean
      theorem SCV.exists_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport
          {m : Γäò} {K U : Set (Fin m -> Γä¥)}
          (hK : IsCompact K) (hU : IsOpen U) (hKU : K Γèå U) :
          Γêâ ╧ç : SchwartzMap (Fin m -> Γä¥) Γäé,
            HasCompactSupport (╧ç : (Fin m -> Γä¥) -> Γäé) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x Γêê K, ╧ç x = 1) Γêº
            tsupport (╧ç : (Fin m -> Γä¥) -> Γäé) Γèå U
      ```

      This is pure real analysis: the existing proof already constructs the
      cutoff from a compactly supported smooth function before coercing it to
      a `SchwartzMap`.  It is not a theorem-2 shortcut; it only turns the
      regular one-branch real-slice value into a global Schwartz CLM after the
      source common germ has already been proved.

      The OS45 branch theorem also needs the cutoff on
      `NPointDomain d n`, so the flat cutoff theorem is transported through
      the same real-coordinate equivalence:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_nPoint_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport
          {d n : Γäò} {K U : Set (NPointDomain d n)}
          (hK : IsCompact K) (hU : IsOpen U) (hKU : K Γèå U) :
          Γêâ ╧ç : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé,
            HasCompactSupport (╧ç : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ x Γêê K, ╧ç x = 1) Γêº
            tsupport (╧ç : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γèå U
      ```

      Proof transcript: set `e := flattenCLEquivReal n (d + 1)`,
      `Kflat := e '' K`, and `Uflat := e '' U`.  The homeomorphism
      `e.toHomeomorph` sends compact sets to compact sets and open sets to
      open sets, and `hKU` gives `Kflat Γèå Uflat`.  Apply
      `SCV.exists_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport`
      on the flat sets, then pull the cutoff back by
      `SchwartzMap.compCLMOfContinuousLinearEquiv Γäé e`.  Compact support and
      topological-support containment are transported by the homeomorphism,
      and the equality-on-`K` field is the pointwise identity
      `╧ç x = ╧çflat (e x) = 1`.

      The checked closed-ball integration CLM
      `SCV.exists_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn` is stated on flat
      coordinate spaces `Fin m -> Γä¥`.  The OS45 branch theorem works on
      `NPointDomain d n = Fin n -> SpacetimeDim d`, so the implementation
      first proves the following flattening adapter before the branch theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_nPoint_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn
          {d n : Γäò} {R : Γä¥} {g : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé}
          (hg_cont :
            ContinuousOn g (Metric.closedBall (0 : NPointDomain d n) R)) :
          Γêâ T : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé ->L[Γäé] Γäé,
            ΓêÇ ╧ê : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé,
              T ╧ê =
                Γê½ x in Metric.closedBall (0 : NPointDomain d n) R,
                  g x * ╧ê x
      ```

      Proof transcript: set `e := flattenCLEquivReal n (d + 1)`.  The norm
      identity `flattenCLEquivReal_norm_eq` identifies
      `e '' Metric.closedBall 0 R` with the flat closed ball.  Apply
      `SCV.exists_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn` to
      `gflat u := g (e.symm u)`, with continuity transported through
      `e.symm`.  Pull Schwartz tests forward by
      `SchwartzMap.compCLMOfContinuousLinearEquiv Γäé e.symm`, define
      `T ╧ê := Tflat (SchwartzMap.compCLMOfContinuousLinearEquiv Γäé e.symm ╧ê)`,
      and rewrite the restricted integral by
      `integral_flattenCLEquivReal` applied to the closed-ball indicator.
      This adapter is representation bookkeeping, not a new analytic input.

      The real-analysis consumer of the common germ is isolated before the
      OS-specific branch theorem is implemented:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45RegularBoundaryValue_from_commonGerm
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Γäò) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (E C U : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (H╬▓ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé) -> Γäé)
          (hE_precompact : IsCompact (closure E))
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hU_real :
            BHW.realEmbed '' closure E Γèå U)
          (hH_cont :
            ContinuousOn H╬▓ U)
          (hA_eq :
            ΓêÇ z Γêê U,
              z Γêê BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ ->
                H╬▓ z =
                  BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
          (hB_eq :
            ΓêÇ z Γêê U,
              z Γêê BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ ->
                H╬▓ z =
                  BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
          (hlocal :
            ΓêÇ K : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K -> K Γèå E ->
              ΓêÇ K╬╖ : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬╖ -> K╬╖ Γèå C ->
                Γêâ r : Γä¥, 0 < r Γêº
                  ΓêÇ y Γêê K, ΓêÇ ╬╖ Γêê K╬╖, ΓêÇ ╬╡ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬╡ -> ╬╡ < r ->
                    BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ ╬╡ Γêê
                      BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Γêº
                    BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ (-╬╡) Γêê
                      BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓) :
          Γêâ T : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé ->L[Γäé] Γäé,
            (ΓêÇ K╬╖ : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬╖ -> K╬╖ Γèå C ->
              ΓêÇ ╧å : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé,
                HasCompactSupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) ->
                tsupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γèå E ->
                TendstoUniformlyOn
                  (fun (╬╡ : Γä¥) ╬╖ =>
                    Γê½ y : NPointDomain d n,
                      BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative
                        (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
                        (BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ ╬╡) *
                      ╧å y)
                  (fun _ : NPointDomain d n => T ╧å)
                  (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0)) K╬╖) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ K╬╖ : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬╖ -> K╬╖ Γèå C ->
              ΓêÇ ╧å : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé,
                HasCompactSupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) ->
                tsupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γèå E ->
                TendstoUniformlyOn
                  (fun (╬╡ : Γä¥) ╬╖ =>
                    Γê½ y : NPointDomain d n,
                      BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative
                        (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
                        (BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ (-╬╡)) *
                      ╧å y)
                  (fun _ : NPointDomain d n => T ╧å)
                  (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0)) K╬╖)
      ```

      Proof transcript:

      1. Let `Ureal = {y | BHW.realEmbed y Γêê U}`.  It is open by continuity
         of `BHW.realEmbed`, and `closure E Γèå Ureal` by `hU_real`.
      2. Choose `╧ç : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé` with compact support,
         `╧ç = 1` on `closure E`, and `tsupport ╧ç Γèå Ureal` using
         `BHW.exists_nPoint_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport`.
      3. Choose `R╧ç > 0` with
         `tsupport (╧ç : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γèå Metric.closedBall 0 R╧ç`,
         by boundedness of the compact support.  Define the continuous
         coefficient on that closed ball

         ```lean
         g y := ╧ç y * H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)
         ```

         and construct the CLM by the flattened real-analysis adapter
         `BHW.exists_nPoint_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn`:

         ```lean
         T ╧å =
           Γê½ y in Metric.closedBall (0 : NPointDomain d n) R╧ç,
             g y * ╧å y
         ```

         The continuity hypothesis for `g` is proved locally on the closed
         ball.  At points of `tsupport ╧ç`, `hH_cont` applies because
         `tsupport ╧ç Γèå Ureal`; at points outside `tsupport ╧ç`, the complement
         of `tsupport ╧ç` is open and `╧ç` is identically zero there, so `g` is
         locally zero regardless of the arbitrary total values of `H╬▓` outside
         `U`.  This avoids reproving the `SchwartzMap.mkCLMtoNormedSpace`
         seminorm estimate inside the OS file.
      4. For all `╧å`, the closed-ball formula equals the full-space integral
         `Γê½ y, ╧ç y * H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y) * ╧å y`, because `╧ç` vanishes off
         its topological support and that support is contained in the chosen
         closed ball.  For a test `╧å` with `tsupport ╧å Γèå E`, `╧ç y = 1` on
         `tsupport ╧å`
         because `tsupport ╧å Γèå E Γèå closure E`; hence
         `T ╧å = Γê½ y, H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y) * ╧å y`.

         The shrink that keeps the plus and minus approach points inside the
         common-germ neighborhood `U` is a separate pure-topology lemma, not a
         reuse of the branch-domain local-wedge theorem:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.exists_uniform_realEdgeAddImag_mem_open_of_compact
             {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé)}
             (hU_open : IsOpen U)
             {K K╬╖ : Set (NPointDomain d n)}
             (hK : IsCompact K) (hK╬╖ : IsCompact K╬╖)
             (hK_U : ΓêÇ y Γêê K, BHW.realEmbed y Γêê U) :
             Γêâ r : Γä¥, 0 < r Γêº
               ΓêÇ y Γêê K, ΓêÇ ╬╖ Γêê K╬╖, ΓêÇ ╬╡ : Γä¥, |╬╡| < r ->
                 BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ ╬╡ Γêê U
         ```

         Proof transcript: set
         `S = (K ├ù╦ó K╬╖) ├ù╦ó ({0} : Set Γä¥)` and
         `F ((y,╬╖),╬╡) = BHW.realEdgeAddImag y ╬╖ ╬╡`.  The set `S` is compact,
         `F` is continuous by `BHW.continuous_realEdgeAddImag`, and
         `S Γèå F Γü╗┬╣' U` follows from `BHW.realEdgeAddImag_zero` and `hK_U`.
         Apply `IsCompact.exists_cthickening_subset_open` to the open preimage
         `F Γü╗┬╣' U`.  If `|╬╡| < r`, then `((y,╬╖),╬╡)` lies in the thickening of
         `S` around `((y,╬╖),0)`, so the cthickening inclusion gives the result.
         This proof shape was checked against Lean as the same compact-
         thickening argument used by the implemented local-wedge theorem.
      5. For compact `K╬╖ Γèå C`, let `K = tsupport ╧å`.  It is compact by
         `HasCompactSupport ╧å`, and `K Γèå E` by the support hypothesis.
         Apply `hlocal K K╬╖` to get the branch-domain radius.  Separately
         apply `BHW.exists_uniform_realEdgeAddImag_mem_open_of_compact` to
         `U`, with `hK_U` obtained from `hU_real` and
         `K Γèå E Γèå closure E`, to get a common-germ radius.  Use the minimum
         of the two radii.  The plus point uses `|╬╡| < r`; the minus point
         uses `|-╬╡| = |╬╡| < r`.
      6. On that smaller radius, rewrite the plus integrand by `hA_eq` and
         the minus integrand by `hB_eq`; both become
         `H╬▓ (realEdgeAddImag y ╬╖ (┬▒╬╡)) * ╧å y`.
      7. Uniform continuity of `H╬▓` is used only on the compact set
         `P = {BHW.realEdgeAddImag y ╬╖ t |
              y Γêê K, ╬╖ Γêê K╬╖, |t| Γëñ r}`.  This set is compact by continuity of
         `realEdgeAddImag`, and it is contained in `U` by the uniform-open
         radius above.  Hence `hH_cont.mono` gives `ContinuousOn H╬▓ P`, and
         compactness gives uniform continuity on `P`.  The maps
         `(y,╬╖,t) Γåª realEdgeAddImag y ╬╖ (┬▒t)` converge uniformly to
         `(y,╬╖) Γåª BHW.realEmbed y` as `t -> 0`, either from the explicit
         affine formula or by applying
         `BHW.exists_open_nhds_forall_mem_of_compact_parameter` to the compact
         parameter space `K ├ù K╬╖`.  Combining these two facts gives
         `H╬▓ (realEdgeAddImag y ╬╖ (┬▒╬╡)) ΓåÆ H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)` uniformly in
         `y Γêê K` and `╬╖ Γêê K╬╖`.
         Use compact support of `╧å` only through the facts
         `Function.support ╧å Γèå tsupport ╧å = K` and
         `Integrable (fun y => ΓÇû╧å yΓÇû)`.  For all small `╬╡`, set
         `M╬╡` to the supremum on `K ├ù K╬╖` of
         `ΓÇûH╬▓ (realEdgeAddImag y ╬╖ (┬▒╬╡)) - H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)ΓÇû`.  Then
         for every `╬╖ Γêê K╬╖`,
         `ΓÇûΓê½ y, (H╬▓ (realEdgeAddImag y ╬╖ (┬▒╬╡)) -
           H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)) * ╧å yΓÇû Γëñ M╬╡ * Γê½ y, ΓÇû╧å yΓÇû`.
