         One may also bound the last integral by
         `(sSup ((fun y => ΓÇû╧å yΓÇû) '' K)) *
          volume (Metric.closedBall (0 : NPointDomain d n) R╧å)`,
         where `R╧å` is any radius with
         `K Γèå Metric.closedBall 0 R╧å`.  Equivalently, avoid the finite-volume
         closed-ball bound and use
         `(Γê½ y, ΓÇû╧å yΓÇû)` as the fixed multiplier.  The multiplier is finite
         because `╧å` is Schwartz.  Since the last sup tends to zero uniformly
         on `K╬╖`, the required `TendstoUniformlyOn` limits follow.  The proof
         must not integrate over `K` as if arbitrary compact sets were the
         canonical measure domain; the all-space integral is localized by
         `╧å = 0` off `tsupport ╧å`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalBoundaryValue
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Γäò) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (E C : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hE_open : IsOpen E)
          (hE_precompact : IsCompact (closure E))
          (hsource :
            ΓêÇ y Γêê closure E,
              BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
                (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ y)
          (hlocal :
            ΓêÇ K : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K -> K Γèå E ->
              ΓêÇ K╬╖ : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬╖ -> K╬╖ Γèå C ->
                Γêâ r : Γä¥, 0 < r Γêº
                  ΓêÇ y Γêê K, ΓêÇ ╬╖ Γêê K╬╖, ΓêÇ ╬╡ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬╡ -> ╬╡ < r ->
                    BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ ╬╡ Γêê
                      BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Γêº
                    BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ (-╬╡) Γêê
                      BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓) :
          Γêâ T : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé ->L[Γäé] Γäé,
            (ΓêÇ K╬╖ : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬╖ -> K╬╖ Γèå C ->
              ΓêÇ ╧å : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé,
                HasCompactSupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) ->
                tsupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γèå E ->
                TendstoUniformlyOn
                  (fun (╬╡ : Γä¥) ╬╖ =>
                    Γê½ y : NPointDomain d n,
                      BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative
                        (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
                        (BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ ╬╡) *
                      ╧å y)
                  (fun _ : NPointDomain d n => T ╧å)
                  (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0)) K╬╖) Γêº
            (ΓêÇ K╬╖ : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬╖ -> K╬╖ Γèå C ->
              ΓêÇ ╧å : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé,
                HasCompactSupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) ->
                tsupport (╧å : NPointDomain d n -> Γäé) Γèå E ->
                TendstoUniformlyOn
                  (fun (╬╡ : Γä¥) ╬╖ =>
                    Γê½ y : NPointDomain d n,
                      BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative
                        (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
                        (BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ (-╬╡)) *
                      ╧å y)
                  (fun _ : NPointDomain d n => T ╧å)
                  (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0)) K╬╖)
      ```

      Proof source and order:

      1. Call `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalCommonGerm` with `hE_precompact` and
         `hsource` to obtain the open common neighborhood `U`, the canonical
         germ `H╬▓`, and the two agreement fields.  Then call
         `BHW.os45RegularBoundaryValue_from_commonGerm` with `hlocal` to
         construct the CLM `T` and both uniform compact-direction limits.
         The remaining bullets explain the source of those two inputs.
      2. Use `bvt_F_acrOne_package` for the OS-II branch: holomorphy on
         ACR-one, total finite-permutation symmetry, and Euclidean
         restriction.  The permutation symmetry is used only to rewrite the
         ACR branch representative on `os45ACRBranchDomain ╬▓` to the ordinary
         forward-tube chart expression; it is not a boundary-locality claim.
      3. Use `bvt_F_holomorphic`, `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube`,
         `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn`, and the definition of
         `BHW.os45PulledRealBranch` for the BHW branch.  Local polynomial
         bounds for the BHW branch are obtained from the local representation
         of `extendF` near each point of the compact horizontal edge as
         `bvt_F` composed with one inverse complex-Lorentz chart; compactness
         gives finitely many such charts.
      4. Use the local OS I ┬º4.5 / Streater-Wightman Figure 2-4 common-real
         environment to place the chosen OS45 horizontal edge inside the
         **single branch** BHW domain.  This step is a patch-level geometric
         source theorem for the selected adjacent real environment.  It is not
         the later Hall-Wightman source theorem on all of `S''_n`, and it does
         not compare the identity and adjacent branches with each other.
      5. On that source patch, invoke the local source-germ predicate
         `hsource`: the ACR representative and the BHW pulled representative
         are the two side representatives of the same one-branch analytic
         germ.  The equality input is the OS-II symmetric `S'_n` datum on the
         selected Euclidean/Jost edge together with the single-branch BHW
         enlargement of that germ.  This is exactly the local common-boundary
         statement OS I uses before the final Jost locality theorem; it must
         not be replaced by a pointwise `extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` argument
         for a permuted horizontal point.
      6. Define the branch boundary CLM from the common germ.  In the selected
         source patch the common germ is holomorphic on a complex neighborhood of
         `BHW.realEmbed '' closure E`, so the boundary distribution is regular:
         it is integration against the real-slice function
         `y Γåª H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)` on a real neighborhood of `closure E`.
         The extra hypotheses `hE_open : IsOpen E` and
         `hE_precompact : IsCompact (closure E)` are exactly what turn this
         into the displayed global CLM: choose a smooth cutoff supported inside
         that real neighborhood and equal to one on `closure E` using
         `BHW.exists_nPoint_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport`,
         choose a closed ball containing the cutoff support, and build the CLM
         by the flattening adapter
         `BHW.exists_nPoint_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn` applied
         to the compactly supported smooth density
         `╧ç y * H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)`.  Compact supports and compact direction
         sets are used for the boundary limits; no physical real-edge
         distribution is used.
      7. Prove the displayed uniform compact-direction BV limits from
         continuity of the canonical germ.  Given compact `K Γèå E` and compact
         `K╬╖ Γèå C`, `hlocal` gives a radius so the plus and minus side points
         stay in the relevant branch domains.  The separate topology lemma
         `BHW.exists_uniform_realEdgeAddImag_mem_open_of_compact` gives a
         radius so both side points also stay in the common-germ neighborhood
         `U`; take the minimum of the two radii.  On that smaller window the
         ACR and BHW representatives are literally `H╬▓`.  The compact image
         `P = {realEdgeAddImag y ╬╖ t | y Γêê tsupport ╧å, ╬╖ Γêê K╬╖, |t| Γëñ r}` is
         contained in `U`, so `hH_cont` gives uniform continuity of `H╬▓` on
         `P`; the explicit affine formula for `realEdgeAddImag` (or the
         compact-parameter tube lemma) gives uniform convergence of the input
         points to `realEmbed y` as `╬╡ ΓåÆ 0+`, uniformly for
         `╬╖ Γêê K╬╖`.  Since `Function.support ╧å Γèå tsupport ╧å Γèå K`, the
         all-space integral
         difference is bounded by
         `╬┤ ╬╡ * Γê½ y, ΓÇû╧å yΓÇû`, where `╬┤ ╬╡` is the supremum of
         `ΓÇûH╬▓ (realEdgeAddImag y ╬╖ (┬▒╬╡)) - H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)ΓÇû` over
         `y Γêê tsupport ╧å` and `╬╖ Γêê K╬╖`.  The factor `Γê½ y, ΓÇû╧å yΓÇû` is finite
         because `╧å` is Schwartz.  The proof must not replace the all-space
         integral by an integral over an arbitrary compact `K`; localization
         comes from `╧å = 0` off `tsupport ╧å`.  The compact-uniform continuity
         step proves `╬┤ ╬╡ -> 0`, giving the required `TendstoUniformlyOn`
         integral convergence on `K╬╖`.
      8. Verify the compact Jost restriction of `T` by transporting a
         supported test through `BHW.os45CommonEdgePullbackCLM`, applying
         `BHW.zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap`, and then using
         `bvt_euclidean_restriction`.  For the adjacent branch, insert
         `BHW.permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz` before applying `OS.E3_symmetric`.
         This verification is downstream evidence that the branch CLM has the
         correct OS restriction; it is not the definition of `T`.

      Concrete proof order for the `hlocal` domain hypothesis:

      1. Name the branch domains

         ```lean
         Dplus ╬▓  := BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓
         Dminus ╬▓ := BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓
         ```

         `Dplus ╬▓` is open because it is the preimage of
         `BHW.ForwardTube d n` under the continuous linear equivalence
         `(BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE).symm`.  `Dminus ╬▓` is open by the checked
         theorem `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`.

         Checked status: `BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain` and
         `BHW.isOpen_os45ACRBranchDomain` are now implemented in
         `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45TraceMembership.lean`.
      2. Prove the pure coordinate edge-membership theorem

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered
             [NeZero d]
             (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) {x : NPointDomain d n}
             (hx :
               x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓) :
             BHW.realEmbed
               (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x) Γêê
               BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓
         ```

         Proof transcript: unfold `os45ACRBranchDomain` and
         `os45QuarterTurnCLE_symm_apply`; the imaginary part of the time
         coordinate is exactly
         `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint ╬▓ x`, hence the forward-cone differences are
         half of the ordered Euclidean time differences.  The spatial
         imaginary parts are zero.  Conclude with
         `os45HalfTimeDirection_mem_forwardCone_of_ordered`.

         Checked status: `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered`
         is now implemented.  The proof uses the checked
         `os45QuarterTurn_perm_wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_ordered` theorem
         and the coordinate identity between the inverse quarter-turn of the
         horizontal real edge and the ordinary ordered Wick trace.
      3. Prove the BHW/Jost common-real-environment edge theorem in the
         active adjacent patch form.  The formerly tempting pointwise surface
         with only `x Γêê JostSet`, `realEmbed x Γêê ExtendedTube`, and ordered
         time hypotheses is too broad for implementation: the paper supplies a
         selected local real environment, not an all-Jost formula.  The
         Lean-facing theorem should therefore be a strengthened OS45 selector:
         choose, or shrink to, one patch carrying both the existing
         `BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_localEOWGeometry` fields and the new
         horizontal pulled-branch-domain facts.  It must not take an arbitrary
         previously selected `V` and assert that `V` was already inside the
         Figure-2-4 environment.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
             Γêâ (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n)) (xseed : NPointDomain d n),
               IsOpen Ufig Γêº
               IsOpen V Γêº IsConnected V Γêº V.Nonempty Γêº xseed Γêê V Γêº
               IsCompact (closure V) Γêº
               closure V Γèå Ufig Γêº
               (let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
                  Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig, x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                  BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (╧ä k)) Γêê
                    BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä)) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê V, x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê V, BHW.realEmbed x Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
                 BHW.realEmbed
                   (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                     BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
                 x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                   (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
                 (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                   EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                     (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐)) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
                 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêê
                   adjacentOS45WickSeedDomain (d := d) (n := n) i hi
                     (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
                 BHW.realEmbed x Γêê
                   adjacentOS45RealEdgeDomain (d := d) (n := n) i hi) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
                 BHW.OS45OppositeTubeBranchGeometry (d := d) (n := n)
                   (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
                 BHW.OS45OppositeTubeBranchGeometry (d := d) (n := n)
                   (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐)
                   (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k))) Γêº
               (let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
                  Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê V,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê closure V,
                  x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                    (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê closure V,
                  (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐ k)) Γêê
                    EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê closure V,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1) Γêº
                (ΓêÇ x Γêê closure V,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä) Γêº
                (let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
                 ΓêÇ x Γêê closure V,
                  let y :=
                    BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x
                  Γêâ (╬ô ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
                    Continuous ╬ô Γêº
                    Continuous ╬ö Γêº
                    ╬ô (0 : unitInterval) =
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêº
                    ╬ô (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Γêº
                    (ΓêÇ t, ╬ô t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                    (ΓêÇ t, ╬ö t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                    (ΓêÇ t,
                      BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                        BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                          (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ô t)))))
         ```

         This is not a wrapper around `hV_ET` or `hV_swapET`: those hypotheses
         put the lower real traces
         `Q(realEmbed x) = y - i╬╖` and
         `Q(realEmbed (x Γêÿ ╧ä)) = y - i╬╖` in their pulled BHW branch domains.
         The theorem above puts the **horizontal** quarter-turn edge `y`
         itself in the pulled domains for the two one-branch representatives.
         Its mathematical source is the adjacent common-real-environment
         paragraph in Streater-Wightman Section 2-4 / Figure 2-4, used in OS
         I ┬º4.5.  In the current Lean infrastructure the required geometric
         environment is obtained from the checked witness
         `BHW.adjacent_overlap_real_jost_witness_exists` by the equal-time
         OS45 identities and openness of the relevant domains.  The proof
         first constructs this Figure-2-4 source environment, then chooses the
         identity-order positive-time perturbation inside it, and only then
         takes a connected ball with compact closure.  The theorem does not
         assert a global all-Jost fact and does not compare branch values.

         Proof transcript for the selector:

         - Start from one shared Figure-2-4 anchor, not from independently
           chosen existential outputs.  In Lean this can be done either by
           inlining the two constructions below, or by first proving the
           combined selector
           `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithPathStability`.
           It is proved from the checked adjacent witness and finite
           intersections of open preimages.  It gives the same equal-time
           Figure-2-4 witness `x0`, an open source environment `Ufig`
           carrying Jost, the two ordinary real extended-tube memberships, and
           the two horizontal pulled-branch memberships, and an open
           path-stability neighborhood `Upath` around that same `x0`.
         - Apply
           `BHW.exists_ordered_small_time_perturb_in_adjacent_overlap_of_lt`
           with `U := Ufig Γê⌐ Upath`.  The output seed `xseed` is identity
           ordered, its adjacent relabelled configuration is ordered in the
           adjacent sector, and it remains inside both the Figure-2-4 source
           environment and the path-stability neighborhood.
         - Let
           `Hid x = (BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
             (BHW.realEmbed (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x))`
           and
           `H╧ä x = BHW.permAct (d := d) ╧ä.symm Hid x`, where
           `╧ä = Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐`.  These are continuous maps from
           the real patch into the complex configuration space; the source
           environment fields say exactly that `Hid x` and `H╧ä x` lie in the
           relevant extended-tube domains.
         - Build the path-stability field `hV_figPath_closure` in the same source
           patch, before any scalar theorem is called, and keep it valid on
           `closure V`.  The identity
           realization is the explicit interpolation
           `╬ô t k 0 = c t * x k 0`, `╬ô t k ╬╝ = x k ╬╝` for `╬╝ Γëá 0`, with
           `c t = t / 2 + (1 - t / 2) * I`.  The adjacent realization is the
           Figure-2-4 rotated realization of the adjacent permuted Gram point,
           not the bare relabelled path.  The proof uses the promoted
           two-plane support from `AdjacentOverlapWitness.lean` and a
           compact-open shrink around the ordered seed over `unitInterval`.
         - Intersect `Ufig` with the two ordered-sector preimages and with the
           open trace-domain preimages needed for the checked OS45 Wick/real
           fields.  The trace-domain fields can also be obtained from the
           checked ordered/raw-overlap lemmas, but the intersection form is the
           safest Lean route because it gives closure-level inheritance.
         - Choose a connected open ball around the seed whose closed ball is
           still inside this finite intersection.  This gives
           `IsCompact (closure V)` in the finite-dimensional real chart.  All
           old geometry fields hold on the ball, and the order and horizontal
           pulled-branch fields also hold on `closure V`, because the closed
           ball was chosen inside the same finite intersection.  The two
           pulled-back Figure 2-4 inclusions give the new horizontal
           memberships in
           `BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1` and
           `BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä`, and the
           compact-open path-stability shrink gives `hV_figPath_closure` on
           `closure V`.
         - No equality of branch values is concluded here.  The theorem only
           selects the source patch on which the later one-branch boundary
           value construction is allowed to run.

         Lean-ready decomposition of the selector:

         1. The only source-facing geometric selector is the following local
            Streater-Wightman Section 2-4 / Figure 2-4 theorem.  In the
            current codebase its proof is not an additional analytic axiom:
            it is a finite-dimensional open-neighborhood refinement of the
            checked witness theorem
            `BHW.adjacent_overlap_real_jost_witness_exists`.  It is a theorem
            about one anchored adjacent **extended-tube real environment**
            only; it has no function values, no `extendF` equality, no
            `bvt_W`, and no final locality content.

            The anchor is important.  Figure 2-4 does not say that every
            arbitrary real seed satisfying broad Jost/extended-tube
            memberships has the required horizontal OS45 common environment.
            It constructs a particular adjacent real configuration and then
            says all real points in a sufficiently small neighborhood have the
            same common-environment property.  The Lean source theorem must
            therefore be existential/anchored, and the ordered OS45 seed must
            be obtained by the already checked bounded time perturbation
            inside this source environment.

            ```lean
            theorem BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalRealEnvironment
                [NeZero d]
                (hd : 2 <= d)
                (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
                Γêâ (Ufig : Set (NPointDomain d n))
                  (x0 : NPointDomain d n),
                  IsOpen Ufig Γêº x0 Γêê Ufig Γêº
                  (ΓêÇ k : Fin n, x0 k 0 = 0) Γêº
                  (let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
                    Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
                   (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig, x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n) Γêº
                   (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                    BHW.realEmbed x Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                   (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                    BHW.realEmbed
                      (fun k => x (╧ä k)) Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                   (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                    BHW.realEmbed
                      (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                      BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1) Γêº
                   (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                    BHW.realEmbed
                      (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                      BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä))
            ```

            Lean meaning: with
            `Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)` and
            `y(x) := BHW.realEmbed (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
            (d := d) (n := n) 1 x)`, the two displayed conclusions are
            exactly
            `Q.symm (y(x)) Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n` and
            `BHW.permAct ╧ä.symm (Q.symm (y(x))) Γêê
            BHW.ExtendedTube d n`.  At the equal-time witness these reduce
            to the already checked ordinary adjacent-overlap memberships
            `BHW.realEmbed x0 Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n` and
            `BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x0 (╧ä k)) Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n`.
            The zero-time field is what makes the OS45 horizontal common
            edge literally the ordinary real witness at the anchor before the
            checked positive time perturbation.

            Proof transcript:

            - Start from
              `BHW.adjacent_overlap_real_jost_witness_exists (d := d)
              (n := n) hd i hi`, obtaining `x0` with
              `x0 Γêê BHW.JostSet d n`, the equal-time normalization
              `ΓêÇ k, x0 k 0 = 0`, and the two ordinary extended-tube
              memberships for `x0` and `x0 Γêÿ ╧ä`.
            - Prove the two anchor identities:

              ```lean
              BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x0 = x0

              (BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
                (BHW.realEmbed
                  (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                    (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x0)) =
                BHW.realEmbed x0

              BHW.permAct (d := d) ╧ä.symm
                ((BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
                  (BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                      (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x0))) =
                BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x0 (╧ä k))
              ```

              The first is `simp [BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint, hx_time0]`;
              the second uses `os45QuarterTurnCLE_symm_apply` and
              `hx_time0`; the third is the second identity followed by the
              definition of `BHW.permAct` and the exact Lean normalization
              `Equiv.swap_inv` for `╧ä.symm = ╧ä`.
            - Define `Ufig` as the finite intersection

              ```lean
              BHW.JostSet d n Γê⌐
                {x | BHW.realEmbed x Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n} Γê⌐
                {x |
                  BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (╧ä k)) Γêê
                    BHW.ExtendedTube d n} Γê⌐
                {x |
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                      (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Γêê
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                      (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))} Γê⌐
                {x |
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                      (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Γêê
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä}
              ```

              and take `x0` as the anchor.
            - Prove `IsOpen Ufig` by finite intersections:
              `BHW.isOpen_jostSet`;
              `BHW.isOpen_extendedTube.preimage BHW.continuous_realEmbed`;
              `BHW.isOpen_extendedTube.preimage
              (BHW.continuous_realEmbed.comp
              (BHW.continuous_permNPoint ╧ä))`;
              and the two preimages of
              `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain` along the continuous
              map
              `x Γåª BHW.realEmbed (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 x)`.
              If the currently private continuity helpers in
              `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45.lean` are needed in production,
              promote them or restate them in the new source-patch file; do
              not duplicate an incompatible convention.
            - Prove `x0 Γêê Ufig` using the adjacent witness fields and the two
              anchor identities above.  The fourth and fifth fields of
              `hUfig_source` are just the definitions of
              `os45PulledRealBranchDomain 1` and
              `os45PulledRealBranchDomain ╧ä` after those identities.
            - Do not conclude any equality of branch values.  This theorem is
              only source geometry and topology.  The branchwise local germ
              equality is the separate `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt`
              input above.

         The path-stability neighborhood must share the same equal-time
         anchor as this source environment.  The implementation contract is
         therefore the combined theorem below; the two previous displayed
         theorems are its projections and should not be called independently
         in a way that loses anchor equality.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithPathStability
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
             let ╧ä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
               Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐
             Γêâ (Ufig Upath : Set (NPointDomain d n))
               (x0 : NPointDomain d n),
               IsOpen Ufig Γêº IsOpen Upath Γêº
               x0 Γêê Ufig Γêº x0 Γêê Upath Γêº
               (ΓêÇ k : Fin n, x0 k 0 = 0) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig, x Γêê BHW.JostSet d n) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                 BHW.realEmbed x Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (╧ä k)) Γêê
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                 BHW.realEmbed
                   (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                   BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê Ufig,
                 BHW.realEmbed
                   (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Γêê
                   BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä) Γêº
               (ΓêÇ x Γêê Upath,
                 x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                   (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) ->
                 let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
                 let y :=
                   BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                     (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
                 Γêâ (╬ô ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé),
                   Continuous ╬ô Γêº
                   Continuous ╬ö Γêº
                   ╬ô (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Γêº
                   ╬ô (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Γêº
                   (ΓêÇ t, ╬ô t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                   (ΓêÇ t, ╬ö t Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Γêº
                   (ΓêÇ t,
                     BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                       BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╧ä
                         (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ô t))))
         ```

         Proof transcript for the last field: take
         `╬ô := BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x`.  The
         ordered-sector argument is essential: `Upath` itself is an open
         neighborhood of an equal-time anchor and cannot be contained in the
         strict positive-time sector.  The endpoint and continuity fields are
         `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero`,
         `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_one`, and
         `BHW.continuous_os45Figure24IdentityPath`.  Its extended-tube
         membership follows from
         `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_mem_forwardTube` and
         `BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube` using this ordered-sector
         hypothesis.  The rotated path `╬ö` is the
         Figure-2-4 two-plane realization supplied by the compact-parameter
         shrink, and the Gram identity is exactly
         `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction` followed by
         `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm` and `Equiv.swap_inv`.

         Code-level transcript, with no hidden source choice:

         - Construct the combined theorem from the same equal-time witness as
           `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists`.  Do not call
           independent source-environment and path-stability selectors and then
           try to identify their anchors afterwards.
         - Let
           `╧ä := Equiv.swap i Γƒ¿i.val + 1, hiΓƒ⌐`.  The source environment is the
           finite intersection

           ```lean
           let Ufig : Set (NPointDomain d n) :=
             BHW.JostSet d n Γê⌐
             {x | BHW.realEmbed x Γêê BHW.ExtendedTube d n} Γê⌐
             {x |
               BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (╧ä k)) Γêê
                 BHW.ExtendedTube d n} Γê⌐
             {x |
               BHW.realEmbed
                 (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                   (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Γêê
                 BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                   (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))} Γê⌐
             {x |
               BHW.realEmbed
                 (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                   (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Γêê
                 BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╧ä}
           ```

         - Its openness is exactly the finite intersection of:
           `BHW.isOpen_jostSet`;
           `BHW.isOpen_extendedTube.preimage BHW.continuous_realEmbedNPoint`;
           `BHW.isOpen_extendedTube.preimage
           (BHW.continuous_realEmbedNPoint.comp
           (BHW.continuous_permNPoint ╧ä))`;
           and the two preimages of
           `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain` along
           `x Γåª BHW.realEmbed (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 x)`.  If these
           continuity helpers are not public, promote exactly these
           finite-dimensional continuity lemmas in the Figure-2-4 companion
           file; they are topology inputs, not analytic shortcuts.
         - The anchor membership in the two pulled domains is not assumed.  It
           follows from the zero-time witness identities

           ```lean
           BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
             (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x0 = x0
           (BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
             (BHW.realEmbed x0) = BHW.realEmbed x0
           BHW.permAct (d := d) ╧ä.symm (BHW.realEmbed x0) =
             BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x0 (╧ä k))
           ```

           together with the two ordinary extended-tube fields supplied by the
           Figure-2-4 witness.  This is the only place where `x0 k 0 = 0` is
           used in the combined selector.
        - The final closure path field is conditional on
          `x Γêê EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector 1`.  For such `x`, set
          `╬ô := BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath x`.  The endpoint, continuity,
          and forward/extended-tube membership are the checked
          `os45Figure24IdentityPath_*` lemmas.  Separately, the open-chart
          scalar-source field `hV_adjLift_ET` is the unconditional
          compact-open statement that
          `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift hd ╧ä x t` stays in `ExtendedTube`.
          The adjacent path `╬ö` in the closure field is this same rotated
          realization from `Upath`; the scalar equality is the checked
          Figure-2-4 Lorentz-invariance calculation.  No statement in this
          theorem says that `BHW.realEmbed (x Γêÿ ╧ä)` or the bare relabelled
          identity path is the adjacent analytic branch.

         2. The patch-shrinking step is separate finite-dimensional topology,
            not source geometry:

            ```lean
            theorem BHW.exists_connected_open_precompact_subset
                {x0 : NPointDomain d n} {U : Set (NPointDomain d n)}
                (hU_open : IsOpen U) (hx0 : x0 Γêê U) :
                Γêâ V : Set (NPointDomain d n),
                  IsOpen V Γêº IsConnected V Γêº x0 Γêê V Γêº
                  IsCompact (closure V) Γêº closure V Γèå U
            ```

            Proof: choose `r > 0` with `Metric.ball x0 r Γèå U`, set
            `V = Metric.ball x0 (r / 2)`, use convexity of balls for
            connectedness, `closure V Γèå Metric.closedBall x0 (r / 2)`,
            compactness of closed balls in the finite-dimensional real chart,
            and `Metric.closedBall x0 (r / 2) Γèå Metric.ball x0 r`.

         3. To prove
            `BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch`, start
            from
            `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithPathStability hd i hi`
            and obtain the source environment `Ufig`, the path-stability
            neighborhood `Upath`, and their shared equal-time witness `x0`.
            The construction of that combined theorem uses the two-plane
            rotation support calculation and the compact-parameter tube lemma;
            the important implementation point is that `Ufig` and `Upath`
            are anchored at the same `x0`.  The `Upath` field is source
            geometry only; it has no function values.
            Apply the checked bounded perturbation theorem
            `BHW.exists_ordered_small_time_perturb_in_adjacent_overlap_of_lt`
            with `U := Ufig Γê⌐ Upath`; this chooses an ordered identity-sector
            seed `xseed = BHW.adjacentTimePerturb x0 ╬╡` still inside both the
            Figure-2-4 source environment and the path-stability neighborhood.
            Then form the finite open intersection of:

            - `Ufig`;
            - `Upath`;
            - the identity ordered sector;
            - the adjacent swapped ordered sector;
            - no value-equality or final-real-boundary condition.

            Apply
            `BHW.exists_connected_open_precompact_subset` at this `xseed`.
            Since `closure V` is contained in that finite intersection, all
            open-condition fields hold both on `V` and, where the selector
            statement asks for them, on `closure V`.  The ACR closure
            membership is supplied by the checked
            `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered`; the pulled
            BHW closure memberships are the two Figure-2-4
            source-environment fields; and the Figure-2-4 path field on
            `closure V` is obtained by applying the conditional `Upath` field
            with both `closure V Γèå Upath` and `hV_ordered_closure`.  This order of
            construction is mandatory: first source environment plus
            path-stability neighborhood, then ordered perturbation inside
            their intersection, then precompact shrink.

            The Wick-seed, real-edge, and opposite-tube fields on `V` are then
            derived, not stored as separate source assumptions:
            `hV_wick` follows from
            `BHW.wickRotate_mem_adjacentOS45WickSeedDomain_of_ordered`,
            `hV_real` follows from
            `BHW.realEmbed_mem_adjacentOS45RealEdgeDomain_of_ET`, and the two
            opposite-tube geometry fields follow from
            `BHW.os45OppositeTubeBranchGeometry_of_ordered`.  Since
            `closure V Γèå Ufig Γê⌐ Upath Γê⌐ ordered Γê⌐ swappedOrdered`, the
            closure-level ordered, pulled-domain, and Figure-2-4 path fields
            are inherited from the same finite intersection; they are not
            extrapolated from pointwise branch values.

         This decomposition is the implementation contract for the selector.
         Any attempted proof that derives the horizontal pulled-branch fields
         only from `hV_ET`/`hV_swapET`, or from a pointwise
         `extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` comparison, is off route.

         Checked status: the Figure-2-4 companion file now implements
         `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithPathStability` and
         `BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch`.  The Lean
         endpoint field is written in the direct normal form
         `(BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE).symm
         (BHW.realEmbed (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 x))`, equivalent to the
         displayed `let Q, y` form above.  The proofs use no branch-value
         comparison, no `extendF`, no `bvt_W`, and no final locality input.
      4. With the two edge-membership theorems and the two openness facts,
         prove the compact uniform local-wedge lemma

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontal_localWedge_of_edgeDomain
             [NeZero d]
             (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
             (E C : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hE_plus :
               ΓêÇ y Γêê E,
                 BHW.realEmbed y Γêê
                   BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
             (hE_minus :
               ΓêÇ y Γêê E,
                 BHW.realEmbed y Γêê
                   BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓) :
             ΓêÇ K : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K -> K Γèå E ->
               ΓêÇ K╬╖ : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬╖ -> K╬╖ Γèå C ->
                 Γêâ r : Γä¥, 0 < r Γêº
                   ΓêÇ y Γêê K, ΓêÇ ╬╖ Γêê K╬╖, ΓêÇ ╬╡ : Γä¥, 0 < ╬╡ -> ╬╡ < r ->
                     BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ ╬╡ Γêê
                       BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Γêº
                     BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬╖ (-╬╡) Γêê
                       BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓
         ```

         Proof transcript: the map `(y,╬╖,╬╡) Γåª realEdgeAddImag y ╬╖ ╬╡` is
         continuous.  At `╬╡ = 0`, it is `realEmbed y`, which lies in both
         open branch domains by `hE_plus` and `hE_minus`.  Compactness of
         `K ├ù K╬╖` gives a uniform positive radius.  No cone convexity is used
         here; cone data only controls which compact direction sets `K╬╖` are
         later supplied by the one-chart EOW setup.

         Checked status: `BHW.realEdgeAddImag`,
         `BHW.realEdgeAddImag_zero`,
         `BHW.continuous_realEdgeAddImag_of_continuous`,
         `BHW.continuous_realEdgeAddImag`, and
         `BHW.os45BranchHorizontal_localWedge_of_edgeDomain` are now
         implemented.  The proof is purely topological: take the compact set
         `(K ├ù K╬╖) ├ù {0}` in the product with the approach parameter, pull the
         two open branch domains back along the plus and minus perturbation
         maps, use `IsCompact.exists_cthickening_subset_open`, and choose the
         smaller of the two thickening radii.  This adds no OS/BHW source
         content; it only discharges the uniform-radius consequence once the
         edge-domain memberships have been supplied.

      The adjacent difference input is then:

      ```lean
      let Tdiff : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Γäé ->L[Γäé] Γäé := T╧ä - Tid
      ```

      and the `hplus_bv`/`hminus_bv` hypotheses for the checked one-chart
      theorem are obtained by subtracting the two branchwise BV statements.
      This is non-circular: `Tdiff` is a horizontal quarter-turn boundary
      distribution.  It is not asserted to be zero, and it is not the final
      real-edge adjacent distribution whose vanishing proves locality.
   9. Apply the checked `SCV.chartDistributionalEOW_local_envelope` at the
      ordered horizontal edge.  The local wedge input now follows by openness
      of `╬⌐plus/╬⌐minus` at the ordered horizontal edge and compactness of
      `K ├ù K╬╖`; it is not the false equal-time argument.  The fixed basis is
      chosen by `SCV.open_convex_cone_basis_with_positive_sum` so the OS45
      half-time seed ray is a strict-positive chart ray.
   10. Glue the returned local envelope to the positive and negative side
      components.  This is a genuine holomorphic gluing step, not an
      identity-theorem shortcut.  The side separation is fixed in the same
      chart coordinates used by `SCV.chartDistributionalEOW_local_envelope`,
      not by an unrelated original-coordinate half-space.  Let
      `A := SCV.localEOWComplexAffineEquiv xΓéü ys hli` be the affine chart
      (after the harmless `SCV.flattenCLE`/unflattening identification when
      the OS45 configuration space is written as
      `Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Γäé`).  Define the open chart orthants

      ```lean
      def SCV.ChartPositiveOrthant (m : Γäò) :
          Set (SCV.ComplexChartSpace m) :=
        {w | ΓêÇ j, 0 < (w j).im}

      def SCV.ChartNegativeOrthant (m : Γäò) :
          Set (SCV.ComplexChartSpace m) :=
        {w | ΓêÇ j, (w j).im < 0}
      ```

      In coordinate space set

      ```lean
      U0 := Metric.ball (0 : SCV.ComplexChartSpace m) R
      Uplus :=
        connectedComponentIn
          (╬⌐plus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartPositiveOrthant m) wplusSeed
      Uminus :=
        connectedComponentIn
          (╬⌐minus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartNegativeOrthant m) wminusSeed
      ```

      where `wplusSeed Γêê SCV.StrictPositiveImagBall R` and
      `wminusSeed Γêê SCV.StrictNegativeImagBall R`.  The one-chart output gives
      `SCV.StrictPositiveImagBall R Γèå ╬⌐plus` and
      `SCV.StrictNegativeImagBall R Γèå ╬⌐minus`.  Since each strict side ball is
      open and convex, it is contained in the corresponding connected
      component.  Therefore the overlaps are exactly

      ```lean
      U0 Γê⌐ Uplus  = SCV.StrictPositiveImagBall R
      U0 Γê⌐ Uminus = SCV.StrictNegativeImagBall R
      ```

      because `Uplus` is contained in the positive orthant and `Uminus` is
      contained in the negative orthant.  This exact-overlap choice is the
      important correction: the OS45 proof must not define side components by
      a broad linear sign condition and then hope that equality on one strict
      seed ball propagates to every possible overlap component.  The checked
      one-chart side identities already give equality on the whole overlap
      used for gluing.

      The small pure topology lemmas needed here are elementary implementation
      prerequisites for the OS45 gluing theorem unless Mathlib already exposes
      the exact forms:

      ```lean
      theorem SCV.isPreconnected_StrictPositiveImagBall
          (hm : 0 < m) {R : Γä¥} (hR : 0 < R) :
          IsPreconnected (SCV.StrictPositiveImagBall (m := m) R)

      theorem SCV.isPreconnected_StrictNegativeImagBall
          (hm : 0 < m) {R : Γä¥} (hR : 0 < R) :
          IsPreconnected (SCV.StrictNegativeImagBall (m := m) R)

      theorem SCV.strictPositiveImagBall_nonempty
          (hm : 0 < m) {R : Γä¥} (hR : 0 < R) :
          (SCV.StrictPositiveImagBall (m := m) R).Nonempty

      theorem SCV.strictNegativeImagBall_nonempty
          (hm : 0 < m) {R : Γä¥} (hR : 0 < R) :
          (SCV.StrictNegativeImagBall (m := m) R).Nonempty

      theorem SCV.disjoint_chartPositiveOrthant_chartNegativeOrthant
          (hm : 0 < m) :
          Disjoint (SCV.ChartPositiveOrthant m)
            (SCV.ChartNegativeOrthant m)
      ```

      The disjointness theorem explicitly uses `hm` to choose a coordinate;
      for `m = 0` both coordinatewise strict predicates are vacuous, so this
      hypothesis must not be omitted.  In the OS45 instantiation, `hm` is the
      positive chart-dimension hypothesis already required by
      `SCV.chartDistributionalEOW_local_envelope`.

      The exact-overlap rewrite used by the gluing theorem is isolated as two
      pure topology lemmas:

      ```lean
      theorem SCV.ball_inter_positiveOrthantComponent_eq_strictPositiveImagBall
          {m : Γäò} {R : Γä¥} {╬⌐plus : Set (SCV.ComplexChartSpace m)}
          {wplusSeed : SCV.ComplexChartSpace m}
          (hwplusSeed :
            wplusSeed Γêê SCV.StrictPositiveImagBall (m := m) R)
          (hstrict_plus :
            SCV.StrictPositiveImagBall (m := m) R Γèå ╬⌐plus) :
          Metric.ball (0 : SCV.ComplexChartSpace m) R Γê⌐
              connectedComponentIn
                (╬⌐plus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartPositiveOrthant m) wplusSeed =
            SCV.StrictPositiveImagBall (m := m) R

      theorem SCV.ball_inter_negativeOrthantComponent_eq_strictNegativeImagBall
          {m : Γäò} {R : Γä¥} {╬⌐minus : Set (SCV.ComplexChartSpace m)}
          {wminusSeed : SCV.ComplexChartSpace m}
          (hwminusSeed :
            wminusSeed Γêê SCV.StrictNegativeImagBall (m := m) R)
          (hstrict_minus :
            SCV.StrictNegativeImagBall (m := m) R Γèå ╬⌐minus) :
          Metric.ball (0 : SCV.ComplexChartSpace m) R Γê⌐
              connectedComponentIn
                (╬⌐minus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartNegativeOrthant m) wminusSeed =
            SCV.StrictNegativeImagBall (m := m) R
      ```

      Proof: the forward inclusion uses
      `connectedComponentIn_subset` and the definition of the chart orthant.
      For the reverse inclusion, the strict side ball is convex, hence
      preconnected; it is contained in `╬⌐┬▒ Γê⌐ Chart┬▒Orthant` by the one-chart
      side-domain inclusion and by definition of the strict side ball; and it
      contains the chosen seed.  Apply
      `IsPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn`.

      The connected components of the open sets
      `╬⌐plus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartPositiveOrthant m` and
      `╬⌐minus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartNegativeOrthant m` are open because the
      finite-dimensional chart space is locally connected.  The component
      hypotheses consumed by the gluing helper are supplied by these pure
      topology lemmas:

      ```lean
      theorem SCV.isOpen_connectedComponentIn_complexChart_of_isOpen
          {m : Γäò} {D : Set (SCV.ComplexChartSpace m)}
          {z : SCV.ComplexChartSpace m}
          (hD_open : IsOpen D) :
          IsOpen (connectedComponentIn D z)

      theorem SCV.isConnected_connectedComponentIn_of_mem
          {X : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X]
          {D : Set X} {z : X} (hz : z Γêê D) :
          IsConnected (connectedComponentIn D z)
      ```

      The first theorem is finite-dimensional topology: products of `Γäé` are
      locally path connected, hence locally connected, so connected components
      of open subsets are open.  The second theorem is the standard
      connected-component-in fact; if Mathlib already exposes it under a
      different name, the OS45 file should use that name directly rather than
      adding a wrapper with no proof content.
      The only additional set-level gluing fact is the connected three-union
      lemma.  It is pure topology and has the following implementation
      contract:

      ```lean
      theorem SCV.isConnected_threeUnion_of_connected_core_meets
          {X : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X]
          {U0 Uplus Uminus : Set X}
          (hU0_conn : IsConnected U0)
          (hUplus_conn : IsConnected Uplus)
          (hUminus_conn : IsConnected Uminus)
          (hplus_meets : (U0 Γê⌐ Uplus).Nonempty)
          (hminus_meets : (U0 Γê⌐ Uminus).Nonempty) :
          IsConnected (U0 Γê¬ Uplus Γê¬ Uminus)
      ```

      Proof transcript: first apply `IsConnected.union hplus_meets` to
      `U0` and `Uplus`.  The point in `U0 Γê⌐ Uminus` also lies in
      `(U0 Γê¬ Uplus) Γê⌐ Uminus`, so a second `IsConnected.union` glues in
      `Uminus`.  This helper is not an analytic shortcut; it only discharges
      the connectedness hypothesis for the actual glued chart domain.

      The pure complex-analysis helper has the following concrete shape:

      ```lean
      theorem SCV.glue_localEnvelope_to_disjoint_sideComponents
          {m : Γäò}
          {U0 Uplus Uminus : Set (SCV.ComplexChartSpace m)}
          {H0 Hplus Hminus : SCV.ComplexChartSpace m -> Γäé}
          (hU0_open : IsOpen U0) (hUplus_open : IsOpen Uplus)
          (hUminus_open : IsOpen Uminus)
          (hU0_conn : IsConnected U0)
          (hUplus_conn : IsConnected Uplus)
          (hUminus_conn : IsConnected Uminus)
          (hH0_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé H0 U0)
          (hHplus_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé Hplus Uplus)
          (hHminus_holo : DifferentiableOn Γäé Hminus Uminus)
          (hplus_meets : (U0 Γê⌐ Uplus).Nonempty)
          (hminus_meets : (U0 Γê⌐ Uminus).Nonempty)
          (hplus_agree_on_overlap :
            EqOn H0 Hplus (U0 Γê⌐ Uplus))
          (hminus_agree_on_overlap :
            EqOn H0 Hminus (U0 Γê⌐ Uminus))
          (hplus_minus_disjoint : Disjoint Uplus Uminus) :
          Γêâ U H, U = U0 Γê¬ Uplus Γê¬ Uminus Γêº
            IsOpen U Γêº IsConnected U Γêº
            DifferentiableOn Γäé H U Γêº
            EqOn H H0 U0 Γêº EqOn H Hplus Uplus Γêº EqOn H Hminus Uminus
      ```

      In the OS45 instantiation, `hplus_agree_on_overlap` is obtained by
      rewriting `U0 Γê⌐ Uplus` to `StrictPositiveImagBall R` and using the
      positive side identity returned by
      `SCV.chartDistributionalEOW_local_envelope`; the negative side is
      identical.  No additional identity theorem is used at this single-chart
      side-gluing stage.

      Proof transcript for the gluing helper itself:

      - Define `U := U0 Γê¬ Uplus Γê¬ Uminus`.
      - Define `H z` by cases: use `Hplus z` on `Uplus`, `Hminus z` on
        `Uminus`, and `H0 z` elsewhere in `U0`.  The disjointness of
        `Uplus` and `Uminus`, plus the two overlap equalities, makes this
        definition independent of case choices on overlaps.
      - `IsOpen U` follows from the three open sets.
      - `IsConnected U` is exactly
        `SCV.isConnected_threeUnion_of_connected_core_meets hU0_conn
        hUplus_conn hUminus_conn hplus_meets hminus_meets`.
      - Prove the three local equality facts before differentiability:
        on `Uplus`, `H = Hplus` by the first branch of the definition; on
        `Uminus`, disjointness rules out the `Uplus` branch and then
        `H = Hminus`; on `U0`, split into membership in `Uplus`,
        membership in `Uminus`, or neither, and use
        `hplus_agree_on_overlap.symm`, `hminus_agree_on_overlap.symm`, or
        reflexivity respectively.
      - Transport the three holomorphy hypotheses to `H` on the three open
        pieces by `DifferentiableOn.congr`.
      - Combine them by two applications of
        `DifferentiableOn.union_of_isOpen`: first on `U0 Γê¬ Uplus`, then on
        `(U0 Γê¬ Uplus) Γê¬ Uminus`.  Rewrite associativity of `Γê¬` to the
        displayed `U`.
      - Return the definitional equality `U = U0 Γê¬ Uplus Γê¬ Uminus` in the
        witness packet.  Downstream theorem-2 code must not treat the glued
        domain as an unnamed superset; the side path and equality consumers
        use exactly this three-piece union.

      The chart-orthant restrictions are open subsets of `╬⌐plus` and `╬⌐minus`
      and are disjoint by
      `SCV.disjoint_chartPositiveOrthant_chartNegativeOrthant hm`.  Their
      connected components are open by
      `SCV.isOpen_connectedComponentIn_complexChart_of_isOpen`; the OS45 file
      uses that lemma directly unless Mathlib already provides exactly the
      same theorem under an upstream name.
   11. Prove the OS45 side-component path lemmas.  For `x Γêê V`, let
      `y(x) = os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 x` and
      `╬╖(x) = os45HalfTimeDirection 1 x`.  In chart real coordinates set
      `c(x) := (SCV.localEOWRealLinearCLE ys hli).symm ╬╖(x)`.  The basis was
      chosen so `╬╖Γéü = Γêæ j, ys j`, hence `c(xΓéü) j = 1` for every `j`; after
      shrinking `V`, continuity gives `0 < c(x) j` for all `x Γêê V` and all
      coordinates `j`.  The positive path is
      `s Γåª realEmbed y(x) + I * s ΓÇó ╬╖(x)`; in chart coordinates its imaginary
      part is `s ΓÇó c(x)`, so for `0 < s` it lies in
      `SCV.ChartPositiveOrthant m`.  For sufficiently small `sΓéÇ > 0`, the
      chart coordinate is in `SCV.StrictPositiveImagBall R`, hence in
      `U0 Γê⌐ Uplus`; the path segment from `sΓéÇ` to `1` stays in
      `╬⌐plus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartPositiveOrthant m`, so the Wick trace lies in the same
      positive side component.  The negative path is
      `s Γåª realEmbed y(x) - I * s ΓÇó ╬╖(x)`; its chart imaginary part is
      `-s ΓÇó c(x)`, so the same argument puts the real trace in the negative
      component.  The domain membership along these paths is the existing
      OS45 quarter-turn calculation: on the positive side, after inverse
      quarter-turn the imaginary forward-cone part is `(1 + s)╬╖(x)`; on the
      negative side it is `(1 - s)╬╖(x)` for `s < 1`, and the endpoint
      `s = 1` is in the pulled-real branch domains by the selected
      `ExtendedTube` facts.  The adjacent swapped branch is the same
      calculation with the checked `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq`
      and `os45HalfTimeDirection_adjacent_swap_eq`.

      The generic topology helper for these path lemmas should be named once:

      ```lean
      theorem SCV.path_endpoint_mem_connectedComponentIn
          {X : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X]
          {D : Set X} {z0 z1 : X}
          (╬│ : unitInterval -> X)
          (h╬│_cont : Continuous ╬│)
          (h╬│0 : ╬│ 0 = z0)
          (h╬│1 : ╬│ 1 = z1)
          (h╬│D : ΓêÇ t, ╬│ t Γêê D) :
          z1 Γêê connectedComponentIn D z0
      ```

      Proof: the image of `╬│` is preconnected, lies in `D`, and contains
      `z0`; apply `IsPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn`, then use
      `h╬│1`.  The OS45 positive and negative trace lemmas then only need to
      supply the concrete continuous paths and the two membership calculations:
      `╬⌐plus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartPositiveOrthant m` for the Wick trace and
      `╬⌐minus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartNegativeOrthant m` for the real trace.
   12. Finish the trace equations by rewriting:
      - the positive trace by the definition of `Hplus`,
        `Q╧üe.symm_apply_apply`, `BHW.permAct_wickRotatePoint`, and `╧ä`;
      - the negative trace by
        `BHW.os45PulledRealBranch_sub_eq_adjacentOS45RealEdgeDifference`.

Why this does not smuggle in locality:

- the retired shortcut would have smuggled in a false premise: the adjacent
  swapped horizontal branch argument is not generally in `BHW.ForwardTube d n`.
  The corrected common-boundary theorem sources the adjacent branch by treating
  `x Γêÿ ╧ä` as a separate ordinary OS-II branch and using the OS45 reindexing
  identity to return to the same quarter-turn chart point;
- the finite Wick-side zero is still supplied downstream by the OS symmetry
  argument already present in
  `os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector` and the
  `bvt_F_acrOne_package`;
- the real trace is represented by the pulled `extendF` branch difference, not
  by a pre-assumed real-edge equality;
- no selected PET branch-independence, final locality theorem, or
  boundary-locality transfer theorem appears in the construction of the local
  common-chart envelope.  The later
  `hallWightman_source_permutedBranch_compatibility_of_distributionalAnchor`
  theorem consumes the compact-test anchor produced here; it is not an input.

Active single-chart decomposition of Slot 1 after the SCV keystone:

1. Use `BHW.adjacent_overlap_real_jost_witness_exists` to choose the
   equal-time adjacent Jost witness `xΓéÇ`.  Define the Figure-2-4 source
   environment `Ufig` as the finite intersection of `JostSet`, the two
   ordinary extended-tube preimages, and the two OS45 horizontal
   pulled-domain preimages, and obtain the path-stability neighborhood `Upath`
   from the same anchor via
   `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithPathStability`.  The
   equal-time identities
   `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 xΓéÇ = xΓéÇ` and
   `Q.symm (realEmbed (os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 xΓéÇ)) = realEmbed xΓéÇ`
   put `xΓéÇ` in both horizontal pulled domains.  This is the implementation
   content of `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalRealEnvironment`, plus the
   compact-open Figure-2-4 path-stability field on `Upath`.
   Let `Uraw = adjacentOS45RawOverlap d n i hi`; it is the first three
   fields of `Ufig` and remains useful for the checked perturbation theorem.
2. Work in the identity OS45 order.  The existing perturbation lemma
   `exists_ordered_small_time_perturb_in_adjacent_overlap` already returns
   order `╧ü = 1`; the bounded version
   `exists_ordered_small_time_perturb_in_adjacent_overlap_of_lt` is now
   checked, with input `U := Ufig Γê⌐ Upath` and `a > 0`, and output perturbation
   parameter `0 < ╬╡ < a`; hence the ordered seed remains in both the source
   environment and the path-stability neighborhood.
3. Choose the perturbation `xΓéü = adjacentTimePerturb xΓéÇ ╬╡` first, then take a
   connected real-open ball `V` around `xΓéü` inside `Ufig Γê⌐ Upath` and the two
   ordered sectors.  Set
   `yΓéü = os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 xΓéü` and
   `╬╖Γéü = os45HalfTimeDirection (d := d) (n := n) 1 xΓéü`.  This selector is
   the new source-patch selector
   `BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch`.  The older raw
   selector `BHW.choose_os45_identity_real_open_edge_for_adjacent_swap` and
   its domain/trace/geometry package
   `BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_localEOWGeometry` remain checked support, but
   they do not by themselves carry the two horizontal pulled-domain fields.
4. Choose the chart basis using
   `SCV.open_convex_cone_basis_with_positive_sum` with `╬╖Γéü` after flattening.
   This makes the seed half-time ray have coordinate vector `1` in the fixed
   chart basis.  After shrinking the selected patch, every nearby half-time
   direction has strictly positive chart coordinates.  These chart-coordinate
   signs, not an auxiliary original-coordinate half-space, define the side
   components used for gluing.
5. Do **not** implement the retired ordered horizontal-edge forward-tube pair
   theorem.  The identity branch is in `BHW.ForwardTube d n`, but the adjacent
   swapped branch is generally not.  The proof document must first replace the
   false pointwise equality
   `Hplus = Hminus` on `Eseed = os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 '' Vseed` with the
   genuine branchwise OS45 horizontal common-boundary theorem surface: produce
   the identity and swapped branch CLMs `Tid` and `T╧ä`, prove both ACR-side and
   BHW-side uniform compact-direction boundary values for each branch, and
   subtract them to obtain the one-chart `Tdiff = T╧ä - Tid` input.  The
   swapped branch proof is the ordinary branch proof on the relabelled ordered
   patch `x Γêÿ ╧ä`, not a theorem saying that the swapped horizontal identity
   edge lies in the identity forward tube.
6. After the corrected common-boundary source is fixed, prove
   `BHW.os45_orderedHorizontalEdge_localWedge_id` for
   `Eseed`, the chosen side cone `Cseed`, and the two branch domains
   `╬⌐plus/╬⌐minus`.  This is the ordered-edge compactness/openness argument:
   at `╬╡ = 0`, the horizontal edge already lies in both branch domains, and
   compactness of `K ├ù K╬╖` gives one side radius.
7. Do not define a compact-cutoff boundary CLM from a continuous pointwise
   edge value.  The boundary CLMs are the branchwise horizontal distributions
   `Tid` and `T╧ä` supplied by step 5; the branch-difference CLM is their
   difference.  A cutoff is allowed only after the one-branch common germ has
   been constructed on a real neighborhood of `closure Eseed`; it turns that
   regular branch boundary value into a full Schwartz CLM.  It is not a
   substitute for the branchwise OS-II/BHW boundary construction and it never
   defines a common boundary by assuming `Hplus = Hminus`.
8. Apply `SCV.chartDistributionalEOW_local_envelope` at the ordered horizontal
   edge only after steps 5-7 provide true `hplus_bv` and `hminus_bv`
   hypotheses.  Its output is a local envelope `H0` on a small coordinate ball
   and side identities only on the strict positive/negative seed balls.
9. Define the positive and negative side components in the one-chart
   coordinates as the connected components of
   `╬⌐plus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartPositiveOrthant m` and
   `╬⌐minus Γê⌐ SCV.ChartNegativeOrthant m` containing the strict side seed
   balls.  Because the local ball is `Metric.ball 0 R`, its overlaps with
   these components are exactly
   `SCV.StrictPositiveImagBall R` and `SCV.StrictNegativeImagBall R`; the
   pure topology rewrite targets are
   `SCV.ball_inter_positiveOrthantComponent_eq_strictPositiveImagBall` and
   `SCV.ball_inter_negativeOrthantComponent_eq_strictNegativeImagBall`.
   Prove the finite Wick and real traces for every `x Γêê Vseed` lie in those
   components by the explicit paths
   `realEmbed y(x) + I * s ΓÇó ╬╖(x)` and
   `realEmbed y(x) - I * s ΓÇó ╬╖(x)`, using positivity of the chart-coordinate
   vector `(SCV.localEOWRealLinearCLE ys hli).symm (╬╖(x))`.
10. Glue `H0`, `Hplus`, and `Hminus` on the union of the local ball and the two
   side components.  Agreement on the local side overlaps is exactly the side
   identity output of the checked one-chart theorem after rewriting the
   overlaps to the strict positive/negative side balls.  The union is
   connected because the local ball intersects both side components in
   nonempty open side seeds.
11. Finish the Wick and real trace equations from the glued function:
    - the Wick trace uses the `Hplus` agreement, `Q╧üe.symm_apply_apply`,
      `BHW.permAct_wickRotatePoint`, and `╧ä`;
    - the real trace uses the `Hminus` agreement and
      `BHW.os45PulledRealBranch_sub_eq_adjacentOS45RealEdgeDifference`.
12. Package the result with
    `BHW.adjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope_of_commonChartEnvelope`.

This decomposition is the Slot 1 proof-doc gate.  Lean implementation may
start only at the first theorem surface in the list below whose upstream proof
transcript is already written here; no downstream theorem may be implemented
ahead of its source packet.  The checked Lean layer now covers the ACR branch
domain, the ordered horizontal-edge ACR membership, the real-edge imaginary
perturbation map, and the compactness/openness proof of the `hlocal` radius.
The Figure-2-4 algebra/topology subpacket is now checked through path
stability as well: the
public axes, finite `(3,4,5)` rotation, Lorentz inverse, concrete adjacent
two-plane support packet, compact-parameter tube lemma, OS45 identity path,
zero-time identity lemma, joint identity-path continuity, rotated-path
continuity, source-Gram Lorentz invariance, and
`BHW.swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists` all compile and
verify without new axioms or sorrys.  The Figure-2-4 source-selector pass is
also now checked in checkpoint `bb009da`.  The next Lean pass is therefore not
a local EOW pass, not a final `bvt_W` transport pass, and not another
Figure-2-4 selector.  It is blocked on the upstream scalar-source proof gate,
in this order:

1. checked Figure-2-4 support packet, without changing the route:
   - in `ComplexLieGroups/AdjacentOverlapWitness.lean`, keep the existing
     private witness calculations private; the public support theorem
     `BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlaneRotationSupport`, whose proof simply
     exports the concrete equal-time witness, rotated witness, forward-Jost
     certificate, and `(3,4,5)` Lorentz rotation already proved there, is now
     checked.  The
     public axes `BHW.figure24Axis1`, `BHW.figure24Axis2`, and finite rotation
     formula `BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig` also live in this file, along
     with `BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse`, so the support
     export and scalar path proof can use the same private `(3,4,5)` matrix
     calculation without importing any OS45 companion module;
   - in a small OS45 Figure-2-4 companion file, the following are now checked:
     `BHW.exists_open_nhds_forall_mem_of_compact_parameter`,
     `BHW.os45Figure24TimeCoeff`,
     `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath` with its endpoint, continuity, and
     forward-tube membership theorems,
     `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_of_time_zero`,
     `BHW.continuous_os45Figure24IdentityPath_joint`,
	     `BHW.continuous_figure24RotateAdjacentConfig`,
	     `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction`,
	     `BHW.continuous_figure24RotatedIdentityPath`, and
	     `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists`;
	   - the checked proof of the path-stability theorem already uses the
	     canonical rotated map internally.  Before the scalar-source Lean pass,
	     its public export must be strengthened, or a public projection added, so
	     that it exposes both the canonical
	     `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift` extended-tube field used by the source
	     pairing and the closure-level Figure-2-4 path field needed by the
	     scalar corridor;
   - do not export a theorem asserting that the bare adjacent relabelling of
     the identity path lies in the forward tube.  The adjacent path is the
     rotated Figure-2-4 realization, and its scalar identity is Lorentz
     invariance plus `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`.
2. checked Figure-2-4 source environment/source-patch selector:
   `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithPathStability` and
   `BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch`;
3. finish the scalar-source analytic inputs before source-germ Lean:
   the ordinary Hall-Wightman scalar representative
   `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` through
   `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq`,
   `BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank`, `BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank`,
   `BHW.HWSourceGramLowRank`,
   `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected`,
   `BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent`,
   `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw`, and
	   `BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData`; and the adjacent
	   `S'_n` package
	   `BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at`,
	   `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_extendF_eq_permutedWick_zero`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_acrPermutedBoundary`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`,
	   `BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24`,
	   `BHW.OS45SPrimeFigure24LocalSourceSeedData`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24SourceEqOnUsrc_of_compactWickPairing`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalSourceSeedData_of_OSI45`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalSourceEq_of_seedData`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalSourceEq_of_BHWJost`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalBranchCompatibility_of_BHWJost`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_extendF_eq_identityWick_of_BHWJost`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_extendF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_bvtF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_extendF_pairing_eq_bvt_F`,
	   `BHW.os45SPrime_permutedWickExtendF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`,
   `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24`,
   `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq`,
   `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq`, and
   `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq`;
4. prove the branch-local OS I ┬º4.5/BHW source suppliers
   `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_id` and
   `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent`; these are
   one-branch ACR/BHW compatibility theorems and may not use any
   `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`;
5. prove the compact common-germ gluing theorem
   `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalCommonGerm`;
6. expose the pure real-analysis cutoff projection
   `SCV.exists_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport`,
   expose its `NPointDomain` adapter
   `BHW.exists_nPoint_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport`,
   expose the `NPointDomain` closed-ball adapter
   `BHW.exists_nPoint_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn`, then prove the
   regular boundary-CLM construction and branchwise BV theorem
   `BHW.os45RegularBoundaryValue_from_commonGerm` and
   `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalBoundaryValue`;
7. subtract the checked identity/adjacent branchwise BV packets to obtain the
   `hplus_bv` and `hminus_bv` inputs for the branch difference;
8. prove the OS45 branch-difference common-boundary envelope
   `BHW.os45_adjacent_commonBoundaryEnvelope` from those `Tid/T╧ä`
   boundary-value packets and the checked one-chart local EOW theorem; this is
   where the chart-linear kernel pushforward, chart-orthant side components,
   exact-overlap rewrites, side path lemmas, and
   `SCV.glue_localEnvelope_to_disjoint_sideComponents` are used;
8. only after that, prove the downstream scalar consequence
   `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarGerm_of_OSII_Figure24` from the resulting
   `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`, the real-edge compact equality consumer,
   and `sourceVariety_localChart_totallyReal_zero`.

The route avoids both retired gaps: the one-chart theorem is not applied at
the equal-time edge, and finite traces are reached by holomorphic gluing
through side components rather than forced inside the local EOW ball.

Coarse Slot 1 decomposition retained for context:

1. build the local connected common-chart domain on which the adjacent Wick-side
   and real-side branch differences are both represented;
2. run the local distributional common-boundary / EOW step to produce the
   holomorphic common-chart branch-difference function `Hc`;
3. pull `Hc` back through the OS45 common-chart equivalence `Q╧üe`;
4. package the resulting direct-coordinate trace identities as
   `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`.

Steps 3 and 4 are now checked in Lean as
`BHW.adjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope_of_commonChartEnvelope`.  The live
mathematical work in Slot 1 is therefore exactly steps 1 and 2: prove the
common-chart OS45 distributional EOW supplier with the Wick and real trace
identities on the same selected patch `V`.

With that choice:

- the positive-side envelope trace is the honest direct Wick-side adjacent
  difference, whose vanishing is supplied downstream by
  `bvt_F_acrOne_package`;
- the negative-side envelope trace is the honest adjacent real-edge
  `extendF` difference;
- no tautological selected-branch cancellation appears anywhere in the active
  route;
- no local theorem slot is allowed to bypass the OS sequence
  symmetry -> continuation -> BHW -> Jost -> boundary locality.

The old "common-chart Wick difference" route is dead and should not be revived
in production code except as a cautionary note.

Historical local-descent gate, superseded for Slot 1:

The bullets below record the route that was active before
`SCV.chartDistributionalEOW_local_envelope` was checked.  They remain useful
as background for a future global/patching theorem, but they are no longer the
implementation gate for theorem-2 Slot 1.  For the local proof work now in
front of us, the authoritative gate is the checkpoint near the top of this
file: identity-order ordered-edge selection, horizontal-edge continuous common
boundary, one-chart local seed, side-component gluing, and then
`BHW.os45_adjacent_commonBoundaryEnvelope`.

* Hard proof-doc gate for the local descent step: no further Lean
  implementation should proceed until the scalarized local quotient packet in
  `docs/scv_infrastructure_blueprint.md` is treated as the authoritative
  route transcript.  That packet now fixes the finite-seminorm decay theorem
  for `schwartzPartialEvalΓééCLM` down to the singleton source family
  `{(k,l), (k+N,l)}`, the finite-sup aggregation and empty-family case, the
  mixed real-fiber integral CLM derivative/decay theorem surfaces, the dense
  split-tensor reduction through the factored flat two-block Hermite theorem,
  the zero-dimensional tensor case, all parameter-test CLE signs and inverse
  formulas, the two scalar change-of-variables identities, the exact
  scalarized local quotient equality chain, and the final packaging proof for
  `translationCovariantProductKernel_descends_local`.  The Lean pass must
  follow those theorem names and proof dependencies literally; it must not
  invent a `SchwartzMap`-valued Bochner average, a global covariance quotient,
  or a new density/quotient axiom while filling this step.
  File ownership is also fixed: the next local descent implementation belongs
  in a companion file after `SCV/LocalProductDescent.lean` rather than growing
  that near-threshold substrate file.  The right mixed-fiber
  change-of-variables identity is the affine map `a Γåª t - a`; in Lean it is
  decomposed as `integral_neg_eq_self` followed by
  `integral_add_right_eq_self`, since `integral_sub_right_eq_self` alone has
  the opposite sign convention.
* Do **not** add another distributional EOW axiom or wrapper.  The checked SCV
  global recovery endpoint
  `SCV.regularizedEnvelope_chartEnvelope_from_productKernel` remains available
  only for a genuinely globally covariant product kernel.  The active
  pure-SCV theorem-2 route uses the local recovery consumer
  `SCV.regularizedEnvelope_chartEnvelope_from_localProductKernel`, because the
  cutoff-localized mixed functional constructed from a local fixed-window
  family satisfies only local covariance.
* The local continuous-EOW geometry supplier is now checked through
  `SCV.local_continuous_edge_of_the_wedge_envelope`; the fixed-kernel
  distributional-to-continuous bridge is checked as
  `SCV.regularizedLocalEOW_fixedKernelEnvelope_from_clm`; the fixed-window
  version needed for one coherent family is checked as
  `SCV.regularizedLocalEOW_fixedWindowEnvelope_from_clm`; and the explicit
  family package is checked as
  `SCV.regularizedLocalEOW_family_from_fixedWindow`.
* The old next-stage target
  `SCV.regularizedLocalEOW_productKernel_from_continuousEOW` is retired under
  its global-covariance surface.  The next QFT-free SCV stage is Lean
  implementation of the local descent package named below, in the exact order
  recorded in `docs/scv_infrastructure_blueprint.md`.  Its output must feed a
  local recovery consumer with `K`, `Gchart`, a local `Hdist`,
  `IsDistributionalHolomorphicOn Hdist Udesc`, local product-test descent, and
  the product-test representation on supported tests.  It must not claim
  `ProductKernelRealTranslationCovariantGlobal K`.
* Before proving the covariance field, use the checked
  `SCV/LocalEOWChartLinear.lean` transport API.  The real smoothing kernel must
  first be pushed from chart coordinates to the original real edge through
  `SCV.localEOWRealLinearKernelPushforwardCLM`; this is the surface that carries
  the inverse determinant factor and the checked support-radius transport.
  Then apply
  `SCV.realMollifyLocal_localEOWRealLinearKernelPushforwardCLM` to rewrite the
  side mollifier as the corresponding chart-coordinate integral before invoking
  fixed-window uniqueness/covariance.
* The immediate Lean-ready covariance support lemmas are the chart-local
  versions of that identity:
  `SCV.realMollifyLocal_localEOWChart_kernelPushforwardCLM`,
  `SCV.realMollifyLocal_localEOWChart_kernelPushforwardCLM_pullback`, and
  `SCV.realMollifyLocal_localEOWChart_translate_kernelPushforwardCLM`.  The
  pullback theorem is the direct side-agreement form
  `realMollifyLocal F (P ╧å) (localEOWChart x0 ys w) =
   realMollifyLocal (fun ╬╢ => F (localEOWChart x0 ys ╬╢)) ╧å w`; the
  second theorem is the exact translated-kernel side-branch identity needed for
  the uniqueness proof of regularized-family covariance:
  pushing `translateSchwartz a ╧å` through the chart kernel and evaluating at
  `localEOWChart x0 ys w` is the same as pushing `╧å` and evaluating at
  `localEOWChart x0 ys (w - realEmbed a)`.
* Route correction before the full product-kernel supplier: a local
  fixed-window family on `Metric.ball 0 (╬┤ / 2)` does **not** by itself
  produce a globally translation-covariant mixed Schwartz functional
